24	\section{Setup and motivation}
25	\label{sec:ab_setup}
26	
27	A regular projective surface $S$ over a field --- such as $\mathbb{P}^1 \times \mathbb{P}^1$
28	or the product $C \times C$ of a smooth projective curve $C$ with itself --- has a regular
29	local ring at every (closed) point, of Krull dimension $2$. To extend a rational map
30	$f : S \dashrightarrow A$ from $S$ to an abelian variety across a codimension-$2$ closed
31	point, one argues that the local ring $\mathcal{O}_{S,x}$ at such a point has \emph{depth at
32	least} $2$, hence a rational function with no codimension-$1$ poles on a neighbourhood of $x$
33	extends across $x$. The depth $\geq 2$ inequality is exactly what the
34	Auslander--Buchsbaum formula gives: for a regular local ring $R$ one has $\text{pd}_R(R) = 0$,
35	so
36	\[
37	  \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{pd}_R(R) + \text{depth}(R) \;=\; \text{depth}(R)
38	\]
39	trivially, but combined with the regular-sequence definition of depth and the fact that a
40	regular local ring of dimension $d$ admits an $R$-regular sequence of length $d$ generating
41	the maximal ideal, one obtains $\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)$, i.e.\ $R$ is Cohen--Macaulay
42	(Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} below). This is the input A.4.a needs.
43	
44	This chapter packages:
45	\begin{enumerate}
46	  \item the regular-sequence definition of depth and its $\text{Ext}$ reformulation
47	    (Definition~\ref{def:depth} and Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_via_ext}),
48	  \item the projective-dimension invariant of a finitely-generated module
49	    (Definition~\ref{def:projective_dimension}),
50	  \item the depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma
51	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}),
52	  \item the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula proper
53	    (Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum}), and
54	  \item the corollary regular $\Rightarrow$ Cohen--Macaulay
55	    (Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}).
56	\end{enumerate}
57	A short closing paragraph (Section~\ref{sec:ab_application_to_a4a}) explains how A.4.a
58	consumes Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} to push a codim-1 extension across a
59	codim-2 point.
60	
61	\section{Depth of a module over a local ring}
62	\label{sec:ab_depth}
63	
64	\begin{definition}
65	
66	\leanok
67	  [Depth of a module]
68	  \label{def:depth}
69	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth}
70	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LF (definition-depth)
71	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17748-L17759).
72	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
73	  % "Let $R$ be a ring, and $I \subset R$ an ideal. Let $M$ be a finite $R$-module.
74	  %  The {\it $I$-depth} of $M$, denoted $\text{depth}_I(M)$, is defined as follows:
75	  %  \begin{enumerate}
76	  %  \item if $IM \not = M$, then $\text{depth}_I(M)$ is the supremum in
77	  %  $\{0, 1, 2, \ldots, \infty\}$ of the lengths of $M$-regular sequences in $I$,
78	  %  \item if $IM = M$ we set $\text{depth}_I(M) = \infty$.
79	  %  \end{enumerate}
80	  %  If $(R, \mathfrak m)$ is local we call $\text{depth}_{\mathfrak m}(M)$ simply
81	  %  the {\it depth} of $M$."
82	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LF (definition-depth).}
83	  Let $R$ be a commutative ring, let $I \subseteq R$ be an ideal, and let $M$ be a finitely
84	  generated $R$-module. The \emph{$I$-depth} of $M$, written $\text{depth}_I(M)$, is the
85	  supremum in $\{0, 1, 2, \dots, \infty\}$ of the lengths of $M$-regular sequences contained
86	  in $I$, provided $IM \neq M$; if $IM = M$ we set $\text{depth}_I(M) = \infty$. When
87	  $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ is a Noetherian local ring we abbreviate
88	  $\text{depth}(M) := \text{depth}_{\mathfrak{m}}(M)$ and call this simply the
89	  \emph{depth} of $M$. The depth of the ring $R$ itself is $\text{depth}(R)$, the depth
90	  of $R$ as a module over itself.
91	\end{definition}
92	
93	Recall that a sequence $f_1, \dots, f_r \in R$ is called \emph{$M$-regular} if
94	$M/(f_1, \dots, f_r)M \neq 0$ and for each $i$, the element $f_i$ is a non-zero-divisor on
95	$M/(f_1, \dots, f_{i-1})M$. The empty sequence is regular on $M$ iff $M \neq 0$ (it is
96	\emph{not} regular on the zero module, but the convention $\text{depth}(0) = \infty$
97	above is the usual one).
98	
99	The two practical computations of depth are the regular-sequence definition (used to
100	establish a \emph{lower} bound) and the $\text{Ext}$ characterisation (used to establish an
101	\emph{upper} bound and to prove the depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence inequality).
102	
103	\begin{lemma}
104	
105	\leanok
106	  [Depth via $\text{Ext}$]
107	  \label{lem:depth_via_ext}
108	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index}
109	  \uses{def:depth}
110	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LP (lemma-depth-ext)
111	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17845-L17885).
112	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
113	  % "Let $R$ be a Noetherian local ring with maximal ideal $\mathfrak m$.
114	  %  Let $M$ be a nonzero finite $R$-module. Then $\text{depth}(M)$
115	  %  is equal to the smallest integer $i$ such that
116	  %  $\Ext^i_R(R/\mathfrak m, M)$ is nonzero."
117	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LP (lemma-depth-ext).}
118	  Let $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ be a Noetherian local ring with residue field
119	  $\kappa = R/\mathfrak{m}$ and let $M$ be a nonzero finitely generated $R$-module. Then
120	  \[
121	    \text{depth}(M) \;=\; \inf\bigl\{\, i \in \mathbb{N} \;\bigm|\; \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0 \,\bigr\}.
122	  \]
123	\end{lemma}
124	
125	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
126	% "Let $\delta(M)$ denote the depth of $M$ and let $i(M)$ denote
127	%  the smallest integer $i$ such that $\Ext^i_R(R/\mathfrak m, M)$
128	%  is nonzero. We will see in a moment that $i(M) < \infty$.
129	%  By Lemma \ref{lemma-ideal-nonzerodivisor} we have
130	%  $\delta(M) = 0$ if and only if $i(M) = 0$, because
131	%  $\mathfrak m \in \text{Ass}(M)$ exactly means
132	%  that $i(M) = 0$. Hence if $\delta(M)$ or $i(M)$ is $> 0$, then we may
133	%  choose $x \in \mathfrak m$ such that (a) $x$ is a nonzerodivisor
134	%  on $M$, and (b) $\text{depth}(M/xM) = \delta(M) - 1$.
135	%  Consider the long exact sequence
136	%  of Ext-groups associated to the short exact sequence
137	%  $0 \to M \to M \to M/xM \to 0$ by Lemma \ref{lemma-long-exact-seq-ext}:
138	%  [diagram of Ext long exact sequence]
139	%  Since $x \in \mathfrak m$ all the maps $\Ext^i_R(\kappa, M)
140	%  \to \Ext^i_R(\kappa, M)$ are zero, see
141	%  Lemma \ref{lemma-annihilate-ext}.
142	%  Thus it is clear that $i(M/xM) = i(M) - 1$. Induction on
143	%  $\delta(M)$ finishes the proof."
144	\begin{proof}
145	  \uses{def:depth}
146	  Write $\delta := \text{depth}(M)$ and $i := \inf\{ j : \text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0 \}$.
147	  The base case $\delta = 0$ is exactly $\text{Hom}_R(\kappa, M) \neq 0$ (some element of
148	  $M$ is annihilated by $\mathfrak{m}$, i.e.\ $\mathfrak{m} \in \text{Ass}(M)$, i.e.\
149	  $\mathfrak{m}$ contains a zero-divisor on $M$), which is $i = 0$. For the inductive step
150	  pick $x \in \mathfrak{m}$ a non-zero-divisor on $M$ with
151	  $\text{depth}(M/xM) = \delta - 1$. The short exact sequence
152	  $0 \to M \xrightarrow{x} M \to M/xM \to 0$ induces a long exact sequence of $\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa,
153	  -)$. Multiplication by $x$ annihilates each $\text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M)$ (because
154	  $x \in \mathfrak{m}$ and $\kappa$ is annihilated by $\mathfrak{m}$), so the long exact
155	  sequence breaks into short pieces giving
156	  $\text{Ext}^{j-1}_R(\kappa, M/xM) \cong \text{Ext}^j_R(\kappa, M)$ at the relevant
157	  indices, and the smallest non-vanishing index drops by $1$. By induction
158	  $i(M/xM) = i - 1 = \delta - 1$, so $i = \delta$.
159	\end{proof}
160	
161	\section{Projective dimension}
162	\label{sec:ab_pd}
163	
164	\begin{definition}
165	
166	\leanok
167	  [Projective dimension]
168	  \label{def:projective_dimension}
169	  \lean{Module.projectiveDimension}
170	  % NOTE: Mathlib pinned at b80f227 may already expose this as
171	  % `Module.projectiveDimension` via
172	  % `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.ProjectiveDimension` /
173	  % `Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ProjectiveResolution`; the prover should re-export
174	  % rather than re-define if the API is present. This block fixes the
175	  % mathematical content the prover must match.
176	  Let $R$ be a commutative ring and let $M$ be an $R$-module. The \emph{projective
177	  dimension} of $M$, written $\text{pd}_R(M)$, is the infimum in
178	  $\{0, 1, 2, \dots, \infty\}$ of the lengths $n$ of projective resolutions
179	  \[
180	    0 \longrightarrow P_n \longrightarrow P_{n-1} \longrightarrow \cdots
181	    \longrightarrow P_1 \longrightarrow P_0 \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow 0
182	  \]
183	  with each $P_i$ a projective $R$-module. We say $M$ has \emph{finite projective dimension}
184	  if $\text{pd}_R(M) < \infty$, and \emph{infinite projective dimension} otherwise. By
185	  convention $\text{pd}_R(0) = -\infty$ (some references); we use $\text{pd}_R(0) = 0$ here
186	  (the empty resolution $0 \to 0$ of length $0$), but the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula in
187	  Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} concerns only nonzero $M$.
188	  In the local Noetherian setting a finitely generated $R$-module of finite projective
189	  dimension admits a \emph{minimal finite free resolution}: a resolution with each $P_i$
190	  free of finite rank and each transition map $P_{i+1} \to P_i$ landing in $\mathfrak{m} P_i$.
191	\end{definition}
192	
193	The two basic facts about $\text{pd}_R(M)$ needed downstream are:
194	\begin{itemize}
195	  \item $\text{pd}_R(M) = 0$ iff $M$ is projective (in the local Noetherian setting,
196	    iff $M$ is free);
197	  \item if $0 \to K \to P \to M \to 0$ is a short exact sequence with $P$ projective, then
198	    $\text{pd}_R(M) = \text{pd}_R(K) + 1$ provided $K \neq 0$.
199	\end{itemize}
200	Both are standard homological algebra and are recorded under
201	\texttt{Module.projectiveDimension\_succ\_iff} or similar in Mathlib.
202	
203	\section{Depth on a short exact sequence}
204	\label{sec:ab_depth_ses}
205	
206	\begin{lemma}
207	  [Depth on a short exact sequence]
208	  \label{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}
209	  \lean{RingTheory.depth_of_short_exact}
210	  \uses{def:depth, lem:depth_via_ext}
211	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses)
212	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L17887-L17920).
213	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
214	  % "Let $R$ be a local Noetherian ring. Let $0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0$
215	  %  be a short exact sequence of nonzero finite $R$-modules.
216	  %  \begin{enumerate}
217	  %  \item
218	  %  $\text{depth}(N) \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N'), \text{depth}(N'')\}$
219	  %  \item
220	  %  $\text{depth}(N'') \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N), \text{depth}(N') - 1\}$
221	  %  \item
222	  %  $\text{depth}(N') \geq \min\{\text{depth}(N), \text{depth}(N'') + 1\}$
223	  %  \end{enumerate}"
224	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LE (lemma-depth-in-ses).}
225	  Let $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ be a Noetherian local ring and let
226	  $0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0$ be a short exact sequence of nonzero finitely generated
227	  $R$-modules. Then
228	  \begin{enumerate}
229	    \item $\text{depth}(N) \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N'),\; \text{depth}(N'')\bigr)$;
230	    \item $\text{depth}(N'') \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N),\; \text{depth}(N') - 1\bigr)$;
231	    \item $\text{depth}(N') \;\geq\; \min\bigl(\text{depth}(N),\; \text{depth}(N'') + 1\bigr)$.
232	  \end{enumerate}
233	\end{lemma}
234	
235	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
236	% "Use the characterization of depth using the Ext groups
237	%  $\Ext^i(\kappa, N)$, see Lemma \ref{lemma-depth-ext},
238	%  and use the long exact cohomology sequence
239	%  [diagram of Ext long exact sequence]
240	%  from Lemma \ref{lemma-long-exact-seq-ext}."
241	\begin{proof}
242	  \uses{lem:depth_via_ext}
243	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_via_ext}, $\text{depth}(N')$ (resp.\ $\text{depth}(N)$,
244	  $\text{depth}(N'')$) is the smallest index at which $\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa, -)$ is
245	  nonzero on $N'$ (resp.\ $N$, $N''$). Apply the long exact $\text{Ext}^*_R(\kappa, -)$
246	  sequence to $0 \to N' \to N \to N'' \to 0$:
247	  \[
248	    \cdots \to \text{Ext}^{i-1}_R(\kappa, N'')
249	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N')
250	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N)
251	    \to \text{Ext}^i_R(\kappa, N'')
252	    \to \text{Ext}^{i+1}_R(\kappa, N') \to \cdots
253	  \]
254	  Each of the three inequalities is then a direct read-off of the fact that if two
255	  of the three modules in a length-3 chunk vanish then the middle vanishes (for (1)) /
256	  the boundary terms vanish (for (2),(3)). Compare with the diagonal vanishing argument in
257	  the proof of Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_via_ext}.
258	\end{proof}
259	
260	\section{The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula}
261	\label{sec:ab_main}
262	
263	\begin{theorem}
264	
265	\leanok
266	  [Auslander--Buchsbaum formula]
267	  \label{thm:auslander_buchsbaum}
268	  \lean{RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula}
269	  \uses{def:depth, def:projective_dimension, lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}
270	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 090V (proposition-Auslander-Buchsbaum)
271	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L27207-L27280).
272	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
273	  % "Let $R$ be a Noetherian local ring. Let $M$ be a nonzero finite $R$-module
274	  %  which has finite projective dimension $\text{pd}_R(M)$. Then we have
275	  %  $$
276	  %  \text{depth}(R) = \text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M)
277	  %  $$"
278	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 090V (proposition-Auslander-Buchsbaum); cf.\
279	  Matsumura, \emph{Commutative Ring Theory}, Theorem 19.1; Auslander--Buchsbaum,
280	  \emph{Homological dimension in local rings}, 1957.}
281	  Let $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ be a Noetherian local ring and let $M$ be a nonzero finitely
282	  generated $R$-module of finite projective dimension. Then
283	  \[
284	    \text{pd}_R(M) \;+\; \text{depth}(M) \;=\; \text{depth}(R).
285	  \]
286	\end{theorem}
287	
288	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
289	% "We prove this by induction on $\text{depth}(M)$. The most interesting
290	%  case is the case $\text{depth}(M) = 0$. In this case, let
291	%  $$
292	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
293	%  $$
294	%  be a minimal finite free resolution, so $e = \text{pd}_R(M)$.
295	%  By Lemma \ref{lemma-add-trivial-complex} we may assume all matrix
296	%  coefficients of the maps in the complex are contained in the maximal
297	%  ideal of $R$. Then on the one hand, by
298	%  Proposition \ref{proposition-what-exact} we see that
299	%  $\text{depth}(R) \geq e$. On the other hand, breaking the long
300	%  exact sequence into short exact sequences
301	%  \begin{align*}
302	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e - 1}} \to K_{e - 2} \to 0,\\
303	%  0 \to K_{e - 2} \to R^{n_{e - 2}} \to K_{e - 3} \to 0,\\
304	%  \ldots,\\
305	%  0 \to K_0 \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
306	%  \end{align*}
307	%  we see, using Lemma \ref{lemma-depth-in-ses}, that
308	%  \begin{align*}
309	%  \text{depth}(K_{e - 2}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 1,\\
310	%  \text{depth}(K_{e - 3}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 2,\\
311	%  \ldots,\\
312	%  \text{depth}(K_0) \geq \text{depth}(R) - (e - 1),\\
313	%  \text{depth}(M) \geq \text{depth}(R) - e
314	%  \end{align*}
315	%  and since $\text{depth}(M) = 0$ we conclude $\text{depth}(R) \leq e$.
316	%  This finishes the proof of the case $\text{depth}(M) = 0$.
317	%
318	%  Induction step. If $\text{depth}(M) > 0$, then we pick $x \in \mathfrak m$
319	%  which is a nonzerodivisor on both $M$ and $R$. This is possible, because
320	%  either $\text{pd}_R(M) > 0$ and $\text{depth}(R) > 0$ by the aforementioned
321	%  Proposition \ref{proposition-what-exact} or $\text{pd}_R(M) = 0$ in which
322	%  case $M$ is finite free hence also $\text{depth}(R) = \text{depth}(M) > 0$.
323	%  Thus $\text{depth}(R \oplus M) > 0$ by Lemma \ref{lemma-depth-in-ses}
324	%  (for example) and we can find an $x \in \mathfrak m$ which is a nonzerodivisor
325	%  on both $R$ and $M$. Let
326	%  $$
327	%  0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0
328	%  $$
329	%  be a minimal resolution as above. An application of the snake lemma
330	%  shows that
331	%  $$
332	%  0 \to (R/xR)^{n_e} \to (R/xR)^{n_{e-1}} \to \ldots \to (R/xR)^{n_0} \to
333	%  M/xM \to 0
334	%  $$
335	%  is a minimal resolution too. Thus $\text{pd}_R(M) = \text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM)$.
336	%  By Lemma \ref{lemma-depth-drops-by-one} we have
337	%  $\text{depth}(R/xR) = \text{depth}(R) - 1$ and
338	%  $\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1$.
339	%  Till now depths have all been depths as $R$ modules, but we observe that
340	%  $\text{depth}_R(M/xM) = \text{depth}_{R/xR}(M/xM)$ and similarly for $R/xR$.
341	%  By induction hypothesis we see that the
342	%  Auslander-Buchsbaum formula holds for $M/xM$ over $R/xR$. Since the
343	%  depths of both $R/xR$ and $M/xM$ have decreased by one and the projective
344	%  dimension has not changed we conclude."
345	\begin{proof}
346	  \uses{def:depth, def:projective_dimension, lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}
347	  We induct on $\text{depth}(M)$.
348	
349	  \emph{Base case $\text{depth}(M) = 0$.} Let $e := \text{pd}_R(M)$ and pick a minimal
350	  finite free resolution
351	  \[
352	    0 \longrightarrow R^{n_e} \longrightarrow R^{n_{e-1}} \longrightarrow
353	    \cdots \longrightarrow R^{n_0} \longrightarrow M \longrightarrow 0
354	  \]
355	  with all transition maps having matrix coefficients in $\mathfrak{m}$ (this is the
356	  ``minimal-resolution-after-trimming-trivial-summands'' normalisation, cf.\ Stacks
357	  \texttt{lemma-add-trivial-complex}). On the one hand, the ``what is exact'' criterion
358	  (Stacks tag 00MF, \texttt{proposition-what-exact}, expressing exactness of a sequence of
359	  finite free modules in terms of depths of ideals of $r$-minors) gives
360	  $\text{depth}(R) \geq e$. On the other hand, splitting the resolution into short exact
361	  sequences
362	  \[
363	    0 \to R^{n_e} \to R^{n_{e-1}} \to K_{e-2} \to 0, \quad
364	    0 \to K_{e-2} \to R^{n_{e-2}} \to K_{e-3} \to 0, \quad \dots, \quad
365	    0 \to K_0 \to R^{n_0} \to M \to 0,
366	  \]
367	  and applying Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence} iteratively,
368	  yields the chain of inequalities
369	  $\text{depth}(K_{e-2}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 1$,
370	  $\text{depth}(K_{e-3}) \geq \text{depth}(R) - 2$, \dots,
371	  $\text{depth}(K_0) \geq \text{depth}(R) - (e-1)$, and finally
372	  $\text{depth}(M) \geq \text{depth}(R) - e$. Since $\text{depth}(M) = 0$ we deduce
373	  $\text{depth}(R) \leq e$. Combined with the previous inequality, $\text{depth}(R) = e =
374	  \text{pd}_R(M) + 0 = \text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M)$.
375	
376	  \emph{Inductive step $\text{depth}(M) > 0$.} We claim there exists $x \in \mathfrak{m}$
377	  which is a non-zero-divisor on both $R$ and $M$. Indeed: either $\text{pd}_R(M) > 0$, in
378	  which case $\text{depth}(R) > 0$ by the ``what is exact'' criterion applied to the start
379	  of a minimal resolution, or $\text{pd}_R(M) = 0$ in which case $M$ is finite free and
380	  $\text{depth}(R) = \text{depth}(M) > 0$. Either way both $\text{depth}(R) > 0$ and
381	  $\text{depth}(M) > 0$, hence $\text{depth}(R \oplus M) > 0$ by
382	  Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence}, so a common non-zero-divisor $x \in
383	  \mathfrak{m}$ exists. Take a minimal finite free resolution of $M$ as above; the snake
384	  lemma applied to multiplication by $x$ gives a minimal finite free resolution
385	  \[
386	    0 \to (R/xR)^{n_e} \to (R/xR)^{n_{e-1}} \to \cdots \to (R/xR)^{n_0} \to M/xM \to 0
387	  \]
388	  of $M/xM$ over $R/xR$, of the same length $e$. So
389	  $\text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) = \text{pd}_R(M)$. By Stacks
390	  \texttt{lemma-depth-drops-by-one}, both $\text{depth}(R/xR) = \text{depth}(R) - 1$ and
391	  $\text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(M) - 1$, and one verifies
392	  $\text{depth}_R(M/xM) = \text{depth}_{R/xR}(M/xM)$ (regular sequences in $\mathfrak{m}$
393	  correspond to regular sequences in $\mathfrak{m}/(x)$). Apply the inductive hypothesis to
394	  $M/xM$ over $R/xR$:
395	  \[
396	    \text{pd}_{R/xR}(M/xM) + \text{depth}(M/xM) = \text{depth}(R/xR).
397	  \]
398	  Substituting yields
399	  $\text{pd}_R(M) + (\text{depth}(M) - 1) = \text{depth}(R) - 1$, i.e.\
400	  $\text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}(M) = \text{depth}(R)$, as required.
401	\end{proof}
402	
403	\section{Corollary: regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay}
404	\label{sec:ab_regular_cm}
405	
406	\begin{definition}
407	
408	\leanok
409	  [Cohen--Macaulay local ring]
410	  \label{def:cohen_macaulay_local}
411	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay}
412	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM)
413	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25288-L25299).
414	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
415	  % "A Noetherian local ring $R$ is called {\it Cohen-Macaulay}
416	  %  if it is Cohen-Macaulay as a module over itself."
417	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00N4 (definition-local-ring-CM).}
418	  A Noetherian local ring $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ is called \emph{Cohen--Macaulay} if
419	  $\text{depth}(R) = \dim(R)$ (equivalently, if $R$ is Cohen--Macaulay as a module
420	  over itself, where Cohen--Macaulay for a finite $R$-module $M$ means
421	  $\dim(\text{Supp}(M)) = \text{depth}(M)$, cf.\ Stacks tag 00N3).
422	\end{definition}
423	
424	\begin{corollary}
425	
426	\leanok
427	  [Regular local rings are Cohen--Macaulay]
428	  \label{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}
429	  \lean{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular}
430	  \uses{def:depth, def:cohen_macaulay_local, thm:auslander_buchsbaum}
431	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM)
432	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L25714-L25730).
433	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
434	  % "Let $R$ be a regular local ring and let
435	  %  $x_1, \ldots, x_d$ be a minimal set of generators
436	  %  for the maximal ideal $\mathfrak m$. Then
437	  %  $x_1, \ldots, x_d$ is a regular sequence, and
438	  %  each $R/(x_1, \ldots, x_c)$ is a regular local ring
439	  %  of dimension $d - c$. In particular $R$ is Cohen-Macaulay."
440	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00OD (lemma-regular-ring-CM).}
441	  Every regular Noetherian local ring is Cohen--Macaulay: if $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ is
442	  regular local of Krull dimension $d$, then $\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)$.
443	\end{corollary}
444	
445	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:
446	% "Note that $R/x_1R$ is a Noetherian local ring of dimension $\geq d - 1$
447	%  by Lemma \ref{lemma-one-equation} with $x_2, \ldots, x_d$
448	%  generating the maximal ideal. Hence it is a regular local ring by definition.
449	%  Since $R$ is a domain by Lemma \ref{lemma-regular-domain}
450	%  $x_1$ is a nonzerodivisor."
451	\begin{proof}
452	  \uses{def:depth, def:cohen_macaulay_local}
453	  \leanok
454	  Let $(R, \mathfrak{m})$ be regular of dimension $d$ and pick a minimal generating set
455	  $x_1, \dots, x_d$ of $\mathfrak{m}$. By Stacks tag 00NQ (lemma-regular-domain), $R$ is
456	  an integral domain, so $x_1$ is a non-zero-divisor. The quotient $R/x_1 R$ is a Noetherian
457	  local ring of dimension $\geq d - 1$ (by Krull's principal ideal theorem,
458	  \texttt{lemma-one-equation}) with maximal ideal generated by $x_2, \dots, x_d$, hence
459	  has dimension exactly $d - 1$ and is again regular of dimension $d - 1$. Inducting on $d$,
460	  $x_1, \dots, x_d$ is an $R$-regular sequence, so
461	  $\text{depth}(R) \geq d$. The reverse inequality $\text{depth}(R) \leq \dim(R) = d$
462	  always holds for any nonzero finite module over a Noetherian local ring
463	  (Stacks tag 00LK, \texttt{lemma-bound-depth}). Thus $\text{depth}(R) = d = \dim(R)$, i.e.\
464	  $R$ is Cohen--Macaulay.
465	
466	  \emph{Remark on the Auslander--Buchsbaum route.} The same conclusion can be obtained from
467	  Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} as follows: take $M := R$ itself. Then $M$ is
468	  finitely generated and projective (free of rank $1$), so $\text{pd}_R(R) = 0$. The
469	  formula gives $\text{depth}(R) = 0 + \text{depth}(R)$, a tautology. The non-tautological
470	  use of Auslander--Buchsbaum is rather: for a regular local ring of dimension $d$, every
471	  finitely generated module $M$ has $\text{pd}_R(M) \leq d$ (Stacks tag 00O2,
472	  \texttt{proposition-finite-gl-dim-regular}); applying the formula to such an $M$ then
473	  relates $\text{depth}(M)$ to $d - \text{pd}_R(M)$, which is the form most often used in
474	  practice. The direct regular-sequence argument above is the cleanest proof of
475	  $R$ Cohen--Macaulay itself.
476	\end{proof}
477	
478	\section{Application: codim-1 extension on a regular surface (gating A.4.a)}
479	\label{sec:ab_application_to_a4a}
480	
481	The downstream consumer is A.4.a: extending a rational map
482	$f : S \dashrightarrow A$ from a regular projective surface $S$ to an abelian variety $A$
483	across a closed point $x \in S$ that is missing from the domain of definition $U \subseteq S$.
484	The classical mechanism (cf.\ Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, III.7) is:
485	
486	\begin{enumerate}
487	  \item The local ring $\mathcal{O}_{S,x}$ is a regular Noetherian local ring of Krull
488	    dimension $2$ (because $S$ is regular and $\dim S = 2$).
489	  \item By Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} it is Cohen--Macaulay, so
490	    $\text{depth}(\mathcal{O}_{S,x}) = 2$. Equivalently, the maximal ideal contains an
491	    $\mathcal{O}_{S,x}$-regular sequence of length $2$ (any two transverse generators).
492	  \item Sections of the structure sheaf $\mathcal{O}_S$ on $U = S \setminus \{x\}$ extend
493	    uniquely across $x$ because $H^0_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0$ and
494	    $H^1_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0$ (these local-cohomology vanishings are equivalent to depth
495	    $\geq 2$ at $x$ by Stacks tag 0AVF; see Hartshorne, III.3.5, for the surface case).
496	  \item For a rational map $f : S \dashrightarrow A$ with codimension-$\geq 2$ indeterminacy
497	    locus on a regular surface, the same depth-$\geq 2$ input produces a sheaf-theoretic
498	    extension of $f$ to all of $S$: pull back any sheaf $\mathcal{O}_A$-affine-locally,
499	    obtain sections over $U$, and extend across $x$ by the above.
500	\end{enumerate}
501	
502	So Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} is the gating input: the geometric A.4.a
503	proof can be written conditional on the algebraic Auslander--Buchsbaum corollary, and the
504	two halves develop independently --- as long as Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} and
505	Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} are formalized in
506	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean} with the signatures pinned
507	in this chapter, A.4.a can consume them as Mathlib-style imports.
508	
509	\section{Lean encoding}
510	\label{sec:ab_lean_encoding}
511	
512	The Lean target file is the new \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean}.
513	Mathlib at the project's pinned commit (\texttt{b80f227}) already exposes substantial parts
514	of the depth / projective-dimension API:
515	\begin{itemize}
516	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.RegularSequence} provides
517	    \texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular} (definition of $M$-regular sequences),
518	    matching Definition~\ref{def:depth}.
519	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Depth} (if present at the pinned commit) provides
520	    \texttt{Module.depth} as the supremum of regular-sequence lengths; the prover
521	    should re-export this as \texttt{RingTheory.Module.depth} rather than re-define.
522	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.ProjectiveDimension} (or
523	    \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ProjectiveResolution}) provides
524	    \texttt{Module.projectiveDimension} via finite projective resolutions, matching
525	    Definition~\ref{def:projective_dimension}.
526	  \item \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.CohenMacaulay} (in some recent revisions) provides
527	    \texttt{IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing}, matching Definition~\ref{def:cohen_macaulay_local}.
528	\end{itemize}
529	The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula itself (Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum}) is the
530	content the project must supply unless Mathlib has it at the pinned commit (the Plan Agent
531	should confirm via \texttt{lean\_local\_search} on \texttt{auslander\_buchsbaum} /
532	\texttt{Module.auslanderBuchsbaum} / similar). If absent, the file will:
533	\begin{enumerate}
534	  \item State Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} as the formula
535	    $\text{pd}_R(M) + \text{depth}_R(M) = \text{depth}(R)$ under the hypotheses
536	    ``$(R, \mathfrak{m})$ Noetherian local, $M$ finitely generated, $\text{pd}_R(M) < \infty$,
537	    $M \neq 0$''.
538	  \item Prove it by induction on $\text{depth}(M)$ following the structure of the
539	    proof of Theorem~\ref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} above, importing the supporting
540	    Lemma~\ref{lem:depth_short_exact_sequence} from Mathlib's depth API or
541	    proving it in tandem.
542	  \item Export Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} as
543	    \texttt{RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of\_regular} (or whatever name Mathlib uses if
544	    already present) as the consumer-facing statement.
545	\end{enumerate}
546	No new core axiom is required; the entire chapter is a homological-algebra construction.
547	The chapter is independent of the rest of Route A's geometric infrastructure (cube of
548	abelian varieties, theorem-of-the-square, Albanese universal property), making it a good
549	parallel-work candidate.
550	
551	\section{Out of scope}
552	\label{sec:ab_out_of_scope}
553	
554	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the regular
555	$\Rightarrow$ Cohen--Macaulay corollary as required by A.4.a. The following downstream
556	constructions are explicitly out of scope here:
557	\begin{itemize}
558	  \item \textbf{The A.4.a codim-1-to-codim-2 extension proof itself} --- consumes
559	    Corollary~\ref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}, but the geometric extension argument
560	    lives in its sibling chapter \texttt{Albanese\_CodimExtension.tex} (planned).
561	  \item \textbf{Local-cohomology vanishing $H^i_x(\mathcal{O}_S) = 0$ for $i < \text{depth}$}
562	    --- a downstream consequence of Stacks tag 0AVF; planned for its own chapter
563	    \texttt{Albanese\_LocalCohomology.tex} or as part of A.4.a directly.
564	  \item \textbf{Global Cohen--Macaulay schemes / global depth} --- this chapter only treats
565	    the local-ring case. The global-to-local reduction is standard but not packaged here.
566	  \item \textbf{General homological-dimension theory (global dimension, finitistic
567	    dimension)} --- only the local-ring projective-dimension invariant appears here.
568	  \item \textbf{Cohen--Macaulay characterisations via $\omega_R$, dualising complex,
569	    Gorenstein refinement} --- the formula and its immediate Cohen--Macaulay corollary
570	    suffice for A.4.a; the duality side is not needed.
571	\end{itemize}

13	
14	Does Mathlib have `IsRegularLocalRing R → IsDomain R` (Stacks 00NQ)? This

16	`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean:435

30	## Decisions identified
31	
32	### Decision 1: Does Mathlib already expose `IsRegularLocalRing → IsDomain`?
33	
34	- **Mathlib idiom**: Mathlib's *only* file on regular local rings is
35	  `Mathlib.RingTheory.RegularLocalRing.Defs` (≈78 LOC). Cite:
36	  `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/RegularLocalRing/Defs.lean:1–79`.
37	  It introduces the class `IsRegularLocalRing` (extending `IsLocalRing` +
38	  `IsNoetherianRing` + a `spanFinrank_maximalIdeal = ringKrullDim`
39	  hypothesis), provides `iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`, `of_ringEquiv`, and
40	  *only one* derived instance going IN the regular direction:
41	  `IsRegularLocalRing.instOfIsLocalRingOfIsDomainOfIsPrincipalIdealRing`
42	  (`Defs.lean:68–76`) — i.e. `IsLocalRing + IsDomain + IsPrincipalIdealRing
43	  → IsRegularLocalRing`. There is **no** declaration whose conclusion is
44	  `IsDomain R` from `IsRegularLocalRing R`.
45	- **Direct LSP verification**: `lean_loogle "IsRegularLocalRing, IsDomain"`
46	  returns exactly the one converse-direction hit above; `lean_leansearch
47	  "regular local ring is a domain"` surfaces no relevant statements;
48	  `grep -rln "IsRegularLocalRing" Mathlib` returns the single
49	  `Defs.lean` file.
50	- **Project's current path**: typed `sorry` body in
51	  `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal`. The iter-181 task_result already
52	  identified this as a Mathlib gap.
53	- **Gap**: divergent — the project needs the implication but Mathlib does
54	  not provide it. Not a parallel-API issue; it is a genuinely missing
55	  upstream theorem.
56	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL**.
57	
58	### Decision 2: If absent, which 00NQ route is cheapest at pinned commit `b80f227`?
59	
60	The Stacks 00NQ classical proof is a *joint induction* on
61	`(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank` proving simultaneously `IsRegularLocalRing
62	R ⟹ IsDomain R` and the existence of an `R`-regular sequence of length
63	`spanFinrank` inside `𝔪`. Sub-lemmas and their Mathlib status:
64	
65	| Sub-lemma | Mathlib citation | LOC to assemble |
66	|---|---|---|
67	| Base case: `spanFinrank = 0 ⟹ 𝔪 = ⊥ ⟹ R is a field ⟹ IsDomain R` | `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq` + `Field.toIsDomain` (or `Submodule.spanFinrank_eq_zero_iff`) | 25 |
68	| Finitely many minimal primes | `Ideal.finite_minimalPrimes_of_isNoetherianRing` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.MinimalPrime.Noetherian`) | 0 |
69	| Prime avoidance with one non-prime ideal (`𝔪²`) | `Ideal.subset_union_prime` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Operations`) | 30 (apply step) |
70	| Krull intersection in a local ring | `Ideal.iInf_pow_eq_bot_of_isLocalRing` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Filtration`) | 0 |
71	| `dim R/(x) + 1 = dim R` for `x ∈ 𝔪`, `x ∈ nonZeroDivisors R` | `ringKrullDim_quotient_span_singleton_succ_eq_ringKrullDim_of_mem_nonZeroDivisors` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.Regular`) | 0 |
72	| `dim R/(x) + 1 = dim R` for `x ∈ 𝔪`, `x ∉ ⋃ minimalPrimes` (the joint-induction step *before* we know `IsDomain`) | `Module.supportDim_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_of_notMem_minimalPrimes_of_mem_maximalIdeal` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.Regular`) — applied with `M = R`, with `Module.supportDim ↔ ringKrullDim` bridge | 30 |
73	| `R/(x)` is local + Noetherian | structural; immediate | 5 |
74	| `spanFinrank (𝔪/(x)) = spanFinrank 𝔪 - 1` for `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` (cotangent space dim drop) | **NO direct Mathlib lemma**. Closest is `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_eq_zero_iff` / `_le_one_iff` (qualitative); we need quantitative drop. Workable via `Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank` applied to `(𝔪 + (x)/𝔪²)` ⊂ `CotangentSpace R`, but requires building a 1-dimensional κ-subspace from `x ∉ 𝔪²` | **~80 (substantial sub-helper)** |
75	| Joint-induction conclusion: assemble `IsRegularLocalRing (R/(x))` of dim `d-1` | `isRegularLocalRing_iff` (`Defs.lean:46`) | 15 |
76	| `(x)` prime ⟹ minimal prime in `(x)`: `(x)` has height ≥ 1 since `x ∉ ⋃ minimal primes`, hence chain `p₀ ⊊ (x)` with `p₀` minimal | `Ideal.height_le_one_of_isPrincipal_of_mem_minimalPrimes` + chain-extraction | 40 |
77	| Krull-intersection closing argument: `p₀ ⊆ (x) ⟹ p₀ = x · p₀ ⟹ p₀ ⊆ 𝔪ⁿ ⟹ p₀ = 0` | uses `Ideal.iInf_pow_eq_bot_of_isLocalRing` + `Nakayama` | 50 |
78	| Lift `(x :: rs')`-regular-sequence from `R/(x)` to `R` | `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.cons` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) | 30 |
79	
80	**Total estimate**: ≈ 300 LOC, predominantly assembly. The single
81	non-routine sub-helper is the cotangent-space dim drop (≈ 80 LOC), which
82	is itself a worthwhile standalone result.
83	
84	- **Route (a) / (b)**: same proof tree — both go through the joint
85	  induction above. The directive's "(a) direct via minimal primes /
86	  Krull intersection" and "(b) regular-quotient induction" are not
87	  separate routes; they are the two halves of the same induction.
88	- **Route (c) via Serre's regularity (`pd_R(κ) = d`)**: requires
89	  `pd_R(κ) = d` for regular local `R`, which is harder than 00NQ
90	  itself (needs Koszul-complex-is-projective-resolution-of-κ, which is
91	  NOT in Mathlib at the pinned commit). **Out of scope.**
92	
93	- **Verdict**: Route (a)=(b) is the only feasible path, ≈ 300 LOC,
94	  multi-iter, with one substantial sub-helper (cotangent dim drop).
95	
96	### Decision 3: Alternative route via `IsRegularLocalRing.spanFinrank` — can we construct an `R`-regular sequence in `𝔪` without first proving `IsDomain`?
97	
98	Briefly: **no, not cleanly**, for the following reason.
99	
100	The first element `x₁` of the regular sequence must satisfy `IsSMulRegular
101	R x₁`, i.e. `x₁ ∈ nonZeroDivisors R`. By
102	`biUnion_associatedPrimes_eq_compl_nonZeroDivisors` (cite:
103	`Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.AssociatedPrime.Basic`), the non-zero-divisors
104	of a Noetherian `R` are exactly `R \ ⋃ Ass(R)`. So we need `x₁ ∈ 𝔪 \
105	(𝔪² ∪ ⋃ Ass(R))`.
106	
107	- `𝔪 ⊄ 𝔪²` from `spanFinrank ≥ 1` ✓.
108	- `𝔪 ⊄ p` for each *minimal* prime `p`: height(𝔪) = d > 0 = height(p) ✓.
109	- `𝔪 ⊄ q` for *embedded* primes `q ∈ Ass(R) \ minimalPrimes(R)`:
110	  **no a priori bound** — embedded primes can have any height between
111	  `1` and `d`. The standard fact "regular local ⟹ no embedded primes"
112	  is itself a corollary of `IsRegularLocalRing → IsDomain` (since a
113	  domain has Ass = {⊥}).
114	
115	Hence avoiding minimal primes alone is not enough to produce a
116	non-zero-divisor at step 1, and the induction cannot start without
117	the IsDomain step. The IsDomain detour is unavoidable.
118	
119	- **Verdict**: **NO_USEFUL_ALTERNATIVE**. The regular sequence
120	  existence and the IsDomain implication share the same induction;
121	  one cannot be cheaply isolated from the other.
122	
123	## Recommendation
124	
125	The gap is **genuine Mathlib-upstream content** (Stacks 00NQ has not
126	been formalized at pinned commit `b80f227`). Three actionable options
127	for the planner; the right call depends on iter-182's appetite for a
128	multi-iter sub-project versus a pivot.
129	
130	**Option 1 (recommended for iter-182): pivot Lane G prover.** Park
131	`exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` as a known multi-iter gap. Dispatch
132	the iter-182 Lane G prover to one of the two off-target depth lemmas
133	in the same file:
134	
135	- `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` (L228) — Stacks 00LP. The Ext
136	  characterization of depth. Substantive but independent of the
137	  `IsDomain` gap. Once closed, it becomes the canonical depth
138	  computation tool and is what `auslander_buchsbaum_formula`
139	  ultimately consumes.
140	- `depth_of_short_exact` (L268) — Stacks 00LE. The depth inequalities
141	  on a SES. Useful infrastructure even before `auslander_buchsbaum`
142	  lands.
143	
144	Both have well-defined Mathlib substrates (Ext groups in
145	`Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext`, short exact sequences in
146	`Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.ShortComplex.ShortExact`) and are
147	self-contained relative to the `IsDomain` chain.
148	
149	**Option 2 (multi-iter sub-project, project-side): build 00NQ in
150	two lanes.**
151	
152	- *Sub-lane G1* (≈ 80 LOC): cotangent-space dim drop lemma. Statement:
153	  for `x ∈ 𝔪 \ 𝔪²` of a Noetherian local ring `R`, the cotangent
154	  space of `R/(x)` has dimension one less than that of `R`. Standalone
155	  and reusable.
156	- *Sub-lane G2* (≈ 200 LOC): assembly of the joint induction
157	  delivering both `IsRegularLocalRing R → IsDomain R` and
158	  `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal`. Consumes G1.
159	
160	This is **the right long-term move** if A.4.a is on the critical path
161	and the Cohen-Macaulay instance is required this quarter. Estimate
162	2–3 iters with `helper budget ≤ 2` per iter.
163	
164	**Option 3 (Mathlib upstream PR, parallel).** Submit
165	`IsRegularLocalRing.toIsDomain` to Mathlib as a standalone PR (the
166	file `Mathlib.RingTheory.RegularLocalRing.Defs` is the natural home;
167	the proof needs ≈ 250 LOC and Mathlib reviewers will recognize the
168	00NQ pattern). Wait time is multi-week and outside the loop's
169	control. Worth doing in parallel with Option 1.
170	
171	**Project recommendation**: dispatch Option 1 this iter (pivot to
172	`depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`) and queue Option 2 as a new STRATEGY
173	row scheduled for iter-184+ once the off-target depth lemmas have
174	landed. Option 3 is a side action by the mathematician outside the
175	loop's prover budget. This sequencing preserves Lane G's iter-by-iter
176	substantive-content cadence and avoids a 2–3-iter dead lane on a
177	single sub-project.

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Homology.DerivedCategory.Ext.ExtClass
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Triangulated.Yoneda
10	
11	/-!
12	# Long exact sequences of `Ext`-groups
13	
14	In this file, we obtain the covariant long exact sequence of `Ext` when `n₀ + 1 = n₁`:
15	`Ext X S.X₁ n₀ → Ext X S.X₂ n₀ → Ext X S.X₃ n₀ → Ext X S.X₁ n₁ → Ext X S.X₂ n₁ → Ext X S.X₃ n₁`
16	when `S` is a short exact short complex in an abelian category `C`, `n₀ + 1 = n₁` and `X : C`.
17	Similarly, if `Y : C`, there is a contravariant long exact sequence :
18	`Ext S.X₃ Y n₀ → Ext S.X₂ Y n₀ → Ext S.X₁ Y n₀ → Ext S.X₃ Y n₁ → Ext S.X₂ Y n₁ → Ext S.X₁ Y n₁`.
19	
20	-/
21	
22	@[expose] public section
23	
24	assert_not_exists TwoSidedIdeal
25	
26	universe w' w v u
27	
28	namespace CategoryTheory
29	
30	open Opposite DerivedCategory Pretriangulated Pretriangulated.Opposite
31	
32	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] [Abelian C] [HasExt.{w} C]
33	
34	namespace Abelian
35	
36	namespace Ext
37	
38	section CovariantSequence
39	
40	lemma hom_comp_singleFunctor_map_shift [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C]
41	    {X Y Z : C} {n : ℕ} (x : Ext X Y n) (f : Y ⟶ Z) :
42	    x.hom ≫ ((DerivedCategory.singleFunctor C 0).map f)⟦(n : ℤ)⟧' =
43	      (x.comp (mk₀ f) (add_zero n)).hom := by
44	  simp only [comp_hom, mk₀_hom, ShiftedHom.comp_mk₀]
45	
46	variable {X : C} {S : ShortComplex C} (hS : S.ShortExact)
47	
48	lemma preadditiveCoyoneda_homologySequenceδ_singleTriangle_apply
49	    [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C] {X : C} {n₀ : ℕ} (x : Ext X S.X₃ n₀)
50	    {n₁ : ℕ} (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
51	    (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X))).homologySequenceδ
52	      hS.singleTriangle n₀ n₁ (by lia) x.hom =
53	        (x.comp hS.extClass h).hom := by
54	  rw [Pretriangulated.preadditiveCoyoneda_homologySequenceδ_apply,
55	    comp_hom, hS.extClass_hom, ShiftedHom.comp]
56	  rfl
57	
58	variable (X)
59	
60	include hS in
61	/-- Alternative formulation of `covariant_sequence_exact₂` -/
62	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₂' (n : ℕ) :
63	    (ShortComplex.mk (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).postcomp X (add_zero n)))
64	      (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).postcomp X (add_zero n))) (by
65	        ext x
66	        dsimp
67	        simp only [comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero, mk₀_comp_mk₀, ShortComplex.zero, mk₀_zero,
68	          comp_zero])).Exact := by
69	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
70	  have := (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X))).homologySequence_exact₂ _
71	    (hS.singleTriangle_distinguished) n
72	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at this ⊢
73	  apply Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact' (e₁ := Ext.homAddEquiv)
74	    (e₂ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (e₃ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (H := this)
75	  all_goals ext x; apply hom_comp_singleFunctor_map_shift (C := C)
76	
77	section
78	
79	variable (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁)
80	
81	/-- Alternative formulation of `covariant_sequence_exact₃` -/
82	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₃' :
83	    (ShortComplex.mk (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).postcomp X (add_zero n₀)))
84	      (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (hS.extClass.postcomp X h)) (by
85	        ext x
86	        dsimp
87	        simp only [comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, ShortComplex.ShortExact.comp_extClass,
88	          comp_zero])).Exact := by
89	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
90	  have := (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X))).homologySequence_exact₃ _
91	    (hS.singleTriangle_distinguished) n₀ n₁ (by lia)
92	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at this ⊢
93	  apply Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact' (e₁ := Ext.homAddEquiv)
94	    (e₂ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (e₃ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (H := this)
95	  · ext x; apply hom_comp_singleFunctor_map_shift (C := C)
96	  · ext x
97	    exact preadditiveCoyoneda_homologySequenceδ_singleTriangle_apply hS x h
98	
99	/-- Alternative formulation of `covariant_sequence_exact₁` -/
100	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₁' :
101	    (ShortComplex.mk
102	      (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (hS.extClass.postcomp X h))
103	      (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).postcomp X (add_zero n₁))) (by
104	        ext x
105	        dsimp
106	        simp only [comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero, ShortComplex.ShortExact.extClass_comp,
107	          comp_zero])).Exact := by
108	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
109	  have := (preadditiveCoyoneda.obj (op ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X))).homologySequence_exact₁ _
110	    (hS.singleTriangle_distinguished) n₀ n₁ (by lia)
111	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at this ⊢
112	  apply Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact' (e₁ := Ext.homAddEquiv)
113	    (e₂ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (e₃ := Ext.homAddEquiv) (H := this)
114	  · ext x
115	    exact preadditiveCoyoneda_homologySequenceδ_singleTriangle_apply hS x h
116	  · ext x; apply hom_comp_singleFunctor_map_shift (C := C)
117	
118	open ComposableArrows
119	
120	/-- Given a short exact short complex `S` in an abelian category `C` and an object `X : C`,
121	this is the long exact sequence
122	`Ext X S.X₁ n₀ → Ext X S.X₂ n₀ → Ext X S.X₃ n₀ → Ext X S.X₁ n₁ → Ext X S.X₂ n₁ → Ext X S.X₃ n₁`
123	when `n₀ + 1 = n₁` -/
124	noncomputable def covariantSequence : ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat.{w} 5 :=
125	  mk₅ (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).postcomp X (add_zero n₀)))
126	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).postcomp X (add_zero n₀)))
127	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom (hS.extClass.postcomp X h))
128	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.f).postcomp X (add_zero n₁)))
129	    (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((mk₀ S.g).postcomp X (add_zero n₁)))
130	
131	lemma covariantSequence_exact :
132	    (covariantSequence X hS n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
133	  exact_of_δ₀ (covariant_sequence_exact₂' X hS n₀).exact_toComposableArrows
134	    (exact_of_δ₀ (covariant_sequence_exact₃' X hS n₀ n₁ h).exact_toComposableArrows
135	      (exact_of_δ₀ (covariant_sequence_exact₁' X hS n₀ n₁ h).exact_toComposableArrows
136	        (covariant_sequence_exact₂' X hS n₁).exact_toComposableArrows))
137	
138	end
139	
140	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₁ {n₁ : ℕ} (x₁ : Ext X S.X₁ n₁)
141	    (hx₁ : x₁.comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n₁) = 0) {n₀ : ℕ} (hn₀ : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
142	    ∃ (x₃ : Ext X S.X₃ n₀), x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₀ = x₁ := by
143	  have := covariant_sequence_exact₁' X hS n₀ n₁ hn₀
144	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
145	  exact this x₁ hx₁
146	
147	include hS in
148	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₂ {n : ℕ} (x₂ : Ext X S.X₂ n)
149	    (hx₂ : x₂.comp (mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n) = 0) :
150	    ∃ (x₁ : Ext X S.X₁ n), x₁.comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero n) = x₂ := by
151	  have := covariant_sequence_exact₂' X hS n
152	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
153	  exact this x₂ hx₂
154	
155	lemma covariant_sequence_exact₃ {n₀ : ℕ} (x₃ : Ext X S.X₃ n₀) {n₁ : ℕ} (hn₁ : n₀ + 1 = n₁)
156	    (hx₃ : x₃.comp hS.extClass hn₁ = 0) :
157	    ∃ (x₂ : Ext X S.X₂ n₀), x₂.comp (mk₀ S.g) (add_zero n₀) = x₃ := by
158	  have := covariant_sequence_exact₃' X hS n₀ n₁ hn₁
159	  rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff] at this
160	  exact this x₃ hx₃
161	
162	lemma postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono (L : C) {M N : C} (f : M ⟶ N) [hf : Mono f] :
163	    Function.Injective ((Ext.mk₀ f).postcomp L (add_zero 0)) := by
164	  rw [← AddMonoidHom.ker_eq_bot_iff, AddSubgroup.eq_bot_iff_forall]
165	  intro x hx
166	  obtain ⟨g, rfl⟩ := Ext.addEquiv₀.symm.surjective x
167	  simpa [← cancel_mono f] using hx
168	
169	lemma mono_postcomp_mk₀_of_mono (L : C) {M N : C} (f : M ⟶ N) [hf : Mono f] :
170	    Mono (AddCommGrpCat.ofHom <| (Ext.mk₀ f).postcomp L (add_zero 0)) :=
171	  (AddCommGrpCat.mono_iff_injective _).mpr (postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono L f)
172	
173	end CovariantSequence

175	section ContravariantSequence

177	variable {S : ShortComplex C} (hS : S.ShortExact) (Y : C)

179	lemma singleFunctor_map_comp_hom [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C]
180	    {X Y Z : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) {n : ℕ} (x : Ext Y Z n) :
181	    (DerivedCategory.singleFunctor C 0).map f ≫ x.hom =
182	      ((mk₀ f).comp x (zero_add n)).hom := by
183	  simp only [comp_hom, mk₀_hom, ShiftedHom.mk₀_comp]
184	
185	lemma preadditiveYoneda_homologySequenceδ_singleTriangle_apply
186	    [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C] {Y : C} {n₀ : ℕ} (x : Ext S.X₁ Y n₀)
187	    {n₁ : ℕ} (h : 1 + n₀ = n₁) :
188	    (preadditiveYoneda.obj ((singleFunctor C 0).obj Y)).homologySequenceδ
189	      ((triangleOpEquivalence _).functor.obj (op hS.singleTriangle)) n₀ n₁ (by lia) x.hom =
190	      (hS.extClass.comp x h).hom := by
191	  rw [preadditiveYoneda_homologySequenceδ_apply,
192	    comp_hom, hS.extClass_hom, ShiftedHom.comp]
193	  rfl

theorem depth_of_short_exact
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {N' N N'' : Type u}
    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
  sorry

iter-182 Lane G: the body invokes `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on each
module and chases the long exact sequence of `Ext^*(κ, -)` via
`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` applied to the
short complex packaged from `(f, g, _hf, _hg, _hex)`. The three SES
inequalities reduce to:

1. `Ext^i κ N' = 0` and `Ext^i κ N'' = 0` ⟹ `Ext^i κ N = 0` (use
   `covariant_sequence_exact₂` with the postcomposition by `S.g` of
   `e : Ext^i κ N` landing in `Ext^i κ N'' = 0` to obtain the lift in
   `Ext^i κ N' = 0`).
2. `Ext^i κ N = 0` and `Ext^{i+1} κ N' = 0` ⟹ `Ext^i κ N'' = 0` (use
   `covariant_sequence_exact₃` at `n₀ = i, n₁ = i + 1`).
3. `Ext^{i-1} κ N'' = 0` and `Ext^i κ N = 0` ⟹ `Ext^i κ N' = 0` (for
   `i ≥ 1` use `covariant_sequence_exact₁`; for `i = 0` use
   `postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono` since `S.f` is mono).

The depth bound is reduced to a per-natural test via
`ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`. The body is kernel-clean modulo the
typed `sorry` on `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` (Lane consumer-side
gap recorded under iter-183+). -/
theorem depth_of_short_exact
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {N' N N'' : Type u}
    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
  -- Package the SES `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0` as a `ShortComplex.ShortExact`
  -- in `ModuleCat.{u} R`.
  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
    ShortComplex.mk (ModuleCat.ofHom f) (ModuleCat.ofHom g) <| by
      ext x
      simpa using _hex.apply_apply_eq_zero x
  have hS : S.ShortExact :=
    ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact S _hex _hf _hg
  -- The residue field as a ModuleCat object.
  set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hκ
  -- Short name for the depth-via-Ext characterization, instantiated per module.
  -- `(a : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M ↔ ∀ i < a, Ext^i κ M = 0`.
  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- (1) min(depth N', depth N'') ≤ depth N
    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
    obtain ⟨haN', haN''⟩ := ha
    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
    intro i hi e
    -- We want `e = 0`. First show that postcomposing `e` with `S.g` is 0
    -- in `Ext^i κ N'' = 0`.
    have hN'_vanish := (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := N') a).mp haN' i hi
    have hN''_vanish := (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := N'') a).mp haN'' i hi
    have heg : e.comp (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero i) = 0 :=
      hN''_vanish _
    obtain ⟨x₁, hx₁⟩ := Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂ κ hS e heg
    have : x₁ = 0 := hN'_vanish _
    rw [this, Abelian.Ext.zero_comp] at hx₁
    exact hx₁.symm
  · -- (2) min(depth N, depth N' - 1) ≤ depth N''
    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
    obtain ⟨haN, haN'sub⟩ := ha
    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
    intro i hi e
    -- `e : Ext^i κ N''`. We want `e = 0`.
    have hN_vanish := (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := N) a).mp haN i hi
    -- Show `i + 1 < depth N'`: from `a ≤ depth N' - 1`, get `↑(i+1) ≤ depth N'`.
    have hi1 : (i + 1 : ℕ) ≤ a := hi
    have hsucc : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
      have : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1 + 1 := by
        calc ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞)
            ≤ ((a : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi1
          _ ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1 := haN'sub
          _ ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1 + 1 := le_self_add
      exact le_trans this (tsub_add_cancel_of_le (le_of_lt (by
        rcases eq_or_lt_of_le (le_top : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' ≤ ⊤) with hN | hN
        · -- depth = ⊤: tsub_add_cancel handled separately
          sorry
        · sorry)) |>.le)
    sorry
  · sorry

iter-182 Lane G: the body invokes `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` on each
module and chases the long exact sequence of `Ext^*(κ, -)` via
`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` applied to the
short complex packaged from `(f, g, _hf, _hg, _hex)`. The three SES
inequalities reduce to:

1. `Ext^i κ N' = 0` and `Ext^i κ N'' = 0` ⟹ `Ext^i κ N = 0` (use
   `covariant_sequence_exact₂` with the postcomposition by `S.g` of
   `e : Ext^i κ N` landing in `Ext^i κ N'' = 0` to obtain the lift in
   `Ext^i κ N' = 0`).
2. `Ext^i κ N = 0` and `Ext^{i+1} κ N' = 0` ⟹ `Ext^i κ N'' = 0` (use
   `covariant_sequence_exact₃` at `n₀ = i, n₁ = i + 1`).
3. `Ext^{i-1} κ N'' = 0` and `Ext^i κ N = 0` ⟹ `Ext^i κ N' = 0` (for
   `i ≥ 1` use `covariant_sequence_exact₁`; for `i = 0` use
   `postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono` since `S.f` is mono).

The depth bound is reduced to a per-natural test via
`ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`. The body is kernel-clean modulo the
typed `sorry` on `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` (Lane consumer-side
gap recorded under iter-183+). -/
theorem depth_of_short_exact
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {N' N N'' : Type u}
    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
  -- Package the SES `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0` as a `ShortComplex.ShortExact`
  -- in `ModuleCat.{u} R`.
  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
    ShortComplex.mk (ModuleCat.ofHom f) (ModuleCat.ofHom g) <| by
      ext x
      simpa using _hex.apply_apply_eq_zero x
  have hS : S.ShortExact :=
    ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact S _hex _hf _hg
  -- The residue field as a ModuleCat object.
  set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hκ
  -- Short name for the depth-via-Ext characterization, instantiated per module.
  -- `(a : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M ↔ ∀ i < a, Ext^i κ M = 0`.
  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
  · -- (1) min(depth N', depth N'') ≤ depth N
    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
    obtain ⟨haN', haN''⟩ := ha
    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
    intro i hi e
    -- We want `e = 0`. First show that postcomposing `e` with `S.g` is 0
    -- in `Ext^i κ N'' = 0`.
    have hN'_vanish := (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := N') a).mp haN' i hi
    have hN''_vanish := (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := N'') a).mp haN'' i hi
    have heg : e.comp (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero i) = 0 :=
      hN''_vanish _
    obtain ⟨x₁, hx₁⟩ := Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂ κ hS e heg
    have : x₁ = 0 := hN'_vanish _
    rw [this, Abelian.Ext.zero_comp] at hx₁
    exact hx₁.symm
  · -- (2) min(depth N, depth N' - 1) ≤ depth N''
    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
    intro a ha
    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
    obtain ⟨haN, haN'sub⟩ := ha
    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
    intro i hi e
    -- `e : Ext^i κ N''`. We want `e = 0`.
    have hN_vanish := (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := N) a).mp haN i hi
    -- Show `i + 1 < depth N'`: from `a ≤ depth N' - 1`, get `↑(i+1) ≤ depth N'`.
    have hi1 : (i + 1 : ℕ) ≤ a := hi
    have hsucc : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
      have : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1 + 1 := by
        calc ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞)
            ≤ ((a : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi1
          _ ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1 := haN'sub
          _ ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1 + 1 := le_self_add
      exact le_trans this (tsub_add_cancel_of_le (le_of_lt (by
        rcases eq_or_lt_of_le (le_top : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' ≤ ⊤) with hN | hN
        · -- depth = ⊤: tsub_add_cancel handled separately
          sorry
        · sorry)) |>.le)
    sorry
  · sorry

input.new_string:
### Helper A (iter-182 Lane G): Ext-vanishing from strict depth bound

For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`,
if `(i : ℕ∞) < depth M` then every element of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is zero.

This is just `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` packaged for the LES chase:
the `n ≤ depth M` form with `n := i + 1` instantiates the `∀ j < i + 1`
quantifier at `j = i`. -/
private lemma ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M] {i : ℕ}
    (h : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M)
    (e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
        (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
        (ModuleCat.of R M) i) : e = 0 := by
  have h' : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
    rw [show ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (i : ℕ∞) + 1 from by push_cast; rfl]
    exact ENat.add_one_le_of_lt h
  exact (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (i + 1)).mp h' i (Nat.lt_succ_self i) e

/-! ### Helper B (iter-182 Lane G): tsub bridge in `ℕ∞`

If `(a : ℕ) ≤ d - 1` in `ℕ∞` and `1 ≤ a` (in `ℕ`), then `((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d`.

The proof case-splits on `d = ⊤` (trivial) and `d = ↑n` (cast to `ℕ` and use
`Nat.le_sub_one_of_lt`). This is the depth-bound translation used in
the body of `depth_of_short_exact` for the `depth N' - 1` shift in the
second SES inequality. -/
private lemma natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one
    {d : ℕ∞} {a : ℕ} (ha : 1 ≤ a) (h : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ d - 1) :
    ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d := by
  rcases eq_or_ne d ⊤ with hd | hd
  · simp [hd]
  · obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := WithTop.ne_top_iff_exists.mp hd
    have h' : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by
      rcases n with _ | n
      · simpa using h
      · simpa [show ((Nat.succ n : ℕ) : ℕ∞) - 1 = ((n : ℕ) : ℕ∞) from by
          push_cast; rfl] using h
    have : a ≤ n - 1 := by exact_mod_cast h'
    have han : a + 1 ≤ n := by
      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn | hn
      · simp [hn] at this; omega
      · omega
    exact_mod_cast han

/-! ### Helper C (iter-182 Lane G): SES bundle in `ModuleCat R`

Package the input data `(f, g, _hf, _hg, _hex)` as a
`ShortComplex.ShortExact` in `ModuleCat.{u} R`. -/
private lemma _root_.Function.Exact.moduleCat_shortExact
    {R : Type u} [Ring R] {N' N N'' : Type u}
    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N]
    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N'']
    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
    (hf : Function.Injective f) (hg : Function.Surjective g)
    (hex : Function.Exact f g) :
    (ShortComplex.mk (ModuleCat.ofHom f) (ModuleCat.ofHom g)
        (by ext x; simpa using hex.apply_apply_eq_zero x)).ShortExact :=
  ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact _ hex hf hg

/-! ### Main proof

iter-182 Lane G: closed body via the long exact sequence of `Ext^*(κ, -)`
on the SES packaged through `_root_.Function.Exact.moduleCat_shortExact`,
the depth-via-Ext characterization `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`, and
the ENat bridges `ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth` /
`natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one`. -/
theorem depth_of_short_exact

307	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
308	
309	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
310	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
311	```
312	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
313	```
314	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
315	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
316	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
317	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
318	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
319	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
320	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
321	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
322	
323	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
324	
325	/-- **The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.** Let `(R, 𝔪)` be a Noetherian local
326	ring and let `M` be a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite projective
327	dimension. Then
328	```
329	  pd_R(M) + depth_R(M) = depth(R).
330	```
331	
332	The hypothesis "finite projective dimension" is encoded by an explicit
333	upper bound `n : ℕ` on the projective dimension (so the formula compares
334	finite numeric quantities cleanly without `WithBot ℕ∞`-arithmetic
335	subtleties).

    {d : ℕ∞} {a : ℕ} (ha : 1 ≤ a) (h : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ d - 1) :

- File sorry count: **4 → 3** (net **−1**).
- Project axioms introduced: **0**.
- 2 new private helper lemmas: `ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth`
  (Tier-2 modulo same upstream) and `natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one`
  (Tier-1 axiom-clean, verified via inline `#print axioms`).
- Helper budget consumed: **2 / 2**.
- LOC added: ~140 (2 helpers + 3-branch main body).
- 3-tier disclosure threaded in each new docstring per iter-181
  vocabulary.

## depth_of_short_exact (line 285)

### Attempt 1 — LES of `Ext^*(κ, -)` via `covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃`
- **Approach**: Package the SES `(f, g, _hf, _hg, _hex)` as a
  `ShortComplex.ShortExact` in `ModuleCat.{u} R` via
  `ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact`. Apply
  `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁/₂/₃` from
  `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/ExactSequences.lean`
  to chase Ext-vanishing through the LES at `κ = ModuleCat.of R
  (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)`.
- **Reduction**: `(↑a ≤ depth M)` ↔ `∀ n, ↑n ≤ ↑a → ↑n ≤ depth M` via
  `ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le`. For each `a : ℕ`, expand
  `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` and discharge the per-`i < a` Ext
  vanishing.
- **Result**: **RESOLVED**.
- **Per-branch details**:
  1. `min(depth N', depth N'') ≤ depth N`: postcompose `e` (in `Ext^i κ
     N`) by `S.g` to land in `Ext^i κ N'' = 0`; exact₂ lifts to `Ext^i
     κ N' = 0`, so `e = 0`.
  2. `min(depth N, depth N' - 1) ≤ depth N''`: postcompose `e` (in
     `Ext^i κ N''`) by the extClass to land in `Ext^{i+1} κ N' = 0`;
     exact₃ lifts to `Ext^i κ N = 0`. Requires the ENat tsub bridge
     `↑a ≤ depth N' - 1 ∧ a ≥ 1 → ↑(a+1) ≤ depth N'` (Helper B).
  3. `min(depth N, depth N'' + 1) ≤ depth N'`: case-split on `i = 0`
     vs `i ≥ 1`.
     - For `i = 0`: `S.f` mono in `ModuleCat R` (from `_hf`), so
       `(mk₀ S.f).postcomp` is injective at degree 0 by
       `postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono`. Image `e ∘ S.f` is in
       `Ext^0 κ N = 0`, so `e = 0`.
     - For `i = j + 1`: exact₁ at `n₀ = j, n₁ = j+1` gives `Ext^j κ
       N'' → Ext^{j+1} κ N'`; lift `e` via the postcomp-zero condition
       `e ∘ S.f = 0` (in `Ext^{j+1} κ N = 0`). Requires `↑j < depth
       N''`, obtained from `↑(j+2) ≤ ↑a ≤ depth N'' + 1` plus
       `ENat.add_le_add_iff_right` cancellation plus
       `ENat.add_one_le_iff`.

### Helpers (private to the file)
- **Helper A** `ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth`: `(i : ℕ∞) < depth M
  → ∀ e : Ext κ (of R M) i, e = 0`. Body packages
  `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` for the LES chase via `n := i + 1` +
  `Nat.lt_succ_self`. Tier 2 modulo `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`.
- **Helper B** `natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one`: `1 ≤ a → ↑a ≤ d - 1
  → ↑(a+1) ≤ d` in `ℕ∞`. Case-split on `d = ⊤` (trivial via `simp [hd]`)
  vs `d = ↑n` (drop to `ℕ` via `WithTop.ne_top_iff_exists` + `omega`).
  **Tier 1 axiom-clean** — verified via inline `#print axioms` test:
  ```
  '_private._stdin.0.Test.natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one' depends on
   axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]
  ```
  (no `sorryAx`).

### Tooling traps recorded
- `simpa using h0` where `h0 := hinj hef` for the degree-0 mono case
  timed out at 200000 heartbeats (`whnf` cannot reduce
  `(postcomp ...) e = (postcomp ...) 0` automatically). **Workaround**:
  use `apply hinj` first and then `simpa using hef` — splits the
  injectivity application before the `simp` normalization.
- `one_ne_top` does not exist in scope; use
  `(by norm_num : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤)` instead.
- `Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero` returns `i = j.succ` (not `i = j + 1`),
  so `exact_mod_cast Nat.add_lt_add_right hi 1` fails the unification.
  **Workaround**: extract the predecessor and use `omega` for the
  arithmetic.
- `Nat.cast_le.mpr hle` for `(a + 1 : ℕ) ≤ n → ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤
  ((n : ℕ) : ℕ∞)` is the correct form; `exact_mod_cast hle` fails because
  the goal already has the cast applied (not synth-friendly).

### Axiom verification
- `'RingTheory.Module.depth_of_short_exact' depends on axioms:
  [propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — matches
  `'RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index' depends on axioms:
  [propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. The `sorryAx`
  propagates **only** through the single upstream
  `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` typed sorry (L228). Tier-2 disclosure.

## Other sorries in this file (unchanged this iter)

- `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` L228 — typed sorry; substantive Stacks
  00LP proof (induction via LES on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0`). Off-target.
- `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` L453 (line shifted from L326 by helpers)
  — typed sorry; multi-iter Stacks 090V content. Off-target.
- `exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal` L562 (line shifted from L435) —
  typed sorry; Mathlib gap `IsRegularLocalRing → IsDomain` per
  `analogies/isregularlocalring-isdomain.md`. Off-target (deferred
  iter-183+).

## Blueprint marker recommendation

- `lem:depth_short_exact_sequence` proof block (chapter
  `Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex` L241-258): **READY for `\leanok` on
  the proof block** (the statement block was already `\leanok` from
  iter-175). The sync_leanok phase should propagate automatically since
  the body now compiles modulo only typed upstream sorries — though the
  transitive `sorryAx` from `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` may keep
  `sync_leanok` from marking the proof block. Recommend review-agent
  judgment on whether Tier-2 modulo a same-chapter upstream qualifies
  for `\leanok` on the proof block; my read of the project rule is
  **yes** since the upstream is itself a `\leanok`-pinned statement (its
  proof block would not yet be `\leanok`).

## Dead ends (none triggered this iter)

The analogist recipe from `analogies/isregularlocalring-isdomain.md`
**Option 1 PIVOT recommendation** (away from
`exists_isRegular_of_regularLocal`, toward `depth_of_short_exact`) was
the right call. The LES-of-Ext route landed in a single iter with the
helper budget intact. The off-target option `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`
(also in Option 1) remains substantive — its body is the inductive
chase on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0` and was correctly NOT this iter's
target.

## Next-iter consumer hand-offs

- iter-183 `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` body lane can consume
  `depth_of_short_exact` directly as a building block of the SES
  splitting argument (Stacks 090V base case).
- The 3-helper pattern (Helper A Ext-vanish-from-strict-depth-bound +
  Helper B ENat-tsub-bridge + Helper C SES packaging-via-
  `shortComplex_shortExact`) is **reusable** for any future LES chase
  in the depth/Ext layer. Helper A and B are private to this file;
  consider promoting to public file-namespace lemmas if iter-183
  `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` or downstream `CohenMacaulay`-cor body
  reuses them. (No PROGRESS.md guidance to do this yet, so I left
  them `private`.)
