3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).

9	/-!
10	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
11	variety extends (A.4.c)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
15	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
20	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
21	
22	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
23	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
24	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.
31	
32	## Status (iter-175 Lane J file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-175 Lane J** file-skeleton: the single pinned
35	declaration carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the
36	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in
37	`chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50) with a `sorry`
38	body. The body is iter-176+ work, gated on the sibling chapters
39	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (A.4.a) and
40	`Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b) landing their substantive
41	statements.
42	
43	The 1 pinned declaration is:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
46	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an
47	   algebraically closed field `k̄`, `A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, and
48	   `f : X ⇢ A` a rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism
49	   `g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
50	
51	## Note on type expressivity
52	
53	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
54	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
55	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
56	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
57	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The
58	type is non-tautological: the existence is the content of Milne 3.2;
59	uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
60	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`).
61	
62	## Abelian-variety conventions
63	
64	Throughout the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
65	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
66	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
67	
68	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
69	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
70	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
71	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).
72	
73	A **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** (in the convention of
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1) is an
75	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
76	
77	- `[Smooth X.hom]`,
78	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
79	- `[IsSeparated X.hom]`,
80	- `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
81	- `[IsIntegral X.left]`,
82	- `[IsReduced X.left]`.
83	
84	These four-plus-two instances will normally be supplied by `inferInstance` at
85	the call site.
86	
87	## References
88

90	(250 LOC, 1 pin). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.2,
91	p. 17 (`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`, PDF page 23).

106	/-! ## Milne Theorem 3.2 — rational maps to an abelian variety extend
107	
108	Let `k̄` be an algebraically closed field. Let `X` be a nonsingular variety
109	over `k̄` (smooth, geometrically irreducible, separated, locally of finite
110	type, integral, reduced). Let `A` be an abelian variety over `k̄`
111	(a group object that is proper, smooth, and geometrically irreducible). For
112	every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` there is a *unique* regular morphism
113	`g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
114	
115	Milne's argument (`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1.4, Theorem 3.2):
116	let `U ⊆ X` be a maximal open of definition of `f` and `Z := X ∖ U`.
117	By the codim-$\geq 2$ extension theorem (Theorem 3.1, project-side
118	`thm:codim_one_extension`) the closed subset `Z` has codimension `≥ 2`.
119	By the pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy lemma for maps into a group variety
120	(Lemma 3.3, project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`), `Z` is either
121	empty or of pure codimension `1`. Both conditions force `Z = ∅`, so `U = X`
122	and `f` admits a regular representative on all of `X`. Uniqueness is the
123	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle (cf.
124	`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two regular morphisms `X ⟶ A` that
125	agree on a dense open of `X` agree everywhere.
126	
127	Blueprint reference: `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Milne, *Abelian
128	Varieties*, Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17). The pin in the chapter is at
129	`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50.
130	
131	iter-176+ proof skeleton:
132	1. Pick a `PartialMap` representative `φ : X.PartialMap A` of `f` (using
133	   `Scheme.RationalMap.toPartialMap`, which exists for `X` reduced and `A`
134	   separated; both hold from the abelian-variety + variety instances).
135	2. Show `φ.domain = ⊤` by combining `thm:codim_one_extension` (codim `≥ 2`
136	   complement) with `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (complement empty or
137	   pure codim `1`).
138	3. Read off `g := φ.hom` (the morphism on the full domain) and verify
139	   `g.toRationalMap = f`.
140	4. Uniqueness: two `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ A` with `g₁.toRationalMap = g₂.toRationalMap`
141	   agree on a dense open of `X`, so by
142	   `AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` and reducedness of `X` they agree
143	   everywhere. -/
144	
145	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (1/2): integrality of the abelian variety.**
146	
147	For an abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` —
148	encoded in the project as `A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying
149	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` —
150	the underlying scheme `A.left` is integral.
151	
152	**Derivation.** The proof has three steps:
153	
154	1. `Spec k̄` is a singleton scheme (Mathlib instance
155	   `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf` provides `Unique`,
156	   which yields both `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty`).
157	2. `GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom + Spec k̄ a single point ⟹ IrreducibleSpace A.left`
158	   via Mathlib's `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`.
159	3. `IrreducibleSpace + IsReduced ⟹ IsIntegral` via Mathlib's
160	   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`.
161	
162	The substantive Mathlib gap is the implication `Smooth A.hom ⟹ IsReduced A.left`
163	(at the project's pinned commit, Mathlib's `Smooth` morphism class does not
164	ship a `IsReduced` consequence for the source over a reduced base). The
165	gap is isolated to the single `IsReduced A.left` hypothesis in the proof,
166	which is left as a `sorry`; all other steps close axiom-clean.
167	
168	When Mathlib ships the bridge (Stacks `034V` / `02G4` over a field
169	direction), the single `sorry` discharges and this helper becomes
170	axiom-clean automatically.
171	
172	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor of the iter-179 helper
173	`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, splitting its `IsIntegral A.left`
174	conjunct into a self-contained named lemma that does not depend on the
175	rational map `f`. -/
176	private theorem av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred
177	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
178	    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
179	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
180	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] :
181	    IsIntegral A.left := by
182	  -- Step 1: `Spec k̄` has a unique point (Mathlib instance
183	  -- `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`); both
184	  -- `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty` follow.
185	  haveI : Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
186	  haveI : Nonempty (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
187	  -- Step 2: geometric irreducibility over a singleton base gives `IrreducibleSpace`.
188	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace A.left :=
189	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton A.hom
190	  -- Step 3 (Mathlib gap): smoothness over a field forces reducedness of the
191	  -- source. This is Stacks `034V`/`02G4` over a (reduced) base, but the
192	  -- scheme-level packaging does not ship in Mathlib at the project's pinned
193	  -- commit.
194	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := sorry
195	  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced A.left
196	
197	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (2/2): codim-1-indeterminacy-freeness from
198	Milne's two-input argument.**
199	
200	For a rational map `f : X ⇢ A` from a nonsingular variety `X` to an
201	abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
202	codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain` (`CodimOneFree f`).
203	
204	**Derivation (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, proof of Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17).**
205	Two project-side inputs combine to force the indeterminacy locus empty:
206	
207	1. `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 in
208	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): for a rational map from a smooth
209	   integral variety to a *group* variety, the indeterminacy locus is
210	   either empty or pure codim 1.
211	2. The *codim-≥2 conclusion* of Milne Theorem 3.1 (the unbundled half of
212	   `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
213	   for a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety,
214	   the indeterminacy locus has codim `≥ 2`.
215	
216	Combining (1) ∨ (2): pure codim 1 contradicts codim `≥ 2`, so the
217	indeterminacy locus is empty; trivially every point — and in particular
218	every codim-1 point — lies in `f.domain`, so `CodimOneFree f`.
219	
220	**Status of the substantive content.** The codim-≥2 conclusion of
221	Milne 3.1 is not currently exposed by the project as a standalone lemma;
222	it is encapsulated inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body (which
223	is itself `sorry` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`). Once the project
224	exposes a lemma `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` separating
225	out the codim-≥2 conclusion, this helper closes via the two-line
226	combination above. Until then it is left as a named `sorry` whose
227	docstring exhaustively records the combination strategy, replacing the
228	iter-179 conjunction-level `sorry` with a more targeted one.
229	
230	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor's split: the `CodimOneFree f`
231	conjunct is split off into a self-contained named lemma with the
232	substantive missing content localised here. -/
233	private theorem av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy
234	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
235	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
236	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
237	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
238	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
239	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
240	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
241	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
242	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
243	    CodimOneFree f := by
244	  -- Strategy (iter-181+ inline): apply `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
245	  -- to obtain the disjunction `Z(f) = ∅ ∨ ∀ x ∈ Z(f), coheight = 1`; in the
246	  -- empty branch, `CodimOneFree` is immediate; in the pure-codim-1 branch,
247	  -- combine with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 (currently bundled
248	  -- inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body — needs to be exposed
249	  -- as a separate lemma) to derive a contradiction at any putative
250	  -- codim-1 point of `Z(f)`. The remaining content is the codim-≥2
251	  -- exposure, which is the iter-181+ Mathlib-gap consult target.
252	  sorry
253	
254	/-- **Iter-179 Lane E + iter-180 Lane G: the two missing project inputs
255	for `extend_to_av`.**
256	
257	The conjunction of the two named iter-180 Lane G splits:
258	`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` (the `IsIntegral A.left` conjunct, with
259	its `IsReduced A.left` Mathlib gap isolated) and
260	`av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` (the `CodimOneFree f` conjunct, with its
261	codim-≥2-from-Milne-3.1 gap isolated).
262	
263	This wrapper preserves the iter-179 call-site API (`extend_to_av` invokes
264	the conjunction in one `obtain`) while routing the proof to the two
265	narrower lemmas. The body itself is axiom-clean — all substantive content
266	is localised in the two split helpers above. -/
267	private theorem av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree
268	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
269	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
270	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
271	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
272	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
273	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
274	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
275	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
276	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
277	    IsIntegral A.left ∧ CodimOneFree f :=
278	  ⟨av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A,
279	    av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy f⟩
280	
281	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
282	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
283	
284	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
285	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
286	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
287	
288	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
289	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
290	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
291	`J → A`.
292	
293	**Iter-179 Lane E body.** The proof is the standard one-line reduction of
294	Milne's two-line argument:
295	
296	1. Apply `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (Milne Theorem 3.1, project-side
297	   `thm:codim_one_extension` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): given a
298	   `CodimOneFree` rational map from a smooth integral variety to a complete
299	   variety, the map extends uniquely to a regular morphism. This requires
300	   the variety-package instances on `A.left` (separated, locally of finite
301	   type, integral, reduced) in addition to the four AV instances supplied
302	   by the caller.
303	
304	2. Discharge `CodimOneFree f` by invoking the helper
305	   `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, which combines
306	   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure
307	   codim 1 or empty indeterminacy for grpScheme targets) with the codim-≥2
308	   half of Milne 3.1. Both pieces are documented in that helper's
309	   docstring; the helper carries the single `sorry` that captures the
310	   remaining math content the project spec does not yet expose.
311	
312	3. Materialize the remaining variety-package instances on `A.left`:
313	   `IsSeparated A.hom` from `IsProper A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
314	   `LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom` from `Smooth A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
315	   `IsReduced A.left` from `IsIntegral A.left` (Mathlib instance
316	   `isReduced_of_isIntegral`).
317	
318	The proof body therefore introduces NO new inline `sorry`: all missing
319	content is concentrated in the single named helper above. -/
320	theorem extend_to_av
321	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
322	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
323	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
324	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
325	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
326	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
327	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
328	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
329	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
330	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
331	  -- Step 3: materialise the missing variety-package instances on A.left.
332	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
333	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
334	  obtain ⟨hint, hcod⟩ := av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree f
335	  haveI := hint
336	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
337	  -- Steps 1–2: apply Milne Theorem 3.1 with the `CodimOneFree` discharged.
338	  exact extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth f hcod
339	
340	end RationalMap
341	
342	end Scheme
343	
344	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	/-!
10	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
11	
12	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
13	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
14	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
15	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
16	
17	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
18	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
19	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
20	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
21	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
22	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
23	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
24	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
25	  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
26	  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
27	  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
28	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
29	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
30	  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
31	  outside this iter's scope.
32	
33	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
34	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
35	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
36	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
37	
38	## Status (iter-177 Lane 6 file-skeleton)
39	
40	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
41	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
42	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
43	Bodies are iter-178+ work, gated on the local-cohomology / depth-≥2
44	extension lemma (Step 2 of `thm:codim_one_extension`) being supplied either
45	from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (the `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`
46	input) or from a Mathlib upstream once the local-cohomology vanishing
47	`H¹_{x}(V, 𝒪_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point lands.
48	
49	The 6 pinned declarations are:
50	
51	1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
52	   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
53	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
54	   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
55	3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
56	   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
57	4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —
58	   Milne 3.1 specialised to smooth source + complete target +
59	   codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
60	5. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
61	   (theorem, ~8 LOC) — Milne Lemma 3.3.
62	6. `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`
63	   (theorem, ~2 LOC) — definitional re-shuffle of `RationalMap.mem_domain`
64	   exposing a `PartialMap` representative whose domain contains `W.point`.

68	## Note on type expressivity
69	
70	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
71	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
72	
73	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
74	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
75	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
76	  not `True`.
77	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
78	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
79	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
80	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
81	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
82	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
83	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
84	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
85	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
86	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
87	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
88	
89	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
90	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
91	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
92	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
93	
94	## Variety conventions
95	
96	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
97	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
98	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
99	
100	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
101	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
102	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
103	
104	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
105	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.
106

108

110	6 pins). Source: Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, §I.3, Theorem 3.1 (p. 16) and
111	Lemma 3.3 (p. 17); Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.6 pp. 130–131.
112	-/

126	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
127	
128	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
129	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
130	open subset of `X`).
131	
132	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
133	
134	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
135	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
136	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
137	
138	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
139	
140	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
141	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
142	
143	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
144	§I.3, p. 16). -/
145	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
146	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
147	
148	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
149	of an open). -/
150	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
151	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
152	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
153	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
154	
155	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
156	
157	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
158	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
159	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
160	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
161	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
162	`f.domain`.
163	
164	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
165	conclusion). -/
166	
167	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
168	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
169	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
170	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
171	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
172	
173	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
174	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
175	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
176	
177	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
178	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
179	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
180	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
181	
182	end RationalMap
183	
184	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
185	
186	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
187	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
188	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
189	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
190	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
191	
192	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
193	
194	/-- **Helper for `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`.** On a smooth integral variety
195	over an algebraically closed field, the stalk at a codimension-`1` point has a
196	*principal nonzero* maximal ideal. Encodes the substantive geometric content
197	of "regular in codimension one": smoothness ⟹ `IsRegularLocalRing` at the
198	stalk (Stacks `00PD`/`00TT`), and a Noetherian regular local ring with
199	`ringKrullDim = 1` has principal nonzero maximal ideal (Stacks `02IZ`),
200	via the cotangent-space `finrank = 1` characterisation.
201	
202	**Iter-179 Lane D body.** The Mathlib bridges
203	- `Algebra.Smooth k A → IsRegularLocalRing A` at every point (Stacks `00TT`
204	  Jacobian criterion side, scheme-level packaging), and
205	- `Order.coheight z = 1 → ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1`
206	  (the topological-coheight-to-ring-Krull-dim bridge that
207	  `Scheme.RationalMap.order`'s `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` instance is gated on,
208	  see `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean` L148-151)
209	
210	do not ship in Mathlib at commit `b80f227`. We package both as a single
211	internal `sorry` (the `hreg_dim` hypothesis below) and discharge the
212	remaining principal-nonzero conclusion via Mathlib's
213	`IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` +
214	`IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff` chain. The internal sorry
215	documents exactly the bridge needed: once the smooth-scheme regularity
216	and coheight-Krull-dim instances land in Mathlib (or a project upstream),
217	they discharge `hreg_dim` and the helper closes axiom-clean.
218	
219	The previous body was a bare `sorry`; this Lane-D refactor preserves the
220	warning count at 1 (one sorry per declaration) while exposing the proof
221	skeleton for the next prover. -/
222	private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
223	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
224	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
225	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
226	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
227	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
228	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
229	    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
230	      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
231	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk (needed for the cotangent-space API):
232	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the automatic `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
233	  -- through to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk picks up
234	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance).
235	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
236	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
237	  -- The Mathlib-gap content, packaged as a single hypothesis: smoothness +
238	  -- codim-1-witness ⟹ the stalk is a regular local ring of Krull dimension 1.
239	  -- Stacks `00TT` (Jacobian criterion for smoothness ⟹ regular local) and
240	  -- the topological-coheight-to-ring-Krull-dim bridge (Stacks `02IZ`/`005X`)
241	  -- together discharge this; neither half ships in Mathlib at b80f227.
242	  have hreg_dim : IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ∧
243	                    ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1 := sorry
244	  obtain ⟨hreg, hdim⟩ := hreg_dim
245	  -- From regularity + dim = 1, the cotangent space has `finrank = 1`
246	  -- (Mathlib `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
247	  have hfin :
248	      Module.finrank
249	        (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z))
250	        (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) = 1 := by
251	    have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace _).mp hreg
252	    rw [hdim] at h
253	    exact_mod_cast h
254	  -- Principal maximal ideal: `finrank ≤ 1 ↔ IsPrincipal maximalIdeal`
255	  -- (Mathlib `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff`).
256	  have hprin : Submodule.IsPrincipal
257	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
258	    IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff.mp hfin.le
259	  -- `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`: equivalent to "not a field" by Mathlib
260	  -- `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq`, and a field has Krull dim 0,
261	  -- contradicting `hdim : ringKrullDim = 1` via `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField`.
262	  have hne : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
263	    intro hbot
264	    have hF : IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
265	      IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr hbot
266	    have h0 : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 0 :=
267	      ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField hF
268	    -- Contradiction: `1 = 0` in `WithBot ℕ∞`.
269	    rw [hdim] at h0
270	    exact_mod_cast h0
271	  exact ⟨hprin, hne⟩
272	
273	/-- **Smooth + codim-1 ⇒ DVR.** For a nonsingular integral variety `X` over
274	an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with
275	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
276	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).
277	
278	The proof reduces to "regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` is a DVR"
279	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
280	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
281	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
282	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the
283	DVR instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.
284	
285	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
286	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/
287	theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
288	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
289	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
290	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
291	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
292	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
293	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
294	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
295	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
296	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
297	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
298	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
299	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
300	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
301	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
302	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
303	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
304	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
305	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
306	  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
307	  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
308	  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
309	  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
310	  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
311	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
312	    intro hF
313	    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq).mp hF)
314	  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
315	  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.
316	  exact ((tfae.out 0 4).mpr hprin)
317	
318	namespace RationalMap

320	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
321	
322	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
323	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
324	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
325	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
326	
327	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
328	
329	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
330	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
331	
332	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
333	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
334	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
335	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
336	
337	The proof combines two steps:
338	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
339	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
340	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
341	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
342	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
343	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
344	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
345	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
346	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
347	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
348	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
349	
350	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
351	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
352	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
353	
354	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
355	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
356	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
357	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
358	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
359	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
360	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
361	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
362	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
363	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
364	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
365	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
366	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
367	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f :=
368	  sorry
369	
370	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
371	
372	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
373	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
374	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
375	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
376	
377	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
378	
379	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
380	codim 1.**
381	
382	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
383	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
384	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
385	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
386	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
387	
388	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
389	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
390	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
391	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
392	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
393	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
394	
395	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
396	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
397	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
398	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
399	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
400	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
401	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
402	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
403	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
404	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
405	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
406	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
407	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
408	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
409	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
410	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) :=
411	  sorry
412	
413	/-! ## §6. Definedness at a prime-divisor generic point
414	
415	A truthful, lightweight reformulation of definedness at a prime-divisor
416	generic point: `f` is defined at `W.point` iff `f` admits a `PartialMap`
417	representative whose domain contains `W.point`. This is a definitional
418	re-shuffle of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`
419	swapping the order of the two conjuncts under the existential for downstream
420	ergonomics (so the `toRationalMap = f` conjunct lands first, the way later
421	witnesses are constructed in this project).
422	
423	### Status note (iter-179 Lane D, Path D2)
424	
425	The blueprint pin `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` (Hartshorne II.6
426	pp. 130–131) calls for the genuinely substantive statement
427	
428	> `f` is defined at `W.point` iff every regular function on every affine
429	> open `V ⊆ Y` around `f(W.point)` pulls back to an element of
430	> `O_{X,W.point}` ⊂ `K(X)` of `ord_W`-value `≥ 0`,
431	
432	which requires the pullback machinery
433	`Scheme.RationalMap → K(Y) → K(X)` and the project's existing
434	`Scheme.RationalMap.order : X.functionField → ℤ` valuation from
435	`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`. The Lean-level construction of the
436	`RationalMap`-to-function-field ring map is **not** shipped by Mathlib
437	at commit `b80f227`, and building it inside this file would constitute
438	a substantive new sub-build (cf. `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
439	on the pullback of a sheaf-of-algebras along a morphism).
440	
441	iter-178's `extend_iff_order_nonneg` claimed the substantive statement
442	in its docstring but only proved this reshuffle; the lemma's name and
443	documentation are corrected here (auditor 178B's Path D2 honest fallback)
444	to match the proven content. The `[Ring.KrullDimLE 1]` and
445	`[IsLocallyNoetherian X.left]` typeclass binders that the substantive
446	statement would have required are dropped along with the renaming, since
447	the reshuffle does not use them. The substantive statement is deferred to
448	a future lemma (working title: `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg`) once
449	the pullback machinery lands.
450	
451	Blueprint reference (in its current weakened form): a definitional unfold
452	of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`. -/
453	
454	/-- **Definedness at the generic point of a prime divisor, repackaged.**
455	
456	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `Y` a variety over `k̄`,
457	`f : X ⇢ Y` a rational map, and `W : X.left.PrimeDivisor`, the following
458	are equivalent:
459	
460	1. `W.point ∈ f.domain` (i.e.\ `f` is defined at the generic point of `W`).
461	2. There exists a `PartialMap` representative `g` of `f` (a regular morphism
462	   `g.hom : g.domain ⟶ Y.left` from a dense open `g.domain ⊆ X.left` with
463	   `g.toRationalMap = f`) whose domain contains `W.point`.
464	
465	This is the canonical existential characterisation of `f.domain` membership
466	via `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain`, with the two
467	conjuncts under the existential reordered so the `toRationalMap = f`
468	component lands first (matching the way later witnesses in this project
469	construct partial-map representatives).
470	
471	**Iter-179 Lane D scope.** This statement is the auditor-178B Path D2 honest
472	fallback: the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 "regular = no pole" Weil-divisor
473	reformulation (Stacks `02ME`), which would tighten the right-hand side to
474	an `ord_W ≥ 0` condition on every pulled-back regular function, is deferred
475	to a future lemma that builds the missing `RationalMap → function-field`
476	pullback machinery. See the section-level docstring above. -/
477	theorem mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point
478	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
479	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
480	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
481	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
482	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.RingHomProperties
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.FinitePresentation
10	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Flat
11	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField
12	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Noetherian
13	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.RingHom.LocallyStandardSmooth
14	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Flat
15	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Field
16	
17	/-!
18	
19	# Smooth morphisms
20	
21	In this file we define smooth morphisms. The main definitions are:
22	
23	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth`: A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth if for each affine `U ⊆ Y`
24	  and `V ⊆ f ⁻¹' U`, the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is smooth.
25	
26	- `AlgebraicGeometry.SmoothOfRelativeDimension`: A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth of
27	  relative dimension `n` if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open neighborhood `V` of `x`
28	  and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` such that the induced
29	  map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth (of relative dimension `n`).
30	
31	## Main results
32	
33	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth`: A morphism of schemes is smooth
34	  if and only if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open neighborhood `V` of `x`
35	  and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U` such that the induced
36	  map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth.
37	
38	## Notes
39	
40	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop "Formalizing algebraic geometry" in
41	June 2024.
42	
43	-/
44	
45	@[expose] public section
46	
47	noncomputable section
48	
49	open CategoryTheory Limits
50	
51	universe t w v u
52	
53	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
54	
55	open RingHom
56	
57	variable (n m : ℕ) {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y)
58	
59	/-- A morphism of schemes `f : X ⟶ Y` is smooth if for each affine `U ⊆ Y` and
60	`V ⊆ f ⁻¹' U`, The induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is smooth. -/
61	@[mk_iff]
62	class Smooth (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
63	  smooth_appLE (f) :
64	    ∀ {U : Y.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
65	      (f.appLE U V e).hom.Smooth
66	
67	alias Scheme.Hom.smooth_appLE := Smooth.smooth_appLE
68	
69	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-09")] alias IsSmooth := Smooth
70	
71	/-- The property of scheme morphisms `Smooth` is associated with the ring
72	homomorphism property `Smooth`. -/
73	instance : HasRingHomProperty @Smooth RingHom.Smooth where
74	  isLocal_ringHomProperty := RingHom.Smooth.propertyIsLocal
75	  eq_affineLocally' := by
76	    ext X Y f
77	    rw [smooth_iff, affineLocally_iff_forall_isAffineOpen]
78	
79	/--
80	A morphism of schemes is smooth if and only if for each `x : X` there exists an affine open
81	neighborhood `V` of `x` and an affine open neighborhood `U` of `f.base x` with `V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U`
82	such that the induced map `Γ(Y, U) ⟶ Γ(X, V)` is standard smooth.
83	-/
84	lemma Smooth.iff_forall_exists_isStandardSmooth (f : X ⟶ Y) :
85	    Smooth f ↔
86	      ∀ (x : X), ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (_ : x ∈ V)
87	        (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U), (f.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth := by
88	  have : HasRingHomProperty @Smooth.{u} (Locally IsStandardSmooth) := by
89	    convert (inferInstance : HasRingHomProperty @Smooth.{u} RingHom.Smooth)
90	    ext f
91	    rw [RingHom.smooth_iff_locally_isStandardSmooth]
92	  rw [HasRingHomProperty.iff_exists_appLE_locally (P := @Smooth)]
93	  · congr!
94	    simp [Subtype.exists]
95	    grind [Scheme.affineOpens]
96	  · exact isStandardSmooth_stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway.left
97	  · exact isStandardSmooth_respectsIso
98	
99	lemma Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth (f : X ⟶ Y) [Smooth f] (x : X) :
100	    ∃ (U : Y.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V) (_ : x ∈ V)

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Nilpotent.Defs
10	
11	/-!
12	# `IsReduced` is a local property
13	
14	In this file, we prove that `IsReduced` is a local property.
15	
16	## Main results
17	
18	Let `R` be a commutative ring, `M` be a submonoid of `R`.
19	
20	* `isReduced_localizationPreserves` :  `M⁻¹R` is reduced if `R` is reduced.
21	* `isReduced_ofLocalizationMaximal` : `R` is reduced if `Rₘ` is reduced for all maximal ideal `m`.
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	/-- `M⁻¹R` is reduced if `R` is reduced. -/
28	theorem isReduced_localizationPreserves : LocalizationPreserves fun R _ => IsReduced R := by
29	  introv R _ _
30	  constructor
31	  rintro x ⟨_ | n, e⟩
32	  · simpa using congr_arg (· * x) e
33	  obtain ⟨⟨y, m⟩, hx⟩ := IsLocalization.surj M x
34	  dsimp only at hx
35	  let hx' := congr_arg (· ^ n.succ) hx
36	  simp only [mul_pow, e, zero_mul, ← map_pow] at hx'
37	  rw [← (algebraMap R S).map_zero] at hx'
38	  obtain ⟨m', hm'⟩ := (IsLocalization.eq_iff_exists M S).mp hx'
39	  apply_fun (· * (m' : R) ^ n) at hm'
40	  simp only [mul_assoc, zero_mul, mul_zero] at hm'
41	  rw [← mul_left_comm, ← pow_succ', ← mul_pow] at hm'
42	  replace hm' := IsNilpotent.eq_zero ⟨_, hm'.symm⟩
43	  rw [← (IsLocalization.map_units S m).mul_left_inj, hx, zero_mul,
44	    IsLocalization.map_eq_zero_iff M]
45	  exact ⟨m', by rw [← hm', mul_comm]⟩
46	
47	instance {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (M : Submonoid R) [IsReduced R] : IsReduced (Localization M) :=
48	  isReduced_localizationPreserves M _ inferInstance
49	
50	/-- `R` is reduced if `Rₘ` is reduced for all maximal ideal `m`. -/
51	theorem isReduced_ofLocalizationMaximal : OfLocalizationMaximal fun R _ => IsReduced R := by
52	  introv R h
53	  constructor
54	  intro x hx
55	  apply eq_zero_of_localization
56	  intro J hJ
57	  specialize h J hJ
58	  exact (hx.map <| algebraMap R <| Localization.AtPrime J).eq_zero

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.FieldTheory.IsAlgClosed.AlgebraicClosure
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# Geometrically reduced algebras
13	
14	In this file we introduce geometrically reduced algebras.
15	For a field `k`, we say that a `k`-algebra `A` is geometrically reduced (`IsGeometricallyReduced`)
16	if the tensor product `AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] A` is reduced.
17	
18	## Main results
19	- `IsGeometricallyReduced.of_forall_fg`: for a field `k` and a commutative `k`-algebra `A`, if all
20	  finitely generated subalgebras `B` of `A` are geometrically reduced, then `A` is geometrically
21	  reduced.
22	
23	## References
24	- See [https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/05DS] for some theory of geometrically reduced algebras.
25	  Note that their definition differs from the one here, we still need a proof that these are
26	  equivalent (see TODO).
27	
28	## TODO
29	- Prove that if `A` is a geometrically reduced `k`-algebra, then for every field extension `K` of
30	  `k` the `K`-algebra `K ⊗[k] A` is reduced.
31	
32	-/
33	
34	@[expose] public section
35	
36	open TensorProduct
37	
38	noncomputable section
39	
40	namespace Algebra
41	
42	variable {k A : Type*} [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A]
43	
44	/-- The `k`-algebra `A` is geometrically reduced iff its base change to `AlgebraicClosure k` is
45	  reduced. -/
46	@[mk_iff]
47	class IsGeometricallyReduced (k A : Type*) [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A] : Prop where
48	  isReduced_algebraicClosure_tensorProduct : IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] A)
49	
50	attribute [instance] IsGeometricallyReduced.isReduced_algebraicClosure_tensorProduct
51	
52	instance (k A K : Type*) [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A] [Field K] [Algebra k K]
53	    [Algebra.IsAlgebraic k K] [IsGeometricallyReduced k A] : IsReduced (K ⊗[k] A) :=
54	  isReduced_of_injective
55	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.map ((IsAlgClosed.lift : K →ₐ[k] AlgebraicClosure k)) 1)
56	    (Module.Flat.rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ (RingHom.injective _))
57	
58	lemma IsGeometricallyReduced.of_injective {B : Type*} [Ring B] [Algebra k B] (f : A →ₐ[k] B)
59	    (hf : Function.Injective f) [IsGeometricallyReduced k B] : IsGeometricallyReduced k A :=
60	  ⟨isReduced_of_injective (Algebra.TensorProduct.map 1 f)
61	    (Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ hf)⟩
62	
63	variable (k) in
64	theorem isReduced_of_isGeometricallyReduced [IsGeometricallyReduced k A] : IsReduced A :=
65	  isReduced_of_injective
66	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : A →ₐ[k] (AlgebraicClosure k) ⊗[k] A)
67	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight_injective <| FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective _ _)
68	
69	/-- If all finitely generated subalgebras of `A` are geometrically reduced, then `A` is
70	  geometrically reduced. -/
71	@[stacks 030T]
72	theorem IsGeometricallyReduced.of_forall_fg
73	    (h : ∀ B : Subalgebra k A, B.FG → IsGeometricallyReduced k B) :
74	    IsGeometricallyReduced k A := by
75	  simp_rw [isGeometricallyReduced_iff] at h
76	  exact ⟨IsReduced.tensorProduct_of_flat_of_forall_fg h⟩
77	
78	end Algebra

11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalProperties.Reduced
