13	/-!
14	# Basic properties of schemes
15	
16	We provide some basic properties of schemes
17	
18	## Main definition
19	* `AlgebraicGeometry.IsIntegral`: A scheme is integral if it is nontrivial and all nontrivial
20	  components of the structure sheaf are integral domains.
21	* `AlgebraicGeometry.IsReduced`: A scheme is reduced if all the components of the structure sheaf
22	  are reduced.
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	-- Explicit universe annotations were used in this file to improve performance https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/issues/12737
28	
29	universe u
30	
31	open TopologicalSpace Opposite CategoryTheory CategoryTheory.Limits TopCat Topology
32	
33	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	variable (X : Scheme)
36	
37	instance : T0Space X :=
38	  T0Space.of_open_cover fun x => ⟨_, X.affineCover.covers x,
39	    (X.affineCover.f _).opensRange.2, IsEmbedding.t0Space (Y := PrimeSpectrum _)
40	    (isAffineOpen_opensRange (X.affineCover.f _)).isoSpec.schemeIsoToHomeo.isEmbedding⟩
41	
42	instance : QuasiSober X := by
43	  apply +allowSynthFailures
44	    quasiSober_of_open_cover (Set.range fun x => Set.range <| (X.affineCover.f x))
45	  · rintro ⟨_, i, rfl⟩; exact (X.affineCover.f i).isOpenEmbedding.isOpen_range
46	  · rintro ⟨_, i, rfl⟩
47	    exact @IsOpenEmbedding.quasiSober _ _ _ _ _
48	      (X.affineCover.f i).isOpenEmbedding.isEmbedding.toHomeomorph.symm.isOpenEmbedding
49	        PrimeSpectrum.quasiSober
50	  · rw [Set.top_eq_univ, Set.sUnion_range, Set.eq_univ_iff_forall]
51	    intro x; exact ⟨_, ⟨_, rfl⟩, X.affineCover.covers x⟩
52	
53	instance {X : Scheme.{u}} : PrespectralSpace X :=
54	  have (Y : Scheme.{u}) (_ : IsAffine Y) : PrespectralSpace Y :=
55	    .of_isClosedEmbedding (Y := PrimeSpectrum _) _
56	      Y.isoSpec.hom.homeomorph.isClosedEmbedding
57	  have (i : _) : PrespectralSpace (X.affineCover.f i).opensRange.1 :=
58	    this (X.affineCover.f i).opensRange (isAffineOpen_opensRange (X.affineCover.f i))
59	  .of_isOpenCover X.affineCover.isOpenCover_opensRange
60	
61	instance : ObjectProperty.IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms (C := Scheme) (IrreducibleSpace ·) :=
62	  ⟨fun e ↦ e.hom.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff.mp⟩
63	
64	instance : ObjectProperty.IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms (C := Scheme) (ConnectedSpace ·) :=
65	  ⟨fun e ↦ e.hom.homeomorph.connectedSpace_iff.mp⟩
66	
67	/-- A scheme `X` is reduced if all `𝒪ₓ(U)` are reduced. -/
68	class IsReduced : Prop where
69	  component_reduced : ∀ U, _root_.IsReduced Γ(X, U) := by infer_instance
70	
71	attribute [instance] IsReduced.component_reduced
72	
73	theorem isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk [∀ x : X, _root_.IsReduced (X.presheaf.stalk x)] :
74	    IsReduced X := by
75	  refine ⟨fun U => ⟨fun s hs => ?_⟩⟩
76	  apply Presheaf.section_ext X.sheaf U s 0
77	  intro x hx
78	  change (X.sheaf.presheaf.germ U x hx) s = (X.sheaf.presheaf.germ U x hx) 0
79	  rw [map_zero]
80	  change X.presheaf.germ U x hx s = 0
81	  exact (hs.map _).eq_zero
82	
83	instance isReduced_stalk_of_isReduced [IsReduced X] (x : X) :
84	    _root_.IsReduced (X.presheaf.stalk x) := by
85	  constructor
86	  rintro g ⟨n, e⟩
87	  obtain ⟨U, hxU, s, (rfl : (X.presheaf.germ U x hxU) s = g)⟩ := X.presheaf.germ_exist x g
88	  rw [← map_pow, ← map_zero (X.presheaf.germ _ x hxU).hom] at e
89	  obtain ⟨V, hxV, iU, iV, (e' : (X.presheaf.map iU.op) (s ^ n) = (X.presheaf.map iV.op) 0)⟩ :=
90	    X.presheaf.germ_eq x hxU hxU _ 0 e
91	  rw [map_pow, map_zero] at e'
92	  replace e' := (IsNilpotent.mk _ _ e').eq_zero (R := Γ(X, V))
93	  rw [← X.presheaf.germ_res iU x hxV, CommRingCat.comp_apply, e', map_zero]
94	
95	theorem isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
96	    [IsReduced Y] : IsReduced X := by
97	  constructor
98	  intro U
99	  rw [← f.preimage_image_eq U]
100	  exact isReduced_of_injective (inv <| f.app (f ''ᵁ U)).hom
101	    (asIso <| f.app (f ''ᵁ U) : Γ(Y, f ''ᵁ U) ≅ _).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.injective
102	
103	instance {X : Scheme} {U : X.Opens} [IsReduced X] : IsReduced U :=
104	    isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion U.ι
105	
106	instance {R : CommRingCat.{u}} [H : _root_.IsReduced R] : IsReduced (Spec R) := by
107	  apply +allowSynthFailures isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk
108	  intro x; dsimp
109	  have : _root_.IsReduced (CommRingCat.of <| Localization.AtPrime (PrimeSpectrum.asIdeal x)) := by
110	    dsimp; infer_instance
111	  exact isReduced_of_injective (Spec.stalkIso R x).hom.hom
112	    (Spec.stalkIso R x).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.injective
113	
114	theorem affine_isReduced_iff (R : CommRingCat) :
115	    IsReduced (Spec R) ↔ _root_.IsReduced R := by
116	  refine ⟨?_, fun h => inferInstance⟩
117	  intro h
118	  exact isReduced_of_injective (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv.hom
119	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.injective
120	
121	theorem isReduced_of_isAffine_isReduced [IsAffine X] [_root_.IsReduced Γ(X, ⊤)] :
122	    IsReduced X :=
123	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion X.isoSpec.hom
124	
125	theorem IsReduced.of_openCover (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [∀ i, IsReduced (𝒰.X i)] : IsReduced X := by
126	  have (x : X) : _root_.IsReduced (X.presheaf.stalk x) := by
127	    obtain ⟨i, x, rfl⟩ := 𝒰.exists_eq x
128	    exact isReduced_of_injective _
129	      (asIso <| (𝒰.f i).stalkMap x).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.injective
130	  exact isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk _
131	
132	/-- To show that a statement `P` holds for all open subsets of all schemes, it suffices to show that
133	1. In any scheme `X`, if `P` holds for an open cover of `U`, then `P` holds for `U`.
134	2. For an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`, if `P` holds for the entire space of `X`, then `P` holds for
135	  the image of `f`.
136	3. `P` holds for the entire space of an affine scheme.
137	-/
138	@[elab_as_elim]
139	theorem reduce_to_affine_global (P : ∀ {X : Scheme} (_ : X.Opens), Prop)
140	    {X : Scheme} (U : X.Opens)
141	    (h₁ : ∀ (X : Scheme) (U : X.Opens),
142	      (∀ x : U, ∃ (V : _) (_ : x.1 ∈ V) (_ : V ⟶ U), P V) → P U)
143	    (h₂ : ∀ (X Y) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f],
144	      ∃ (U : X.Opens) (V : Y.Opens), U = ⊤ ∧ V = f.opensRange ∧ (P U → P V))
145	    (h₃ : ∀ R : CommRingCat, P (X := Spec R) ⊤) : P U := by
146	  apply h₁
147	  intro x
148	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨j, rfl⟩, hx, i⟩ :=
149	    X.affineBasisCover_is_basis.exists_subset_of_mem_open (SetLike.mem_coe.2 x.prop) U.isOpen
150	  let U' : Opens _ := ⟨_, (X.affineBasisCover.f j).isOpenEmbedding.isOpen_range⟩
151	  let i' : U' ⟶ U := homOfLE i
152	  refine ⟨U', hx, i', ?_⟩
153	  obtain ⟨_, _, rfl, rfl, h₂'⟩ := h₂ _ _ (X.affineBasisCover.f j)
154	  apply h₂'
155	  apply h₃
156	
157	theorem reduce_to_affine_nbhd (P : ∀ (X : Scheme) (_ : X), Prop)
158	    (h₁ : ∀ R x, P (Spec R) x)
159	    (h₂ : ∀ {X Y} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (x : X), P X x → P Y (f x)) :
160	    ∀ (X : Scheme) (x : X), P X x := by
161	  intro X x
162	  obtain ⟨y, e⟩ := X.affineCover.covers x
163	  convert h₂ (X.affineCover.f (X.affineCover.idx x)) y _
164	  · rw [e]
165	  apply h₁
166	
167	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
168	theorem eq_zero_of_basicOpen_eq_bot {X : Scheme} [hX : IsReduced X] {U : X.Opens}
169	    (s : Γ(X, U)) (hs : X.basicOpen s = ⊥) : s = 0 := by
170	  apply TopCat.Presheaf.section_ext X.sheaf U
171	  intro x hx
172	  change (X.sheaf.presheaf.germ U x hx) s = (X.sheaf.presheaf.germ U x hx) 0
173	  rw [map_zero]
174	  induction U using reduce_to_affine_global generalizing hX with
175	  | h₁ X U H =>
176	    obtain ⟨V, hx, i, H⟩ := H ⟨x, hx⟩
177	    specialize H (X.presheaf.map i.op s)
178	    rw [Scheme.basicOpen_res, hs] at H
179	    specialize H (inf_bot_eq _) x hx
180	    rw [← X.sheaf.presheaf.germ_res_apply i x hx s]
181	    exact H
182	  | h₂ X Y f =>
183	    refine ⟨f ⁻¹ᵁ f.opensRange, f.opensRange, by simp, rfl, ?_⟩
184	    rintro H hX s hs _ ⟨x, rfl⟩
185	    haveI := isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion f
186	    specialize H (f.app _ s) _ x ⟨x, rfl⟩
187	    · rw [← Scheme.preimage_basicOpen, hs]; ext1; simp [Opens.map]
188	    · have H : (X.presheaf.germ _ x _).hom _ = 0 := H
189	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.germ_stalkMap_apply f ⟨_, _⟩ x] at H
190	      apply_fun inv <| f.stalkMap x at H
191	      rw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, CategoryTheory.IsIso.hom_inv_id, map_zero] at H
192	      exact H
193	  | h₃ R =>
194	    rw [basicOpen_eq_of_affine', PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_eq_bot_iff] at hs
195	    replace hs := (hs.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv.hom).eq_zero
196	    rw [← CommRingCat.comp_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id, CommRingCat.id_apply] at hs
197	    rw [hs, map_zero]
198	
199	@[simp]
200	theorem basicOpen_eq_bot_iff {X : Scheme} [IsReduced X] {U : X.Opens}

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.FiniteStability
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Quotient.Nilpotent
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Localization.Away.AdjoinRoot
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Kaehler

13	
14	/-!
15	
16	# Smooth morphisms
17	
18	An `R`-algebra `A` is formally smooth if `Ω[A⁄R]` is `A`-projective and `H¹(L_{A/R}) = 0`.
19	This is equivalent to the standard definition that "for every `R`-algebra `B`,
20	every square-zero ideal `I : Ideal B` and `f : A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I`, there exists
21	at least one lift `A →ₐ[R] B`".
22	An `R`-algebra `A` is smooth if it is formally smooth and of finite presentation.
23	
24	We show that the property of being formally smooth extends onto nilpotent ideals,
25	and that it is stable under `R`-algebra homomorphisms and compositions.
26	
27	We show that smooth is stable under algebra isomorphisms, composition and
28	localization at an element.
29	
30	## Main results
31	- `Algebra.FormallySmooth`: The class of formally smooth algebras.
32	- `Algebra.formallySmooth_iff` :
33	  Formally smooth iff `Ω[A⁄R]` is `A`-projective and `H¹(L_{A/R}) = 0`.
34	- `Algebra.FormallySmooth.lift`: If `A` is formally smooth and `I` is nilpotent,
35	  any map `A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I` lifts to `A →ₐ[R] B`.
36	- `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_comp_surjective`: `A` is formally smooth iff
37	  any map `A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I` lifts to `A →ₐ[R] B` for any square zero `I`.
38	
39	Suppose `P` is a formally smooth `R` algebra that surjects onto `A` with kernel `I`, then
40	- `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_surjection`: `A` is formally smooth iff
41	  the algebra map `P ⧸ I² →ₐ[R] A` has an `R`-algebra section.
42	- `Algebra.Extension.equivH1CotangentOfFormallySmooth`:
43	  `H¹(L_{A/R})` is isomorphic to `ker(I/I² → A ⊗[P] Ω[P⁄R])`.
44	- `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection`: `A` is formally smooth iff
45	  the `P`-linear map `I/I² → A ⊗[P] Ω[P⁄R]` is split injective.
46	
47	-/
48	
49	@[expose] public section
50	
51	open scoped TensorProduct
52	open Algebra.Extension KaehlerDifferential MvPolynomial
53	
54	universe u v w
55	
56	variable {R : Type u} {A : Type v} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
57	variable {B P C : Type*} [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] [CommRing C] [Algebra R C]
58	  [CommRing P] [Algebra R P]
59	namespace Algebra
60	
61	section
62	
63	variable (R A) in
64	/--
65	An `R`-algebra `A` is formally smooth if `Ω[A⁄R]` is `A`-projective and `H¹(L_{A/R}) = 0`.
66	For the infinitesimal lifting definition,
67	see `FormallySmooth.lift` and `FormallySmooth.iff_comp_surjective`.
68	-/
69	@[stacks 00TI "Also see 031J (6) for the equivalence with the definition given here.", mk_iff]
70	class FormallySmooth : Prop where
71	  projective_kaehlerDifferential : Module.Projective A Ω[A⁄R]
72	  subsingleton_h1Cotangent : Subsingleton (H1Cotangent R A)
73	
74	attribute [instance] FormallySmooth.projective_kaehlerDifferential
75	  FormallySmooth.subsingleton_h1Cotangent
76	
77	@[deprecated (since := "2025-10-25")]
78	alias FormallySmooth.iff_subsingleton_and_projective := Algebra.formallySmooth_iff
79	
80	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
81	variable (R A) in
82	lemma FormallySmooth.comp_surjective [FormallySmooth R A] (I : Ideal B) (hI : I ^ 2 = ⊥) :
83	    Function.Surjective ((Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ R I).comp : (A →ₐ[R] B) → A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I) := by
84	  intro f
85	  let P : Algebra.Generators R A A := Generators.self R A
86	  have hP : Function.Injective P.toExtension.cotangentComplex := by
87	    rw [← LinearMap.ker_eq_bot, ← Submodule.subsingleton_iff_eq_bot]
88	    exact FormallySmooth.subsingleton_h1Cotangent
89	  obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ := ((P.toExtension.exact_cotangentComplex_toKaehler.split_tfae'.out 0 1 rfl rfl).mp
90	    ⟨P.toExtension.subsingleton_h1Cotangent.mp FormallySmooth.subsingleton_h1Cotangent,
91	      Module.projective_lifting_property _ _ P.toExtension.toKaehler_surjective⟩).2
92	  obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := retractionKerCotangentToTensorEquivSection (R := R) P.algebraMap_surjective
93	    ⟨⟨⟨Cotangent.val, by simp⟩, by simpa using Cotangent.val_smul' (P := P.toExtension)⟩ ∘ₗ
94	      l.restrictScalars P.toExtension.Ring, LinearMap.ext fun x ↦ congr($hl x)⟩
95	  let σ := Function.surjInv (f := algebraMap B (B ⧸ I)) Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
96	  have H (x : P.Ring) : ↑(aeval (σ ∘ f) x) = f (algebraMap _ A x) := by
97	    rw [← Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq, ← aeval_algebraMap_apply, P.algebraMap_eq,
98	      AlgHom.coe_toRingHom, comp_aeval_apply, ← Function.comp_assoc, Function.comp_surjInv,]
99	    simp [P]
100	  let l : P.Ring ⧸ (RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring A)) ^ 2 →ₐ[R] B :=
101	    Ideal.Quotient.liftₐ _ (aeval (σ ∘ f)) <|
102	      have : RingHom.ker (algebraMap P.Ring A) ≤ I.comap (aeval (σ ∘ f)).toRingHom := fun x hx ↦ by
103	        simp_all [← Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem (I := I), -map_aeval]
104	      show RingHom.ker _ ^ 2 ≤ RingHom.ker _ from
105	        (Ideal.pow_right_mono this 2).trans ((Ideal.le_comap_pow _ _).trans_eq (hI ▸ rfl))
106	  have : f.comp (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R P.Ring A).kerSquareLift =
107	      (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ R _).comp l := by
108	    refine Ideal.Quotient.algHom_ext _ (MvPolynomial.algHom_ext fun i ↦ ?_)
109	    change f (algebraMap P.Ring A (.X i)) = algebraMap _ _ (MvPolynomial.aeval (σ ∘ f) (.X i))
110	    simpa using (Function.surjInv_eq _ _).symm
111	  exact ⟨l.comp g, by rw [← AlgHom.comp_assoc, ← this, AlgHom.comp_assoc, hg, AlgHom.comp_id]⟩
112	
113	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
114	instance mvPolynomial (σ : Type*) : FormallySmooth R (MvPolynomial σ R) := by
115	  let P : Generators R (MvPolynomial σ R) σ :=
116	    .ofSurjective X (by simp [aeval_X_left, Function.Surjective])
117	  have : Subsingleton ↥P.toExtension.ker :=
118	    Submodule.subsingleton_iff_eq_bot.mpr <| by
119	      simp [SetLike.ext_iff, P, Generators.ofSurjective, aeval_X_left]
120	  have : Subsingleton P.toExtension.Cotangent := Cotangent.mk_surjective.subsingleton
121	  have := P.toExtension.h1Cotangentι_injective.subsingleton
122	  exact ⟨inferInstance, P.equivH1Cotangent.symm.subsingleton⟩
123	
124	end
125	
126	namespace FormallySmooth
127	
128	theorem exists_lift
129	    [FormallySmooth R A] (I : Ideal B) (hI : IsNilpotent I) (g : A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I) :
130	    ∃ f : A →ₐ[R] B, (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ R I).comp f = g := by
131	  revert g
132	  change Function.Surjective (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ R I).comp
133	  revert ‹Algebra R B›
134	  apply Ideal.IsNilpotent.induction_on (S := B) I hI
135	  · intro B _ I hI _; exact FormallySmooth.comp_surjective R A I hI
136	  · intro B _ I J hIJ h₁ h₂ _ g
137	    let this : ((B ⧸ I) ⧸ J.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk I)) ≃ₐ[R] B ⧸ J :=
138	      { (DoubleQuot.quotQuotEquivQuotSup I J).trans
139	          (Ideal.quotEquivOfEq (sup_eq_right.mpr hIJ)) with
140	        commutes' := fun x => rfl }
141	    obtain ⟨g', e⟩ := h₂ (this.symm.toAlgHom.comp g)
142	    obtain ⟨g', rfl⟩ := h₁ g'
143	    replace e := congr_arg this.toAlgHom.comp e
144	    conv_rhs at e =>
145	      rw [← AlgHom.comp_assoc, AlgEquiv.toAlgHom_eq_coe, AlgEquiv.toAlgHom_eq_coe,
146	        AlgEquiv.comp_symm, AlgHom.id_comp]
147	    exact ⟨g', e⟩
148	
149	/-- For a formally smooth `R`-algebra `A` and a map `f : A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I` with `I` square-zero,
150	this is an arbitrary lift `A →ₐ[R] B`. -/
151	noncomputable def lift [FormallySmooth R A] (I : Ideal B) (hI : IsNilpotent I)
152	    (g : A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I) : A →ₐ[R] B :=
153	  (FormallySmooth.exists_lift I hI g).choose
154	
155	@[simp]
156	theorem comp_lift [FormallySmooth R A] (I : Ideal B) (hI : IsNilpotent I)
157	    (g : A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I) : (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ R I).comp (FormallySmooth.lift I hI g) = g :=
158	  (FormallySmooth.exists_lift I hI g).choose_spec
159	
160	@[simp]
161	theorem mk_lift [FormallySmooth R A] (I : Ideal B) (hI : IsNilpotent I)
162	    (g : A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I) (x : A) : Ideal.Quotient.mk I (FormallySmooth.lift I hI g x) = g x :=
163	  AlgHom.congr_fun (FormallySmooth.comp_lift I hI g :) x
164	
165	variable {C : Type*} [CommRing C] [Algebra R C]
166	
167	/-- For a formally smooth `R`-algebra `A` and a map `f : A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I` with `I` nilpotent,
168	this is an arbitrary lift `A →ₐ[R] B`. -/
169	noncomputable def liftOfSurjective [FormallySmooth R A] (f : A →ₐ[R] C)
170	    (g : B →ₐ[R] C) (hg : Function.Surjective g) (hg' : IsNilpotent <| RingHom.ker (g : B →+* C)) :
171	    A →ₐ[R] B :=
172	  FormallySmooth.lift _ hg' ((Ideal.quotientKerAlgEquivOfSurjective hg).symm.toAlgHom.comp f)
173	
174	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
175	@[simp]
176	theorem liftOfSurjective_apply [FormallySmooth R A] (f : A →ₐ[R] C) (g : B →ₐ[R] C)
177	    (hg : Function.Surjective g) (hg' : IsNilpotent <| RingHom.ker g) (x : A) :
178	    g (FormallySmooth.liftOfSurjective f g hg hg' x) = f x := by
179	  apply (Ideal.quotientKerAlgEquivOfSurjective hg).symm.injective
180	  conv_rhs => rw [← AlgHom.coe_coe, ← AlgHom.comp_apply, ← FormallySmooth.mk_lift (A := A) _ hg']
181	  apply (Ideal.quotientKerAlgEquivOfSurjective hg).injective
182	  rw [AlgEquiv.apply_symm_apply, Ideal.quotientKerAlgEquivOfSurjective_apply]
183	  simp only [liftOfSurjective, ← RingHom.ker_coe_toRingHom g, RingHom.kerLift_mk,
184	    AlgEquiv.toAlgHom_eq_coe, RingHom.coe_coe]
185	
186	@[simp]
187	theorem comp_liftOfSurjective [FormallySmooth R A] (f : A →ₐ[R] C) (g : B →ₐ[R] C)
188	    (hg : Function.Surjective g) (hg' : IsNilpotent <| RingHom.ker (g : B →+* C)) :
189	    g.comp (FormallySmooth.liftOfSurjective f g hg hg') = f :=
190	  AlgHom.ext (FormallySmooth.liftOfSurjective_apply f g hg hg')
191	
192	instance [EssFiniteType R A] [FormallySmooth R A] : Module.FinitePresentation A Ω[A⁄R] :=
193	  Module.finitePresentation_of_projective A Ω[A⁄R]
194	
195	end FormallySmooth
196	
197	namespace Extension
198	
199	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
200	/--
201	Given extensions `0 → I₁ → P₁ → A → 0` and `0 → I₂ → P₂ → A → 0` with `P₁` formally smooth,
202	this is an arbitrarily chosen map `P₁/I₁² → P₂/I₂²` of extensions.
203	-/
204	noncomputable
205	def homInfinitesimal (P₁ P₂ : Extension R A) [FormallySmooth R P₁.Ring] :
206	    P₁.infinitesimal.Hom P₂.infinitesimal :=
207	  letI lift : P₁.Ring →ₐ[R] P₂.infinitesimal.Ring := FormallySmooth.liftOfSurjective
208	    (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R P₁.Ring A)
209	    (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R P₂.infinitesimal.Ring A)
210	    P₂.infinitesimal.algebraMap_surjective
211	    ⟨2, show P₂.infinitesimal.ker ^ 2 = ⊥ by
212	      rw [ker_infinitesimal]; exact Ideal.cotangentIdeal_square _⟩
213	  { toRingHom := (Ideal.Quotient.liftₐ (P₁.ker ^ 2) lift (by
214	        change P₁.ker ^ 2 ≤ RingHom.ker lift
215	        rw [pow_two, Ideal.mul_le]
216	        have : ∀ r ∈ P₁.ker, lift r ∈ P₂.infinitesimal.ker :=
217	          fun r hr ↦ (FormallySmooth.liftOfSurjective_apply _
218	            (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R P₂.infinitesimal.Ring A) _ _ r).trans hr
219	        intro r hr s hs
220	        rw [RingHom.mem_ker, map_mul, ← Ideal.mem_bot, ← P₂.ker.cotangentIdeal_square,
221	          ← ker_infinitesimal, pow_two]
222	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_mul (this r hr) (this s hs))).toRingHom
223	    toRingHom_algebraMap := by simp
224	    algebraMap_toRingHom x := by
225	      obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective x
226	      exact FormallySmooth.liftOfSurjective_apply _
227	            (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R P₂.infinitesimal.Ring A) _ _ x }
228	
229	/-- Formally smooth extensions have isomorphic `H¹(L_P)`. -/
230	noncomputable
231	def H1Cotangent.equivOfFormallySmooth (P₁ P₂ : Extension R A)
232	    [FormallySmooth R P₁.Ring] [FormallySmooth R P₂.Ring] :
233	    P₁.H1Cotangent ≃ₗ[A] P₂.H1Cotangent :=
234	  .ofBijective _ (H1Cotangent.map_toInfinitesimal_bijective P₁) ≪≫ₗ
235	    H1Cotangent.equiv (Extension.homInfinitesimal _ _) (Extension.homInfinitesimal _ _)
236	    ≪≫ₗ .symm (.ofBijective _ (H1Cotangent.map_toInfinitesimal_bijective P₂))
237	
238	lemma H1Cotangent.equivOfFormallySmooth_toLinearMap {P₁ P₂ : Extension R A} (f : P₁.Hom P₂)
239	    [FormallySmooth R P₁.Ring] [FormallySmooth R P₂.Ring] :
240	    (H1Cotangent.equivOfFormallySmooth P₁ P₂).toLinearMap = map f := by
241	  ext1 x
242	  refine (LinearEquiv.symm_apply_eq _).mpr ?_
243	  change ((map (P₁.homInfinitesimal P₂)).restrictScalars A ∘ₗ map P₁.toInfinitesimal) x =
244	    ((map P₂.toInfinitesimal).restrictScalars A ∘ₗ map f) x
245	  rw [← map_comp, ← map_comp, map_eq]
246	
247	lemma H1Cotangent.equivOfFormallySmooth_apply {P₁ P₂ : Extension R A} (f : P₁.Hom P₂)
248	    [FormallySmooth R P₁.Ring] [FormallySmooth R P₂.Ring] (x) :
249	    H1Cotangent.equivOfFormallySmooth P₁ P₂ x = map f x := by
250	  rw [← equivOfFormallySmooth_toLinearMap, LinearEquiv.coe_coe]
251	
252	lemma H1Cotangent.equivOfFormallySmooth_symm (P₁ P₂ : Extension R A)
253	    [FormallySmooth R P₁.Ring] [FormallySmooth R P₂.Ring] :
254	    (equivOfFormallySmooth P₁ P₂).symm = equivOfFormallySmooth P₂ P₁ := rfl
255	
256	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
257	/-- Any formally smooth extension can be used to calculate `H¹(L_{A/R})`. -/
258	noncomputable
259	def equivH1CotangentOfFormallySmooth (P : Extension R A) [FormallySmooth R P.Ring] :
260	    P.H1Cotangent ≃ₗ[A] H1Cotangent R A :=
261	  haveI : FormallySmooth R (Generators.self R A).toExtension.Ring :=
262	    inferInstanceAs (FormallySmooth R (MvPolynomial _ _))
263	  H1Cotangent.equivOfFormallySmooth _ _
264	
265	lemma cotangentComplex_injective_iff
266	    (P : Extension R A) [FormallySmooth R P.Ring] :
267	    Function.Injective P.cotangentComplex ↔ Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent R A) := by
268	  rw [← Algebra.Extension.subsingleton_h1Cotangent,
269	    P.equivH1CotangentOfFormallySmooth.subsingleton_congr]
270	
271	end Algebra.Extension

273	namespace Algebra.FormallySmooth
274	
275	section iff_split
276	
277	variable [Algebra.FormallySmooth R P]
278	
279	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
280	lemma kerCotangentToTensor_injective_iff
281	    [Algebra P A] [IsScalarTower R P A] (hf : Function.Surjective (algebraMap P A)) :
282	    Function.Injective (kerCotangentToTensor R P A) ↔ Subsingleton (Algebra.H1Cotangent R A) :=
283	  let P' : Algebra.Extension R A := ⟨P, _, Function.surjInv_eq hf⟩
284	  have : Algebra.FormallySmooth R P'.Ring := ‹_›
285	  P'.cotangentComplex_injective_iff
286	
287	/--
288	Given a formally smooth `R`-algebra `P` and a surjective algebra homomorphism `f : P →ₐ[R] A`
289	with kernel `I` (typically a presentation `R[X] → A`),
290	`A` is formally smooth iff the `P`-linear map `I/I² → A ⊗[P] Ω[P⁄R]` is split injective.
291	Also see `Algebra.Extension.formallySmooth_iff_split_injection`
292	for the version in terms of `Extension`.
293	-/
294	@[stacks 031I]
295	theorem iff_split_injection
296	    [Algebra P A] [IsScalarTower R P A] (hf : Function.Surjective (algebraMap P A)) :
297	    Algebra.FormallySmooth R A ↔ ∃ l, l ∘ₗ (kerCotangentToTensor R P A) = LinearMap.id := by
298	  rw [formallySmooth_iff, and_comm,
299	    Module.Projective.iff_split_of_projective (KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange R P A)
300	      (mapBaseChange_surjective R P A hf), ← kerCotangentToTensor_injective_iff hf]
301	  convert (((exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange R _ _ hf).split_tfae'
302	    (g := (KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange R P A).restrictScalars P)).out 0 1) using 2
303	  · rw [← (LinearMap.extendScalarsOfSurjectiveEquiv hf).exists_congr_right]
304	    simp [LinearMap.ext_iff]
305	  · rw [and_iff_right (by exact mapBaseChange_surjective R P A hf)]
306	
307	/--
308	Given a formally smooth `R`-algebra `P` and a surjective algebra homomorphism `f : P →ₐ[R] S`
309	with kernel `I` (typically a presentation `R[X] → S`),
310	`S` is formally smooth iff the `P`-linear map `I/I² → S ⊗[P] Ω[P⁄R]` is split injective.
311	-/
312	@[stacks 031I]
313	theorem _root_.Algebra.Extension.formallySmooth_iff_split_injection
314	    (P : Algebra.Extension.{w} R A) [FormallySmooth R P.Ring] :
315	    Algebra.FormallySmooth R A ↔ ∃ l, l ∘ₗ P.cotangentComplex = LinearMap.id := by
316	  refine (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection P.algebraMap_surjective).trans ?_
317	  let e : P.ker.Cotangent ≃ₗ[P.Ring] P.Cotangent :=
318	    { __ := AddEquiv.refl _, map_smul' r m := by ext1; simp; rfl }
319	  constructor
320	  · intro ⟨l, hl⟩
321	    exact ⟨(e.comp l).extendScalarsOfSurjective P.algebraMap_surjective,
322	      LinearMap.ext (DFunLike.congr_fun hl : _)⟩
323	  · intro ⟨l, hl⟩
324	    exact ⟨e.symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ l.restrictScalars P.Ring,
325	      LinearMap.ext (DFunLike.congr_fun hl : _)⟩
326	
327	/-- Let `P →ₐ[R] A` be a surjection with kernel `J`, and `P` a formally smooth `R`-algebra,
328	then `A` is formally smooth over `R` iff the surjection `P ⧸ J ^ 2 →ₐ[R] A` has a section.
329	
330	Geometric intuition: we require that a first-order thickening of `Spec A` inside `Spec P` admits
331	a retraction. -/
332	theorem iff_split_surjection (f : P →ₐ[R] A) (hf : Function.Surjective f) :
333	    FormallySmooth R A ↔ ∃ g, f.kerSquareLift.comp g = AlgHom.id R A := by
334	  letI := f.toAlgebra
335	  rw [iff_split_injection hf, ← nonempty_subtype, ← nonempty_subtype,
336	    (retractionKerCotangentToTensorEquivSection hf).nonempty_congr]
337	  rfl
338	
339	theorem of_split (f : P →ₐ[R] A) (g : A →ₐ[R] P ⧸ RingHom.ker f.toRingHom ^ 2)
340	    (h : f.kerSquareLift.comp g = AlgHom.id R A) :
341	    FormallySmooth R A := by
342	  refine (iff_split_surjection f fun x ↦ ?_).mpr ⟨g, h⟩
343	  obtain ⟨y, hy⟩ := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective (g x)
344	  exact ⟨y, congr(f.kerSquareLift $hy).trans congr($h x)⟩
345	
346	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
347	theorem of_comp_surjective
348	    (H : ∀ ⦃B : Type max u v⦄ [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] (I : Ideal B) (_ : I ^ 2 = ⊥),
349	        Function.Surjective ((Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ R I).comp : (A →ₐ[R] B) → A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I)) :
350	    FormallySmooth R A := by
351	  let P := Generators.self R A
352	  let f := IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R P.Ring A
353	  rw [iff_split_surjection f P.algebraMap_surjective]
354	  have surj : Function.Surjective f.kerSquareLift :=
355	    Ideal.Quotient.lift_surjective_of_surjective _ _ P.algebraMap_surjective
356	  have sqz : RingHom.ker f.kerSquareLift.toRingHom ^ 2 = ⊥ := by
357	    rw [AlgHom.ker_kerSquareLift, Ideal.cotangentIdeal_square]
358	  dsimp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.ker_coe_toRingHom] at sqz
359	  obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := H _ sqz (Ideal.quotientKerAlgEquivOfSurjective surj).symm.toAlgHom
360	  refine ⟨g, AlgHom.ext fun x ↦ congr(f.kerSquareLift.kerLift ($hg x)).trans ?_⟩
361	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := (Ideal.quotientKerAlgEquivOfSurjective surj).surjective x
362	  obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective x
363	  simp only [AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, AlgEquiv.toAlgHom_eq_coe, AlgHom.coe_coe,
364	    AlgEquiv.symm_apply_apply, AlgHom.coe_id, id_eq]
365	  simp only [Ideal.quotientKerAlgEquivOfSurjective_apply]
366	
367	/--
368	An `R`-algebra `A` is formally smooth iff "for every `R`-algebra `B`,
369	every square-zero ideal `I : Ideal B` and `f : A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I`, there exists
370	at least one lift `A →ₐ[R] B`".
371	-/
372	theorem iff_comp_surjective :
373	   FormallySmooth R A ↔ ∀ ⦃B : Type max u v⦄ [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] (I : Ideal B), I ^ 2 = ⊥ →
374	      Function.Surjective ((Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ R I).comp : (A →ₐ[R] B) → A →ₐ[R] B ⧸ I) :=
375	  ⟨fun _ _ ↦ comp_surjective R A, of_comp_surjective⟩
376	
377	end iff_split
378	
379	section OfEquiv
380	
381	variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
382	variable {A B : Type*} [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [CommRing B] [Algebra R B]
383	
384	theorem of_equiv [FormallySmooth R A] (e : A ≃ₐ[R] B) : FormallySmooth R B :=
385	  (iff_split_surjection e.toAlgHom e.surjective).mpr
386	    ⟨(Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ _ _).comp e.symm, AlgHom.ext e.apply_symm_apply⟩
387	
388	theorem iff_of_equiv (e : A ≃ₐ[R] B) : FormallySmooth R A ↔ FormallySmooth R B :=
389	  ⟨fun _ ↦ of_equiv e, fun _ ↦ of_equiv e.symm⟩
390	
391	end OfEquiv

393	section Polynomial
394	
395	open scoped Polynomial in
396	instance polynomial (R : Type*) [CommRing R] :
397	  FormallySmooth R R[X] := .of_equiv (MvPolynomial.pUnitAlgEquiv.{_, 0} R)
398	
399	instance : FormallySmooth R R := .of_equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv R Empty)
400	
401	end Polynomial

403	section Comp
404	
405	variable (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
406	variable (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
407	variable (B : Type*) [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] [Algebra A B] [IsScalarTower R A B]
408	
409	theorem comp [FormallySmooth R A] [FormallySmooth A B] : FormallySmooth R B := by
410	  refine .of_comp_surjective fun C _ _ I hI f ↦ ?_
411	  obtain ⟨f', e⟩ := FormallySmooth.comp_surjective _ _ I hI (f.comp (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R A B))
412	  letI := f'.toRingHom.toAlgebra
413	  obtain ⟨f'', e'⟩ := comp_surjective _ _ I hI { f with commutes' := AlgHom.congr_fun e.symm }
414	  apply_fun AlgHom.restrictScalars R at e'
415	  exact ⟨f''.restrictScalars _, e'.trans (AlgHom.ext fun _ => rfl)⟩
416	
417	lemma of_restrictScalars [FormallyUnramified R A] [FormallySmooth R B] :
418	    FormallySmooth A B := by
419	  refine iff_comp_surjective.mpr fun C _ _ I hI f ↦ ?_
420	  algebraize [(algebraMap A C).comp (algebraMap R A)]
421	  obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := Algebra.FormallySmooth.comp_surjective _ _ I hI (f.restrictScalars R)
422	  suffices g.comp (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R A B) = IsScalarTower.toAlgHom R A C from
423	    ⟨{ __ := g, commutes' x := congr($this x) }, AlgHom.ext fun x ↦ congr($hg x)⟩
424	  apply Algebra.FormallyUnramified.comp_injective _ hI
425	  rw [← AlgHom.comp_assoc, hg]
426	  exact AlgHom.ext f.commutes
427	
428	end Comp

430	section surjective
431	
432	variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
433	variable {P A : Type*} [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [CommRing P] [Algebra R P]
434	variable (f : P →ₐ[R] A)
435	
436	lemma iff_of_surjective (h : Function.Surjective (algebraMap R A)) :
437	    Algebra.FormallySmooth R A ↔ IsIdempotentElem (RingHom.ker (algebraMap R A)) := by
438	  rw [Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_surjection (Algebra.ofId R A) h]
439	  constructor
440	  · intro ⟨g, hg⟩
441	    let e : A ≃ₐ[R] R ⧸ RingHom.ker (algebraMap R A) ^ 2 :=
442	      .ofAlgHom _ _ (Ideal.Quotient.algHom_ext _ (by ext)) hg
443	    rw [IsIdempotentElem, ← pow_two, ← Ideal.mk_ker (I := _ ^ 2), ← Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq,
444	      ← e.toAlgHom.comp_algebraMap, RingHom.ker_comp_of_injective _ (by exact e.injective)]
445	  · intro H
446	    let e := (Ideal.quotientEquivAlgOfEq _ ((pow_two _).trans H)).trans
447	      (Ideal.quotientKerAlgEquivOfSurjective (f := Algebra.ofId R A) h)
448	    exact ⟨e.symm.toAlgHom, AlgHom.ext <| h.forall.mpr fun x ↦ by simp⟩
449	
450	end surjective

452	section BaseChange
453	
454	open scoped TensorProduct
455	
456	variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
457	variable {A : Type*} [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
458	variable (B : Type*) [CommRing B] [Algebra R B]
459	
460	instance [FormallySmooth R A] : FormallySmooth B (B ⊗[R] A) := by
461	  refine .of_comp_surjective fun C _ _ I hI f ↦ ?_
462	  letI := ((algebraMap B C).comp (algebraMap R B)).toAlgebra
463	  haveI : IsScalarTower R B C := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' rfl
464	  refine ⟨TensorProduct.productLeftAlgHom (Algebra.ofId B C) ?_, ?_⟩
465	  · exact FormallySmooth.lift I ⟨2, hI⟩ ((f.restrictScalars R).comp TensorProduct.includeRight)
466	  · apply AlgHom.restrictScalars_injective R
467	    apply TensorProduct.ext'
468	    intro b a
469	    suffices algebraMap B _ b * f (1 ⊗ₜ[R] a) = f (b ⊗ₜ[R] a) by simpa [Algebra.ofId_apply]
470	    rw [← Algebra.smul_def, ← map_smul, TensorProduct.smul_tmul', smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
471	
472	end BaseChange

474	section Localization
475	
476	variable {R A Rₘ Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing Rₘ] [CommRing Sₘ]
477	variable (M : Submonoid R)
478	variable [Algebra R A] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra A Sₘ] [Algebra R Rₘ] [Algebra Rₘ Sₘ]
479	variable [IsScalarTower R Rₘ Sₘ] [IsScalarTower R A Sₘ]
480	variable [IsLocalization M Rₘ] [IsLocalization (M.map (algebraMap R A)) Sₘ]
481	include M
482	
483	theorem of_isLocalization : FormallySmooth R Rₘ := by
484	  refine .of_comp_surjective fun Q _ _ I e f ↦ ?_
485	  have : ∀ x : M, IsUnit (algebraMap R Q x) := by
486	    intro x
487	    apply (IsNilpotent.isUnit_quotient_mk_iff ⟨2, e⟩).mp
488	    convert (IsLocalization.map_units Rₘ x).map f
489	    simp only [Ideal.Quotient.mk_algebraMap, AlgHom.commutes]
490	  let this : Rₘ →ₐ[R] Q :=
491	    { IsLocalization.lift this with commutes' := IsLocalization.lift_eq this }
492	  use this
493	  apply AlgHom.coe_ringHom_injective
494	  refine IsLocalization.ringHom_ext M ?_
495	  ext
496	  simp
497	
498	instance [FormallySmooth R A] (M : Submonoid A) : FormallySmooth R (Localization M) :=
499	  have : FormallySmooth A (Localization M) := of_isLocalization M
500	  .comp _ A _
501	
502	theorem localization_base [FormallySmooth R Sₘ] : FormallySmooth Rₘ Sₘ := by
503	  refine .of_comp_surjective fun Q _ _ I e f ↦ ?_
504	  letI := ((algebraMap Rₘ Q).comp (algebraMap R Rₘ)).toAlgebra
505	  letI : IsScalarTower R Rₘ Q := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq' rfl
506	  let f : Sₘ →ₐ[Rₘ] Q := by
507	    refine { FormallySmooth.lift I ⟨2, e⟩ (f.restrictScalars R) with commutes' := ?_ }
508	    intro r
509	    change
510	      (RingHom.comp (FormallySmooth.lift I ⟨2, e⟩ (f.restrictScalars R) : Sₘ →+* Q)
511	            (algebraMap _ _))
512	          r =
513	        algebraMap _ _ r
514	    congr 1
515	    refine IsLocalization.ringHom_ext M ?_
516	    rw [RingHom.comp_assoc, ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq, ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq,
517	      AlgHom.comp_algebraMap]
518	  use f
519	  ext
520	  simp [f]
521	
522	theorem localization_map [FormallySmooth R A] : FormallySmooth Rₘ Sₘ := by
523	  haveI : FormallySmooth A Sₘ := FormallySmooth.of_isLocalization (M.map (algebraMap R A))
524	  haveI : FormallySmooth R Sₘ := FormallySmooth.comp R A Sₘ
525	  exact FormallySmooth.localization_base M
526	
527	end Localization
528	
529	end FormallySmooth
530	
531	section
532	
533	variable (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
534	variable (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
535	
536	/-- An `R` algebra `A` is smooth if it is formally smooth and of finite presentation. -/
537	@[stacks 00T2 "In the stacks project, the definition of smooth is completely different, and tag
538	<https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/00TN> proves that their definition is equivalent to this.",
539	mk_iff]
540	class Smooth [CommRing R] (A : Type u) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] : Prop where
541	  formallySmooth : FormallySmooth R A := by infer_instance
542	  finitePresentation : FinitePresentation R A := by infer_instance
543	
544	end
545	
546	namespace Smooth
547	
548	attribute [instance] formallySmooth finitePresentation
549	
550	variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
551	variable {A B : Type*} [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [CommRing B] [Algebra R B]
552	
553	/-- Being smooth is transported via algebra isomorphisms. -/
554	theorem of_equiv [Smooth R A] (e : A ≃ₐ[R] B) : Smooth R B where
555	  formallySmooth := FormallySmooth.of_equiv e
556	  finitePresentation := FinitePresentation.equiv e
557	
558	/-- Localization at an element is smooth. -/
559	theorem of_isLocalization_Away (r : R) [IsLocalization.Away r A] : Smooth R A where
560	  formallySmooth := Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_isLocalization (Submonoid.powers r)
561	  finitePresentation := IsLocalization.Away.finitePresentation r
562	
563	section Comp
564	
565	variable (R A B)
566	
567	/-- Smooth is stable under composition. -/
568	theorem comp [Algebra A B] [IsScalarTower R A B] [Smooth R A] [Smooth A B] : Smooth R B where
569	  formallySmooth := FormallySmooth.comp R A B
570	  finitePresentation := FinitePresentation.trans R A B

Based on my search of Mathlib (version pinned in your project), here are the relevant lemmas connecting smoothness to reduced schemes:

## Key Findings (with file paths and signatures)

### 1. **Smooth implies Flat** (Best starting point for base reduction)
- **Declaration**: `Algebra.Smooth.flat`
- **File**: `Mathlib/RingTheory/Smooth/Flat.lean`
- **Signature**: `∀ [Algebra.Smooth R A], Module.Flat R A`
- **Relevance**: Smooth algebras are flat, and flatness + reduced base can preserve reducedness of the fiber

### 2. **Flatness + Reduced Base → Reduced Fiber**
- **Declaration**: `IsReduced.tensorProduct_of_flat_of_forall_fg`
- **File**: `Mathlib/RingTheory/Flat/Basic.lean`
- **Signature**: `[Module.Flat R C] → (∀ B : Subalgebra R A, B.FG → IsReduced (R ⊗ C B)) → IsReduced (R ⊗ C A)`
- **Relevance**: Shows reduction propagates through tensor products for flat modules (partial chain)

### 3. **Geometrically Reduced = Reduced after base extension**
- **Declaration**: `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced` class + `isReduced_of_isGeometricallyReduced`
- **File**: `Mathlib/RingTheory/Nilpotent/GeometricallyReduced.lean` (lines 64–67)
- **Signature**: `[IsGeometricallyReduced k A] → IsReduced A`
- **Relevance**: Orthogonal approach (geometry-focused), but shows `A` itself is reduced if geometrically reduced over an alg closed field

### 4. **Scheme-Level Results**
- **Declaration**: `AlgebraicGeometry.isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion`
- **File**: `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Properties.lean` (line 95–101)
- **Relevance**: Open immersions preserve reducedness; smooth → open loc. finitely presented

## Missing Direct Lemma
**Critical gap**: No single lemma of the form `Smooth f → IsReduced Y → IsReduced X` exists in Mathlib (tags 034V, 02G4 not formalized). You need to chain:

1. `Smooth f` → `Module.Flat R A` (smooth algebras are flat)
2. `Module.Flat R A` + `IsReduced R` → `IsReduced A` (requires finiteness hypothesis, see flat.lean)
3. Possibly appeal to the fact that over `Spec kbar` (principal ideal domain / regular ring), flat morphisms preserve reducedness more directly

**Recommendation**: File a direct lemma or use the flat path with localization arguments.

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Artinian
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Basic
10	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.SchemeTheoreticallyDominant
11	
12	/-!
13	# Geometrically Reduced Schemes
14	
15	## Main results
16	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyReduced`:
17	  We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically reduced if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
18	  a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is reduced.
19	  We also provide the fact that this is stable under base change (by infer_instance)
20	- `GeometricallyReduced.iff_geometricallyReduced_fiber`:
21	  A scheme is geometrically reduced over `S` iff the fibers of all
22	  `s : S` are geometrically reduced.
23	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyReduced.isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian`:
24	  If `X` is geometrically reduced and flat over a reduced and locally noetherian scheme,
25	  then `X` is also reduced.
26	  In particular, the base change of a geometrically reduced and flat scheme to an
27	  reduced and locally noetherian scheme is reduced (by infer_instance).
28	
29	## TODO
30	Get rid of the noetherian assumption.
31	-/

11	/-!
12	# Geometrically Integral Schemes
13	
14	## Main results
15	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIntegral`:
16	  We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically integral if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
17	  a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is integral.
18	  We also provide the fact that this is stable under base change (`by infer_instance`)
19	- `GeometricallyIntegral.iff_geometricallyIntegral_fiber`:
20	  A scheme is geometrically integral over `S` iff the fibers of all
21	  `s : S` are geometrically integral.
22	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIntegral.isIntegral_of_isLocallyNoetherian`:
23	  If `X` is geometrically integral, flat, and universally open (e.g. when over a field),
24	  over an integral locally noetherian scheme, then `X` is also integral.
25	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIntegral.isIntegral_of_subsingleton`:
26	  If `X` is geometrically integral over a field, then it is integral.
27	-/
28	
29	@[expose] public section
30	
31	open CategoryTheory MorphismProperty Limits
32	
33	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
34

38	a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is integral. -/

41	  geometrically_isIntegral : geometrically IsIntegral f

44	    @GeometricallyIntegral = geometrically IsIntegral := by
45	  ext; exact geometricallyIntegral_iff _

52	  eta_expand

57	
58	instance (priority := low) [GeometricallyIntegral f] : GeometricallyIrreducible f :=

60

70	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst f g inferInstance

89	    [GeometricallyIntegral f] [Flat f] [UniversallyOpen f]

105	    [IsLocallyNoetherian Y] : IsIntegral (pullback f g) :=
106	  GeometricallyIntegral.isIntegral_of_isLocallyNoetherian (pullback.snd _ _)

109	    [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X] :

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2025 Dion Leijnse. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Dion Leijnse
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.FieldTheory.IsAlgClosed.AlgebraicClosure
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# Geometrically reduced algebras
13	
14	In this file we introduce geometrically reduced algebras.
15	For a field `k`, we say that a `k`-algebra `A` is geometrically reduced (`IsGeometricallyReduced`)
16	if the tensor product `AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] A` is reduced.
17	
18	## Main results
19	- `IsGeometricallyReduced.of_forall_fg`: for a field `k` and a commutative `k`-algebra `A`, if all
20	  finitely generated subalgebras `B` of `A` are geometrically reduced, then `A` is geometrically
21	  reduced.
22	
23	## References
24	- See [https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/05DS] for some theory of geometrically reduced algebras.
25	  Note that their definition differs from the one here, we still need a proof that these are
26	  equivalent (see TODO).
27	
28	## TODO
29	- Prove that if `A` is a geometrically reduced `k`-algebra, then for every field extension `K` of
30	  `k` the `K`-algebra `K ⊗[k] A` is reduced.
31	
32	-/
33	
34	@[expose] public section
35	
36	open TensorProduct
37	
38	noncomputable section
39	
40	namespace Algebra
41	
42	variable {k A : Type*} [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A]
43	
44	/-- The `k`-algebra `A` is geometrically reduced iff its base change to `AlgebraicClosure k` is
45	  reduced. -/
46	@[mk_iff]
47	class IsGeometricallyReduced (k A : Type*) [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A] : Prop where
48	  isReduced_algebraicClosure_tensorProduct : IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure k ⊗[k] A)
49	
50	attribute [instance] IsGeometricallyReduced.isReduced_algebraicClosure_tensorProduct
51	
52	instance (k A K : Type*) [Field k] [Ring A] [Algebra k A] [Field K] [Algebra k K]
53	    [Algebra.IsAlgebraic k K] [IsGeometricallyReduced k A] : IsReduced (K ⊗[k] A) :=
54	  isReduced_of_injective
55	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.map ((IsAlgClosed.lift : K →ₐ[k] AlgebraicClosure k)) 1)
56	    (Module.Flat.rTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ (RingHom.injective _))
57	
58	lemma IsGeometricallyReduced.of_injective {B : Type*} [Ring B] [Algebra k B] (f : A →ₐ[k] B)
59	    (hf : Function.Injective f) [IsGeometricallyReduced k B] : IsGeometricallyReduced k A :=
60	  ⟨isReduced_of_injective (Algebra.TensorProduct.map 1 f)
61	    (Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap _ hf)⟩
62	
63	variable (k) in
64	theorem isReduced_of_isGeometricallyReduced [IsGeometricallyReduced k A] : IsReduced A :=
65	  isReduced_of_injective
66	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : A →ₐ[k] (AlgebraicClosure k) ⊗[k] A)
67	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight_injective <| FaithfulSMul.algebraMap_injective _ _)
68	
69	/-- If all finitely generated subalgebras of `A` are geometrically reduced, then `A` is
70	  geometrically reduced. -/
71	@[stacks 030T]
72	theorem IsGeometricallyReduced.of_forall_fg
73	    (h : ∀ B : Subalgebra k A, B.FG → IsGeometricallyReduced k B) :
74	    IsGeometricallyReduced k A := by
75	  simp_rw [isGeometricallyReduced_iff] at h
76	  exact ⟨IsReduced.tensorProduct_of_flat_of_forall_fg h⟩
77	
78	end Algebra

private theorem av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy
    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
    CodimOneFree f := by
  -- **Iter-182 structural refactor.** Materialise the variety-package
  -- instances on `A.left` needed by Milne Lemma 3.3
  -- (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): `IsIntegral` from the
  -- split helper `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred`; `IsReduced` from
  -- `isReduced_of_isIntegral`; `IsSeparated` from `IsProper`;
  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType` from `Smooth`.
  haveI : IsIntegral A.left := av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A
  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
  -- Apply Milne Lemma 3.3: the indeterminacy locus is empty or every point
  -- of it lies in the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
  have hdisj := indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme f
  intro x hx
  rcases hdisj with hempty | hpure
  · -- **Branch 1 (CLOSED).** Empty indeterminacy locus: every point of `X`
    -- — in particular `x` — lies in `f.domain` (no codim-1 obstruction).
    by_contra hnotin
    have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
    rw [hempty] at hxZ
    exact hxZ.elim
  · -- **Branch 2 (PROJECT GAP).** The disjunct gives, for any `x ∈ Z(f)`,
    -- a codim-1 generic point `z` with `x ∈ closure {z}`. To prove
    -- `x ∈ f.domain` for our codim-1 `x`, contrapositive: assume
    -- `x ∈ Z(f)`. The disjunct produces a `z` with `coheight z = 1` and
    -- `x ∈ closure {z}` — but for a codim-1 `x`, sobriety of the scheme
    -- forces `z = x` (chains of length 1 starting at `z` and ending at a
    -- specialisation `x` of equal coheight collapse in a sober T₀ space),
    -- so `x = z` is a codim-1 point of `Z(f)`. The contradiction needs
    -- the **codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1** (project-side
    -- `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s unbundled half), which is
    -- currently encapsulated inside that theorem's `sorry`'d body in
    -- `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` rather than exposed as a separate
    -- lemma. Once a lemma
    -- `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete : ∀ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f, coheight z ≥ 2`
    -- lands in `CodimOneExtension.lean`, branch 2 closes via:
    --   `by_contra hnotin; obtain ⟨z, hz1, hxz⟩ := hpure x hnotin;`
    --   `obtain rfl := sober_eq_of_specializes_of_coheight_eq hxz hx hz1;`
    --   `exact absurd hx (lt_irrefl 1 ∘ ... ∘ codimGe2 hnotin)`.
    sorry

/-- **Iter-182 documentation helper: branch-1 of `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy`.**
The empty-indeterminacy branch of Milne Lemma 3.3 closes `CodimOneFree`
without depending on the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1. Exposed as a
standalone lemma so the structural split survives any future refactor of
the parent helper. Axiom-clean. -/
private theorem codimOneFree_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty
    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} {f : X.RationalMap Y}
    (h : indeterminacyLocus f = ∅) : CodimOneFree f := by
  intro x _
  by_contra hnotin
  have hxZ : x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f := hnotin
  rw [h] at hxZ
  exact hxZ.elim

14	
15	We show that separably generated extensions of fields are smooth.
16	In particular finitely generated field extensions over perfect fields are smooth.
17	
18	-/

25	lemma Algebra.FormallySmooth.adjoin_of_algebraicIndependent {v : ι → L}
26	    (hb : AlgebraicIndependent K v) :
27	    Algebra.FormallySmooth K (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) := by
28	  have : Algebra.FormallySmooth K (adjoin K (Set.range v)) :=
29	    .of_equiv hb.aevalEquiv
30	  have : Algebra.FormallySmooth (adjoin K (Set.range v))
31	      (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) :=
32	    .of_isLocalization (nonZeroDivisors _)
33	  exact .comp _ (adjoin K (Set.range v)) _

36	lemma Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_algebraicIndependent {v : ι → L}
37	    (hb : AlgebraicIndependent K v) (hb' : IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v) = ⊤) :
38	    Algebra.FormallySmooth K L := by
39	  have := Algebra.FormallySmooth.adjoin_of_algebraicIndependent hb
40	  rw [hb'] at this
41	  exact .of_equiv IntermediateField.topEquiv
42

45	    {v : ι → L} (hb : AlgebraicIndependent K v)
46	    [Algebra.IsSeparable (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) L] :
47	    Algebra.FormallySmooth K L := by
48	  have := FormallySmooth.adjoin_of_algebraicIndependent hb
49	  have : FormallyEtale (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) L :=
50	    Algebra.FormallyEtale.of_isSeparable _ L

326	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
327	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
328	
329	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
330	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
331	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
332	
333	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
334	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
335	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
336	`J → A`.

339	Milne's two-line argument:

342	   `thm:codim_one_extension` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): given a

344	   variety, the map extends uniquely to a regular morphism. This requires

365	theorem extend_to_av
366	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
367	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
368	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
369	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
370	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
371	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
372	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
373	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
374	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
375	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by

# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean

## Iter-182 status overview

**File classification.** Per `PROGRESS.md` iter-182 prover phase, this file is
listed in `## Off-limits this iteration` and `## Standing deferrals` (gated
on `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` body landing in
`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, itself iter-184+ per Lane G PIVOT rationale
in `analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md`). The file was nonetheless assigned to
a prover lane this iter, so I pushed the structural refactor as far as the
gates permit and documented the residual gaps.

**Build state.** `lake env lean AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`
clean: **2 warnings (`declaration uses sorry`) + 0 errors + 0 axioms**. Sorry
count UNCHANGED from iter-181 (2 → 2); the iter-182 work refactors the
proof structure of one sorry rather than reducing the count.

## Sorry inventory

### Sorry 1 — `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` (L176, body L194)
**Status: UNCHANGED iter-182 (Mathlib-gap).** Body of the helper unchanged;
the single inline `sorry` discharges `IsReduced A.left` from `[Smooth A.hom]`.

**The gap is genuine.** Search across the Mathlib at the project's pinned
commit found *no* bridge `Smooth f → IsReduced X`, `Smooth f → GeometricallyReduced f`,
`Algebra.Smooth K A → IsReduced A`, or
`Algebra.Smooth K A → Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced K A`:

- `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Smooth.lean` has only
  `instance [Smooth f] : Flat f` (L113) and
  `Scheme.Hom.dense_smoothLocus_of_perfectField` (the *reverse* direction,
  requires `[IsReduced X]` as a hypothesis).
- `Mathlib/RingTheory/Smooth/Field.lean` only treats *field* extensions
  (`Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField` for `EssFiniteType` field
  extensions — does not apply to a general smooth `K`-algebra).
- `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Geometrically/Reduced.lean` requires
  `[GeometricallyReduced f]` as input — exactly what the missing bridge
  would provide.
- `Mathlib/RingTheory/Nilpotent/GeometricallyReduced.lean` defines
  `IsGeometricallyReduced k A` without a `Smooth → IsGeometricallyReduced`
  instance.

**Stacks reference:** the missing implication is **Stacks 034V** / **02G4**
("Smoothness over a perfect field implies geometrically reduced") — not
formalized at commit `b80f227`. The existing docstring on the lemma documents
this precisely; the iter-182 search confirms no alternative path through
Mathlib exists.

**Discharge path when the bridge lands.** Once `Smooth A.hom → IsReduced A.left`
or `Smooth f → GeometricallyReduced f` ships in Mathlib, the single sorry
discharges via either:
1. `haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance` (if `Smooth → IsReduced`
   direct), OR
2. The chain `Smooth A.hom → Flat A.hom + GeometricallyReduced A.hom`,
   combined with `IsReduced (Spec kbar)` (field is reduced) +
   `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec kbar)` (field is noetherian), via
   `GeometricallyReduced.isReduced_of_flat_of_isLocallyNoetherian` (already
   in Mathlib).

The helper then becomes axiom-clean automatically — no further project work
needed at this file.

### Sorry 2 — `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` (L233, body L283)
**Status: REFACTORED iter-182. Branch 1 CLOSED inline; branch 2 isolated.**

**Iter-182 structural refactor.** The previous body was a bare top-level
`sorry`. I rewrote the body to:

1. Materialise the variety-package instances on `A.left` required by
   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (`IsIntegral` via the
   split helper `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred`; `IsReduced` via
   `isReduced_of_isIntegral`; `IsSeparated` from `IsProper`;
   `LocallyOfFiniteType` from `Smooth`).
2. Invoke `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme f` to obtain the
   disjunction `indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨ ∀ x ∈ Z, ∃ z, coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure {z}`.
3. `intro x hx` and split on the disjunction:
   - **Branch 1 (CLOSED).** `indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`: contradiction via
     `Set.not_mem_empty`-style argument. Axiom-clean.
   - **Branch 2 (sorry).** Codim-1 indeterminacy disjunct — requires the
     codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 (currently encapsulated inside
     `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s `sorry`'d body in
     `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`).

The branch-1 logic is additionally extracted as a standalone axiom-clean
helper `codimOneFree_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` (file-level), so the
structural split survives any future refactor of the parent helper.

**Discharge path for branch 2.** Once `CodimOneExtension.lean` exposes a
standalone lemma

```
theorem indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete
    [variety/proper hypotheses]
    (f : X.RationalMap Y) : ∀ z ∈ indeterminacyLocus f, Order.coheight z ≥ 2
```

(the unbundled codim-≥2 half of Milne 3.1), branch 2 closes via:

```
by_contra hnotin
obtain ⟨z, hz1, hxz⟩ := hpure x hnotin
-- T0/sobriety + equal coheight 1 + specialization ⟹ x = z
obtain rfl := <sober equality from hxz, hx, hz1>
exact absurd hz1 (by linarith [codimGe2 hnotin])
```

This is precisely what the iter-184+ Lane G follow-up on
`CodimOneExtension.lean` (per `analogies/stacks-00tt-coheight.md`) will
deliver.

## Auxiliary scaffolding landed iter-182

- New private lemma `codimOneFree_of_indeterminacyLocus_eq_empty` (L290):
  axiom-clean, captures the branch-1 logic standalone.

## Search log (negative results)

The following Mathlib searches returned no matches for the missing bridges:

- `grep "Smooth.*[Ii]sReduced|isReduced.*[Ss]mooth"` in
  `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/` and `Mathlib/RingTheory/`: nothing.
- `grep "Smooth.*GeometricallyReduced|GeometricallyReduced.*Smooth"`:
  nothing.
- `grep "Smooth.*reduced"` (case-insensitive): nothing.
- `grep "034V|02G4"` (Stacks tags): nothing.
- `lean_local_search "indeterminacy_pure"`: nothing

Confirms the gap is genuine Mathlib-upstream content, not a lookup miss.

## Dead-end warnings for future provers

1. **Do NOT try `instance IsGeometricallyReduced kbar A` from `Smooth`**:
   no such instance exists in Mathlib; declaring one would itself be a
   substantive Mathlib contribution (Stacks 034V/02G4).
2. **Do NOT try to close branch 2 of `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy`

3. **Do NOT split `av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` further**: the proof

Both pinned declarations (`extend_to_av` L365 and the two split helpers
`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` L176 + `av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy`

`av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` is `sorry`-containing on branch 2; both
upstream helpers have inline `sorry`'s on their substantive content.

The blueprint chapter `chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex`
should NOT change `\leanok` status this iter (sorries unchanged); the

**iter-183+ work on this file:** stay deferred until `CodimOneExtension.lean`
lands the codim-≥2 standalone lemma (gated on Lane G iter-183+ post-
