content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).

15	* the `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f`;
16	* the projective line `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` / `ProjectiveLineBar` as an object of
17	  `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`;
18	* properness of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` (`projectiveLineBar_isProper`);
19	* the scaffold `GeometricallyIrreducible` / `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instances;
20	* the 2-chart affine open cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`.
21	
22	Downstream strata: `ChartIso` (chart-ring iso), `Points` (k̄-points + `Ga` + `Gm`),
23	`GmScaling` (chart-bridge + `σ_×`).
24	-/

37	/-! ### (A) The projective line `ℙ¹` over `Spec k̄` (the `Proj` realisation) -/
38	
39	/-- The **standard ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`** by total degree.
40	
41	This is the homogeneous-component decomposition `MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule`
42	specialised to two variables (`Fin 2`) over `k̄`. The `GradedRing` instance is
43	`MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra` (free).
44	
45	Used to define `ProjectiveLineBar = Proj` of this graded ring. -/
46	abbrev projectiveLineBarGrading (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
47	    ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
48	  MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar
49	
50	instance projectiveLineBarGrading_gradedRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
51	    GradedRing (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) :=
52	  MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra
53	
54	/-- `kbar`-algebra structure on `HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f` via the
55	composition `kbar →+* ↥(𝒜 0) →+* Away 𝒜 f`. Mathlib only ships
56	`Algebra (𝒜 0) (HomogeneousLocalization 𝒜 x)`; this instance bridges the
57	remaining `kbar →+* 𝒜 0` algebra map shipped via `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebra`.
58	Required for `TensorProduct kbar (Away _ _) _` to synthesize `CommRing`/`Algebra kbar`. -/
59	noncomputable instance algebraKbarAway (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
60	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :
61	    Algebra kbar
62	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f) :=
63	  Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))
64	
65	/-- **The projective line over `Spec k̄` as a scheme.** This is `Proj 𝒜` of the standard
66	ℕ-graded `k̄[X₀, X₁]`. Carries an `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance via
67	`Proj.toSpecZero` composed with the algebra-map identification of the degree-`0` piece
68	with `k̄` (Mathlib's `SetLike.GradeZero.instAlgebraSubtypeMemOfNat`).
69	
70	`IsProper` is FREE — see the `instIsProper` instance below. `IsAlgClosed kbar` is *not*
71	required for the scheme itself but is needed for downstream `Smooth`/geometric
72	irreducibility instances. -/
73	def ProjectiveLineBarScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
74	  Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
75	
76	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` structure on `ProjectiveLineBarScheme` via
77	`Proj.toSpecZero` and the identification `k̄ ≃ ↥(𝒜 0)`. -/
78	instance projectiveLineBarScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
79	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
80	  hom := Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
81	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
82	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0)))
83	
84	/-- **The projective line `ℙ¹_{k̄}` as an object of `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** This is the
85	concrete scheme used by `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (iter-166) to formalise the
86	`𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut. -/
87	def ProjectiveLineBar (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
88	  (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
89	
90	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is proper over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
91	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero…` — the algebra `k̄[X₀, X₁]` is finite type
92	over its degree-`0` piece `↥(𝒜 0) ≃ k̄` — chained via the standard properness-of-composition
93	lemma with the `Spec.map` of the algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` (which is bijective hence
94	gives an iso of `Spec`s).
95	
96	The chain:
97	* `Proj.toSpecZero 𝒜` is proper (Mathlib's
98	  `Proj.instIsProperToSpecZeroOfFiniteTypeSubtypeMemOfNatNat`, given
99	  `Algebra.FiniteType ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄)` — supplied by the
100	  `IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ _)` + `Algebra.FiniteType k̄ (MvPolynomial _ _)`
101	  chain via `Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType`);
102	* `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is an iso because `algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective
103	  in this standard ℕ-grading case (`𝒜 0 = MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) k̄ 0`
104	  is the constants subalgebra, naturally ≅ k̄);
105	* composition of proper + iso is proper. -/
106	instance projectiveLineBar_isProper (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
107	    IsProper (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
108	  -- Unfold to expose the Proj.toSpecZero ≫ Spec.map chain.
109	  change IsProper (Proj.toSpecZero (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ≫
110	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
111	      (algebraMap kbar ↥((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0))))
112	  -- The IsScalarTower kbar ↥(𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial _ kbar) needed for the FiniteType derivation.
113	  haveI : IsScalarTower kbar
114	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
115	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
116	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
117	  -- MvPolynomial.{Fin 2} k̄ is finite type over ↥(𝒜 0) (chained from finite-type over k̄).
118	  haveI : Algebra.FiniteType
119	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)
120	      (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
121	    Algebra.FiniteType.of_restrictScalars_finiteType kbar _ _
122	  -- The algebra map `k̄ → ↥(𝒜 0)` is bijective: the degree-`0` piece of the homogeneous
123	  -- decomposition is exactly the constants `C(k̄) ⊆ MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`. Injectivity
124	  -- follows from `MvPolynomial.C_injective`. Surjectivity uses
125	  -- `MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem` (which says the degree-`0` component of a
126	  -- homogeneous-degree-`0` polynomial is itself) plus `homogeneousComponent_zero`
127	  -- (which expresses the degree-`0` component as `C` of the constant coefficient).
128	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (algebraMap kbar
129	      ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)) := by
130	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
131	    · intro x y h
132	      apply MvPolynomial.C_injective (Fin 2) kbar
133	      exact congrArg Subtype.val h
134	    · intro ⟨v, hv⟩
135	      refine ⟨MvPolynomial.coeff 0 v, ?_⟩
136	      apply Subtype.ext
137	      rw [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap]
138	      have key := MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_of_mem hv (m := 0)
139	      -- `key : (homogeneousComponent 0) v = if 0 = 0 then v else 0` which simp resolves
140	      -- and combines with `homogeneousComponent_zero` to give `C (coeff 0 v) = v`.
141	      simp only [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero, if_true] at key
142	      exact key
143	  -- Therefore `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ ↥(𝒜 0))` is iso, and composition of proper + iso is
144	  -- proper.
145	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
146	      (algebraMap kbar ↥(MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) kbar 0)))) := by
147	    rw [isIso_SpecMap_iff]
148	    exact hbij
149	  infer_instance
150	
151	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold sorry
152	(Mathlib does not ship `GeometricallyIrreducible` for `Proj` of a polynomial ring;
153	plan-marked acceptable for iter-165). -/
154	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
155	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
156	  sorry
157	
158	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension `1` over `Spec k̄`.** Project-side scaffold
159	sorry (Mathlib does not ship `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` for `Proj`; plan-marked
160	acceptable for iter-165). -/
161	instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
162	    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom :=
163	  sorry
164	
165	/-! ### The 2-chart affine cover of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` -/
166	
167	/-- **Per-chart degree-1 homogeneity witness** for the affine cover of
168	`ProjectiveLineBarScheme` by `D₊(X 0)` and `D₊(X 1)`. Hoisted to top-level
169	out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> simp` proof inside
170	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (per `analogies/gmscaling-cover-bridge.md`
171	Step 1) so the kernel doesn't `whnf` tactic-built proof closures during
172	downstream defeq in `gmScalingP1`. -/
173	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
174	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
175	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
176	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
177	  fun i => by
178	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
179	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
180	
181	/-- **Positive-degree witness** for the affine cover indexing. Hoisted to
182	top-level out of the inline `by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos` proof
183	inside `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` for the same defeq reasons as
184	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. -/
185	lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
186	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
187	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
188	
189	/-- **The 2-chart affine open cover of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`** by `D₊(X 0)` and
190	`D₊(X 1)`. Specialises `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` to the family
191	`![X 0, X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` with `m := ![1, 1]`.
192	
193	The non-trivial bit is `hf`: the irrelevant ideal `(X 0, X 1)` is contained in
194	`Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` — proved by writing any irrelevant element as a sum of monomials
195	whose multi-index `d ≠ 0`, hence `d j > 0` for some `j ∈ Fin 2`, hence
196	`monomial d r ∈ Ideal.span {X 0, X 1}` via `MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial`. -/
197	noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
198	    (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar).AffineOpenCover :=
199	  Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
200	    (m := ![1, 1])
201	    (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1])
202	    (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar)
203	    projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm
204	    (by
205	      classical
206	      intro p hp
207	      rw [HomogeneousIdeal.mem_iff, HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_iff,
208	        GradedRing.proj_apply] at hp
209	      have hp' : MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p = 0 := by
210	        have := hp
211	        rw [show DirectSum.decompose (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) p 0
212	            = ⟨MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent 0 p,
213	                MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_mem 0 p⟩ from Subtype.ext
214	          (MvPolynomial.decomposition.decompose'_apply p 0)] at this
215	        exact this
216	      have h0 : MvPolynomial.coeff 0 p = 0 := by
217	        rw [MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent_zero] at hp'
218	        exact MvPolynomial.C_injective _ _ (hp'.trans MvPolynomial.C_0.symm)
219	      rw [MvPolynomial.as_sum p]
220	      refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun d hd ↦ ?_
221	      have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff d p ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.mem_support_iff.mp hd
222	      have hd_ne : d ≠ 0 := fun heq => hcoeff (heq ▸ h0)
223	      have hd_nonzero : d 0 ≠ 0 ∨ d 1 ≠ 0 := by
224	        by_contra h
225	        push Not at h
226	        apply hd_ne
227	        ext k
228	        fin_cases k
229	        · simpa using h.1
230	        · simpa using h.2
231	      rcases hd_nonzero with h0' | h1'
232	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
233	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
234	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h0')
235	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
236	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨0, rfl⟩)
237	      · obtain ⟨q, hq⟩ : (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∣
238	            MvPolynomial.monomial d (MvPolynomial.coeff d p) :=
239	          MvPolynomial.X_dvd_monomial.mpr (Or.inr h1')
240	        rw [hq, mul_comm]
241	        exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (Ideal.subset_span ⟨1, rfl⟩))
242	
243	end AlgebraicGeometry
244	
245	end

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Andrew Yang
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.UniversallyOpen
10	
11	/-!
12	# Geometrically Irreducible Schemes
13	
14	## Main results
15	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible`:
16	  We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically irreducible if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
17	  a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is irreducible.
18	  We also provide the fact that this is stable under base change (by infer_instance)
19	- `GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber`:
20	  A scheme is geometrically irreducible over `S` iff the fibers of all
21	  `s : S` are geometrically irreducible.
22	- `AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace`:
23	  If `X` is geometrically irreducible and universally open (e.g. when flat + finite presentation),
24	  over an irreducible scheme, then `X` is also irreducible.
25	  In particular, the base change of a geometrically irreducible and universally open scheme to an
26	  irreducible scheme is irreducible (by infer_instance).
27	-/
28	
29	universe u
30	
31	@[expose] public section
32	
33	open CategoryTheory MorphismProperty Limits
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
36	
37	variable {X Y Z S : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ S) (g : Y ⟶ S)
38	
39	/-- We say that morphism `f : X ⟶ Y` is geometrically irreducible if for all `Spec K ⟶ Y` with `K`
40	a field, `X ×[Y] Spec K` is irreducible. -/
41	@[mk_iff]
42	class GeometricallyIrreducible (f : X ⟶ Y) : Prop where
43	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace : geometrically (IrreducibleSpace ·) f
44	
45	lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically :
46	    @GeometricallyIrreducible = geometrically (IrreducibleSpace ·) := by
47	  ext; exact geometricallyIrreducible_iff _
48	
49	instance : IsStableUnderBaseChange @GeometricallyIrreducible :=
50	  GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically ▸ inferInstance
51	
52	instance [GeometricallyIrreducible g] : GeometricallyIrreducible (pullback.fst f g) :=
53	  MorphismProperty.pullback_fst f g inferInstance
54	
55	instance [GeometricallyIrreducible f] : GeometricallyIrreducible (pullback.snd f g) :=
56	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd f g inferInstance
57	
58	instance (V : S.Opens) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] : GeometricallyIrreducible (f ∣_ V) :=
59	  MorphismProperty.of_isPullback (isPullback_morphismRestrict ..).flip ‹_›
60	
61	instance (s : S) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] :
62	    GeometricallyIrreducible (f.fiberToSpecResidueField s) :=
63	  MorphismProperty.pullback_snd _ _ inferInstance
64	
65	instance (s : S) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] : IrreducibleSpace (f.fiber s) :=
66	  GeometricallyIrreducible.geometrically_irreducibleSpace _ _ _ (.of_hasPullback _ _)
67	
68	instance (priority := low) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] : Surjective f :=
69	  ⟨fun x ↦ ⟨_, (f.range_fiberι x).le ⟨Nonempty.some inferInstance, rfl⟩⟩⟩
70	
71	lemma Scheme.Hom.isIrreducible_preimage
72	    [GeometricallyIrreducible f] (hf : IsOpenMap f)
73	    {s : Set S} (hs : IsIrreducible s) : IsIrreducible (f ⁻¹' s) := by
74	  wlog H : ∃ x, s = {x} generalizing s
75	  · refine hs.preimage_of_isPreirreducible_fiber _ hf
76	      (fun x ↦ (this isIrreducible_singleton ⟨_, rfl⟩).isPreirreducible) ?_
77	    rw [Set.range_eq_univ.mpr f.surjective, Set.inter_univ]
78	    exact hs.nonempty
79	  obtain ⟨s, rfl⟩ := H
80	  rw [← f.range_fiberι, ← Set.image_univ]
81	  exact (IrreducibleSpace.isIrreducible_univ _).image _ (f.fiberι _).continuous.continuousOn
82	
83	/-- If `f : X ⟶ S` is geometrically irreducible and open,
84	then `f` induces an equivalence between the irreducible components of `X` and `S`. -/
85	@[simps!]
86	def Scheme.Hom.irreducibleComponentsEquiv [GeometricallyIrreducible f] (hf : IsOpenMap f) :
87	    irreducibleComponents X ≃ irreducibleComponents S :=
88	  (irreducibleComponentsEquivOfIsPreirreducibleFiber f f.continuous hf
89	    (fun _ ↦ (f.isIrreducible_preimage hf isIrreducible_singleton).isPreirreducible)
90	    f.surjective).symm.toEquiv
91	
92	lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace
93	    [GeometricallyIrreducible f] [IrreducibleSpace S] (hf : IsOpenMap f) : IrreducibleSpace X := by
94	  simpa [irreducibleSpace_def] using
95	    f.isIrreducible_preimage hf (IrreducibleSpace.isIrreducible_univ _)
96	
97	/-- If `X` is geometrically irreducible over a point, then it is irreducible. -/
98	lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton
99	    [GeometricallyIrreducible f] [Subsingleton S] [Nonempty S] : IrreducibleSpace X :=
100	  have : IrreducibleSpace S := ⟨‹_›⟩
101	  GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace (f := f) fun _ _ ↦ isOpen_discrete _
102	
103	/-- If `X` is geometrically irreducible and universally open over `S` and `Y` is irreducible,
104	then `X ×ₛ Y` is irreducible.
105	
106	The universally open assumption in particular holds when it is flat and locally of finite
107	presentation, or when `S` is a field. -/
108	instance [GeometricallyIrreducible f] [UniversallyOpen f] [IrreducibleSpace Y] :
109	    IrreducibleSpace ↥(pullback f g) :=
110	  GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace (pullback.snd _ _) (pullback.snd f g).isOpenMap
111	
112	instance [GeometricallyIrreducible g] [UniversallyOpen g] [IrreducibleSpace X] :
113	    IrreducibleSpace ↥(pullback f g) :=
114	  GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace (pullback.fst _ _) (pullback.fst f g).isOpenMap
115	
116	lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber :
117	    GeometricallyIrreducible f ↔ ∀ s, GeometricallyIrreducible (f.fiberToSpecResidueField s) := by
118	  simp only [GeometricallyIrreducible.eq_geometrically,
119	    ← geometrically_iff_forall_fiberToSpecResidueField]
120	
121	lemma GeometricallyIrreducible.comp
122	    (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [GeometricallyIrreducible f] [GeometricallyIrreducible g]
123	    [UniversallyOpen f] [UniversallyOpen g] :
124	    GeometricallyIrreducible (f ≫ g) := by
125	  refine ⟨geometrically_iff_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms.mpr fun K _ x ↦ ?_⟩
126	  rw [← (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso g x f).hom.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff]
127	  infer_instance
128	
129	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
130	/-- If an open subscheme `U` of `X` surjects onto `S` and `X` is geometrically irreducible over `S`,
131	then also `U` is geometrically irreducible over `S`. -/
132	instance {U : Scheme.{u}} {f : U ⟶ X} {g : X ⟶ S} [IsOpenImmersion f] [GeometricallyIrreducible g]
133	    [Surjective (f ≫ g)] :
134	    GeometricallyIrreducible (f ≫ g) := by
135	  rw [GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber]
136	  intro s
137	  refine ⟨geometrically_iff_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms.mpr fun K _ x ↦ ?_⟩
138	  let i : pullback ((f ≫ g).fiberToSpecResidueField s) x ⟶
139	      pullback (g.fiberToSpecResidueField s) x :=
140	    pullback.map _ _ _ _ (pullback.map _ _ _ _ f (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _) (by simp) (by simp)) (𝟙 _) (𝟙 _)
141	      (by simp [Scheme.Hom.fiberToSpecResidueField]) (by simp)
142	  have : Nonempty ((f ≫ g).fiber s) := by
143	    rw [((f ≫ g).fiberHomeo s).nonempty_congr, Set.nonempty_coe_sort]
144	    exact (Set.singleton_nonempty s).preimage (f ≫ g).surjective
145	  exact i.isOpenEmbedding.irreducibleSpace
146	
147	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Extension.Presentation.Submersive
9	
10	/-!
11	# Standard smooth algebras
12	
13	A standard smooth algebra is an algebra that admits a `SubmersivePresentation`. A standard
14	smooth algebra is of relative dimension `n` if it admits a submersive presentation of
15	dimension `n`.
16	
17	While every standard smooth algebra is smooth, the converse does not hold. But if `S` is `R`-smooth,
18	then `S` is `R`-standard smooth locally on `S`, i.e. there exists a set `{ t }` of `S` that
19	generates the unit ideal, such that `Sₜ` is `R`-standard smooth for every `t` (TODO, see below).
20	
21	## Main definitions
22	
23	All of these are in the `Algebra` namespace. Let `S` be an `R`-algebra.
24	
25	- `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth`: `S` is `R`-standard smooth if `S` admits a submersive
26	  `R`-presentation.
27	- `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.relativeDimension`: If `S` is `R`-standard smooth this is the dimension
28	  of an arbitrary submersive `R`-presentation of `S`. This is independent of the choice
29	  of the presentation (TODO, see below).
30	- `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n`: `S` is `R`-standard smooth of relative dimension
31	  `n` if it admits a submersive `R`-presentation of dimension `n`.
32	
33	## TODO
34	
35	- Show that locally on the target, smooth algebras are standard smooth.
36	
37	## Notes
38	
39	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop "Formalizing algebraic geometry"
40	in June 2024.
41	
42	-/
43	
44	@[expose] public section
45	
46	universe t t' w w' u v
47	
48	open TensorProduct Module MvPolynomial
49	
50	variable (n m : ℕ)
51	
52	namespace Algebra
53	
54	variable (R : Type u) (S : Type v) (ι : Type w) (σ : Type t) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
55	
56	attribute [local instance] Fintype.ofFinite
57	
58	/--
59	An `R`-algebra `S` is called standard smooth, if there
60	exists a submersive presentation.
61	-/
62	class IsStandardSmooth : Prop where
63	  out : ∃ (ι σ : Type) (_ : Finite σ), Finite ι ∧ Nonempty (SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ)
64	
65	variable [Finite σ]
66	
67	variable {R S ι σ} in
68	lemma SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmooth [Finite ι] (P : SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ) :
69	    IsStandardSmooth R S := by
70	  exact ⟨_, _, _, inferInstance, ⟨P.reindex (Fintype.equivFin _).symm (Fintype.equivFin _).symm⟩⟩
71	
72	/--
73	The relative dimension of a standard smooth `R`-algebra `S` is
74	the dimension of an arbitrarily chosen submersive `R`-presentation of `S`.
75	
76	Note: If `S` is non-trivial, this number is independent of the choice of the presentation as it is
77	equal to the `S`-rank of `Ω[S/R]`
78	(see `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`).
79	-/
80	noncomputable def IsStandardSmooth.relativeDimension [IsStandardSmooth R S] : ℕ :=
81	  letI := ‹IsStandardSmooth R S›.out.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose
82	  ‹IsStandardSmooth R S›.out.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.some.dimension
83	
84	/--
85	An `R`-algebra `S` is called standard smooth of relative dimension `n`, if there exists
86	a submersive presentation of dimension `n`.
87	-/
88	class IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension : Prop where
89	  out : ∃ (ι σ : Type) (_ : Finite σ) (_ : Finite ι) (P : SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ),
90	    P.dimension = n
91	
92	variable {R S ι σ n} in
93	lemma SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension [Finite ι]
94	    (P : SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ) (hP : P.dimension = n) :
95	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S := by
96	  refine ⟨⟨_, _, _, inferInstance,
97	    P.reindex (Fintype.equivFin _).symm (Fintype.equivFin σ).symm, ?_⟩⟩
98	  simp [hP]
99	
100	variable {R} {S}
101	
102	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth
103	    [H : IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S] : IsStandardSmooth R S :=
104	  ⟨_, _, _, H.out.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose,
105	    H.out.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.nonempty⟩
106	
107	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algebraMap_bijective
108	    (h : Function.Bijective (algebraMap R S)) :
109	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 R S :=
110	  ⟨_, _, _, inferInstance,
111	    SubmersivePresentation.ofBijectiveAlgebraMap h, Presentation.ofBijectiveAlgebraMap_dimension h⟩
112

5	
6	## Current (false) statement
7	
8	> Let `k` be a field of characteristic 0, `B` a finite-type `k`-algebra that is
9	> standard smooth of relative dimension `n`, and `b ∈ B` with `D_{B/k} b = 0`.
10	> Then `b ∈ range (algebraMap k B)`.
11	
12	## Why it is false
13	
14	`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n k B` means only "`B` admits a submersive
15	presentation of relative dimension `n`" — it implies `Ω[B/k]` is locally free of
16	rank `n`, but imposes **no connectedness**, and crucially no **geometric**
17	connectedness (no condition that `k` be algebraically/separably closed in `B`).
18	
19	Two counterexamples, each satisfying every hypothesis:
20	
21	1. **`B = k × k`, `n = 0`** (any char-0 `k`). `B ≅ k[x]/(x²−x)`; the Jacobian
22	   `2x−1` is a unit, so `B` is standard smooth of relative dimension `0` (finite
23	   étale). Étale ⟹ `Ω[B/k] = 0` ⟹ `D = 0`, so `D b = 0` for *all* `b`. But
24	   `range(algebraMap k B)` is the diagonal, and `(1,0)` is not in it.
25	
26	2. **`k = ℚ`, `B = ℚ(√2)`, `n = 0`.** Finite separable ⟹ étale ⟹ `Ω = 0` ⟹
27	   `D = 0`; `D b = 0` for all `b`. `Spec B` is one point (connected) and `B` is
28	   a field (a domain), yet `range(algebraMap ℚ B) = ℚ ⊊ ℚ(√2)`.
29

# `projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim`: `ℙ¹_{k̄}` is smooth of relative dimension 1

**File:** `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean` (L161-163)
**Status:** scaffold `sorry` since iter-165; Mathlib gap. File flagged "off-limits this iteration" in `PROGRESS.md` iter-182.
**Confirmed-missing Mathlib pieces (search exhaustive iter-182):**
- No `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` instance for `Proj` of any graded ring.
- No `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension R (MvPolynomial σ R)` lemma (would need to construct the `SubmersivePresentation` by hand).

## Claim

```
instance projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom
```

i.e. the projective line `Proj k̄[X₀, X₁] → Spec k̄` is smooth of relative dimension `1`.

## Mathematical proof sketch

Smoothness of relative dimension `n` for a scheme morphism is **local on the source**: it suffices to exhibit, for each point `x ∈ X`, an affine open `V ∋ x` of `X` and an affine open `U ⊇ f(V)` of `Y` such that the induced ring map `Γ(Y,U) → Γ(X,V)` is `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n`. See `AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Smooth.lean:135` (`SmoothOfRelativeDimension.exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`).

For ℙ¹ over `Spec k̄` the 2-chart cover by `D₊(X₀)` and `D₊(X₁)` is the standard choice; both charts are isomorphic to `Spec k̄[T]` (the affine line). The base `Spec k̄` is itself affine. So we reduce to:

**(★)** `MvPolynomial Unit k̄` is standard smooth of relative dimension 1 over `k̄`.

`(★)` is constructed by hand:

```
def affLineSubmersivePresentation (k : Type*) [CommRing k] :
    Algebra.SubmersivePresentation k (MvPolynomial Unit k) Empty Unit where
  -- 1 generator `T = X ()`, 0 relations
  toPreSubmersivePresentation :=
    { toPresentation := Algebra.Presentation.naive (v := Empty.elim) ...,
      map := Empty.elim,
      map_inj := fun a => a.elim }
  jacobian_isUnit := by
    -- empty jacobian matrix has det = 1
    simp [PreSubmersivePresentation.jacobian, ...]
```

Dimension = `Fintype.card Unit - Fintype.card Empty = 1 - 0 = 1`.

## Concrete formalization plan (~150-250 LOC across iter-183+)

1. **Helper 1 (~40 LOC)** — `affLineSubmersivePresentation` above. Lives somewhere central (perhaps `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/AffineLineSmooth.lean` or directly inline in `BareScheme.lean`).
2. **Helper 2 (~10 LOC)** — `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 k (MvPolynomial Unit k)` instance from Helper 1 via `SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension` (`RingTheory/Extension/Presentation/Submersive.lean:93`).
3. **Helper 3 (~20 LOC)** — transfer along the chart-iso `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` (from `Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean`): use `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algEquiv` to get
   ```
   Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 k̄
     (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading k̄) (MvPolynomial.X i))
   ```
   for each `i : Fin 2`.
4. **Helper 4 (~40 LOC)** — the affine chart `D₊(X i)` of `ProjectiveLineBarScheme k̄` has structure sheaf isomorphic (as `k̄`-algebra) to the above `HomogeneousLocalization.Away`. Use `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` (already wired up in `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`) plus the local-iso lemmas it produces.
5. **Final (~40-80 LOC)** — the instance body. Given `x : ProjectiveLineBarScheme k̄`, the cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover k̄` gives an `i : Fin 2` with `x ∈ D₊(X i)`. Provide `(U, V, hVU, ...)` with `U = ⊤` (all of `Spec k̄`) and `V = D₊(X i)`. The ring map `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤) ≅ k̄ → Γ(ℙ¹, D₊(X i)) ≅ HomogeneousLocalization.Away _ (X i)` is `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` by Helper 3.

## Risk

- The chart-iso `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` is a `RingEquiv`, not an `AlgEquiv` — need to upgrade via `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap` (already proved in `ChartIso.lean:347`).
- `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algEquiv` exists (`Smooth/StandardSmooth.lean`) but takes an `AlgEquiv`, so the upgrade above is essential.
- Section-iso threading through `Spec.map` ↔ `Spec.preimage` of the affine open in `Proj` is the messy bit.

## Why not closed iter-182

# `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`: `ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible

**File:** `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean` (L154-156)
**Status:** scaffold `sorry` since iter-165; Mathlib gap. File flagged "off-limits this iteration" in `PROGRESS.md` iter-182.
**Confirmed-missing Mathlib pieces (search exhaustive iter-182):**
- No `GeometricallyIrreducible` instance for `Proj` of any graded ring.
- The class is defined (`AlgebraicGeometry/Geometrically/Irreducible.lean:42`) via `geometrically (IrreducibleSpace ·) f`, i.e. for every field extension `Spec K → Spec k̄`, the pullback `Proj k̄[X,Y] ×_{Spec k̄} Spec K` is `IrreducibleSpace`. Mathlib has no general lemma reducing this to a ring-theoretic statement on the graded ring.

## Claim

```
instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom
```

i.e. for every field `K` and every map `Spec K → Spec k̄`, `Proj k̄[X₀,X₁] ×_{Spec k̄} Spec K` is irreducible as a topological space.

## Mathematical proof sketch

`Proj k̄[X₀,X₁] ×_{Spec k̄} Spec K ≅ Proj K[X₀,X₁]` because tensoring the standard ℕ-grading by `K` over `k̄` preserves the grading. Then `Proj K[X₀,X₁] = ℙ¹_K`, which is irreducible: it has the two affine charts `Spec K[T]` (the affine line, which is irreducible since `K[T]` is a domain — `IsDomain ⟹ IrreducibleSpace (Spec _)` is `AlgebraicGeometry.Properties.lean`'s `instIrreducibleSpaceSpecOfIsDomain`), they cover, and their intersection `Spec K[T,T⁻¹]` is nonempty.

Two-chart irreducibility: a topological space is irreducible iff it is nonempty and any two nonempty opens meet. Cover by two open subsets `U₀, U₁` each irreducible with nonempty `U₀ ∩ U₁` ⟹ space irreducible. (Both charts contain the generic point of their respective `K[T]`, and these generic points map to the same generic point of `Proj K[X₀,X₁]`.)

## Concrete formalization plan (~200-350 LOC across iter-183+)

This is **harder** than the smoothness sketch because we must handle the **base change** `Proj k̄[X₀,X₁] ×_{Spec k̄} Spec K`:

1. **Helper A (~50 LOC)** — `Proj` of a base-changed graded ring is the base-change of `Proj`:
   ```
   Proj (𝒜 ⊗ K) ≅ Proj 𝒜 ×_{Spec k̄} Spec K
   ```
   where `𝒜 ⊗ K` is the natural ℕ-grading on `K[X₀,X₁] = K ⊗_{k̄} k̄[X₀,X₁]`. Mathlib has `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.pullback` family of results but the precise statement here is bespoke for the standard polynomial grading.
2. **Helper B (~80 LOC)** — given a field `K`, build `Proj K[X₀,X₁] = ℙ¹_K` directly as
   `Proj (MvPolynomial.homogeneousSubmodule (Fin 2) K)`. Show its 2-chart cover by `D₊(X₀), D₊(X₁)` plus the chart-iso to `Spec K[T]` (mirroring `ChartIso.lean` over `K` instead of `k̄`). All-in-all a re-instantiation of `BareScheme.lean + ChartIso.lean` over an arbitrary field, NOT just `k̄`.
3. **Helper C (~30 LOC)** — irreducibility of the affine line `Spec K[T]` over a field `K`: `K[T]` is a domain (`Polynomial.instIsDomain`), so `IrreducibleSpace (Spec K[T])` via `instIrreducibleSpaceSpecOfIsDomain`. After the chart-iso of Helper B, this gives `IrreducibleSpace (D₊(X i))` for each chart.
4. **Helper D (~40 LOC)** — two-chart cover lemma: if `X = U₀ ∪ U₁` with both `IrreducibleSpace`s and `U₀ ∩ U₁ ≠ ∅`, then `IrreducibleSpace X`. Search Mathlib for `IrreducibleSpace.of_isCompact_isIrreducible` / `IrreducibleSpace.of_isOpen_cover` — may exist.
5. **Helper E (~20 LOC)** — nonemptiness of `D₊(X₀) ∩ D₊(X₁) = D₊(X₀ * X₁)` over `K`: `K[X₀,X₁]` is a domain, `X₀ * X₁ ≠ 0` is a homogeneous element of positive degree, so `D₊(X₀ * X₁)` is nonempty.
6. **Final (~50-100 LOC)** — `GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber` plus universal-open-ness of `ℙ¹ / Spec k̄` to reduce to fibers. For each `s : Spec k̄` (here = unique point, since `k̄` is a field), the fiber over `s` is `Proj k̄[X₀,X₁] ×_{Spec k̄} Spec κ(s) = Proj k̄[X₀,X₁]`, and we use `GeometricallyIrreducible.geometrically_irreducibleSpace` which unfolds to "for every field `K → κ(s) = k̄`, the further pullback is irreducible" — Helper A reduces this to `IrreducibleSpace (Proj K[X₀,X₁])`, settled by Helpers B-E.

## Risk

- Helper A is the load-bearing piece and is genuinely hard: `Proj` distributing over base change is a substantive scheme-theoretic fact.
- Helper B duplicates infrastructure: avoid by parameterising `BareScheme.lean` constructions over a general field `K` instead of `k̄` (this is a refactor that touches `ChartIso.lean` too).

## Why not closed iter-182

PROGRESS.md off-limits flag; substantial Mathlib infrastructure (~200-350 LOC) gated on a more general `Proj k̄[Xᵢ] ×_S Spec K ≅ Proj K[Xᵢ]` lemma; budgeted iter-183+. No prior task_result for this file (no prover assigned previously).

## Alternative route

If `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced` is the *integrality* test sufficient downstream — but `GeometricallyIrreducible` is the one threaded through the AVR pipeline (called from `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`'s `iotaGm_isDominant` chain), so a weaker `IsIntegral` substitute is **not** acceptable.

# AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean

## Overall verdict — NO LEAN EDITS THIS ITER (documented sketch instead)

PROGRESS.md iter-182 lists this file under **Off-limits this iteration** with
rationale **"Mathlib-gap scaffolds"**. Both sorries are well-documented
Mathlib gaps (re-confirmed by exhaustive Mathlib search this iter) and require
~150-350 LOC of new infrastructure each. They are NOT on any iter-182 lane.

Per the prover prompt's `When infrastructure is missing` section
(steps 1-3), the disciplined move is to:

1. Confirm the Mathlib gap (DONE — search log below).
2. NOT degrade the existing structured `sorry`-with-rich-docstring scaffolds.
3. Write proof-route sketches to `informal/<theorem>.md` (DONE — two files).
4. Document this in the task_result so the next planner can route to a
   dedicated lane.

`lake build AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme` GREEN; 2 sorries
(L154 + L161, both bare scaffold-sorries with multi-line rationale docstrings).
The Lean file is unchanged this iter.

---

## projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (L154)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach**: Search Mathlib for `GeometricallyIrreducible` on `Proj` or `ProjectiveSpace`.
- **Result**: FAILED — `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Geometrically/Irreducible.lean`
  defines the class but ships no instance for any `Proj _`. Search of
  `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/`
  finds zero hits on `GeometricallyIrreducible`. Confirmed via:
  - `lean_leansearch "projective line is geometrically irreducible"`
  - `lean_local_search GeometricallyIrreducible` (3 hits — class def + 2
    structural lemmas, nothing constructive for `Proj`)
  - `grep -r GeometricallyIrreducible Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/`
- **Sketch**: see `informal/projectiveLineBar_geomIrred.md`. Plan is
  Helper A (`Proj 𝒜 ⊗ K ≅ Proj 𝒜 ×_S Spec K`) + Helper B (re-parameterise
  the `ChartIso` infrastructure over an arbitrary field) + Helper C
  (`IsDomain K[T] ⟹ IrreducibleSpace (Spec K[T])`) + Helper D
  (two-chart irreducibility-from-cover lemma) + Helper E (chart intersection
  nonemptiness) + Final assembly. ETA ~200-350 LOC across iter-183+.
- **Risk flagged**: Helper A is genuinely hard (base-change distributes
  over `Proj`); there is NO Mathlib lemma for this in the form needed.
- **Alternative ruled out**: `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced` (the
  `tensor-with-AlgebraicClosure-is-reduced` variant) does NOT substitute
  because downstream `iotaGm_isDominant` consumes `GeometricallyIrreducible`
  specifically.

---

## projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim (L161)

### Attempt 1
- **Approach**: Search Mathlib for `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` on `Proj`,
  on `ProjectiveSpace`, or on any concrete `Spec` of a polynomial ring.
- **Result**: FAILED — `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Smooth.lean`
  defines the class but ships no concrete instance beyond `IsOpenImmersion ⟹
  SmoothOfRelativeDimension 0`. No `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension R (MvPolynomial σ R)`
  lemma exists. Confirmed via:
  - `lean_local_search SmoothOfRelativeDimension` (2 hits — class + reduction
    to `Smooth`)
  - `lean_local_search IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension` (14 hits — all
    structural; nothing for `MvPolynomial` or `Polynomial`)
  - `lean_loogle "Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension _ _ (MvPolynomial _ _)"` — empty
  - `grep -r "MvPolynomial.*StandardSmooth\|StandardSmooth.*MvPolynomial" .lake/packages/mathlib` — no matches
  - `grep -r "SmoothOfRelativeDimension" .lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/` — no matches
- **Sketch**: see `informal/projectiveLineBar_smoothOfRelDim.md`. Plan is
  Helper 1 (`SubmersivePresentation k (MvPolynomial Unit k) Empty Unit` — by-hand) +
  Helper 2 (extract `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` instance) + Helper 3
  (transfer via `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` already in
  `Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean:328`) + Helper 4 (chart sheaf-section iso
  threading) + Final (per-point witness via `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`).
  ETA ~150-250 LOC iter-183+.
- **Key existing assets**: `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` (`ChartIso.lean:328`)
  and `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap` (`ChartIso.lean:347`) provide
  the chart-iso AND the `k̄`-algebra preservation lemma, so Helper 3 is
  well-supported. `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (`BareScheme.lean:197`)
  provides the per-point chart-finding.
- **Risk flagged**: the upgrade `RingEquiv ⟶ AlgEquiv` needed for
  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algEquiv` is the messy bit
  but the existing `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap` lemma is the
  exact statement needed.

---

## Recommendation for plan agent (iter-183+)

These are the right move *whenever the project routes through this file*,
not iter-183 (other lanes have higher leverage):

1. **Smoothness lane (~150-250 LOC, ~2-3 iters)**: open
   `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/AffineLineSmooth.lean` (new file)
   to host Helpers 1+2; land Helper 3 in `ChartIso.lean` extensions; land
   Helpers 4 + Final in `BareScheme.lean` proper. Coordinate with
   smoothness-using callers (`Albanese/CodimOneExtension`, `Jacobian.lean`
   `positiveGenusWitness`).
2. **GeomIrred lane (~200-350 LOC, ~3-5 iters)**: prerequisite — generalise
   `BareScheme.lean` + `ChartIso.lean` over an arbitrary field (Helper B).
   This is the load-bearing refactor; once landed, Helpers C-E + Final are
   routine. **Reach lane** — schedule only if `iotaGm_isDominant` or other
   `GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar _).hom` consumer becomes
   blocking; until then leave the `sorry` documented as it is.

If the project never routes through `GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar _).hom`
(plausible — most downstream uses go through `IrreducibleSpace` directly,
not the geometric variant), iter-183+ planner may consider whether to keep
this instance at all, or relax the file to expose only the unparam'd
`IrreducibleSpace` version.

---

## Lemmas / API discovered

- **`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` + `_algebraMap`** (`ChartIso.lean:328, 347`) —
  the chart-ring iso for `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≅+* MvPolynomial Unit k̄` with
  `k̄`-algebra-map preservation. Both load-bearing for the smoothness sketch.
- **`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algEquiv`** (`RingTheory/Smooth/StandardSmooth.lean`) —
  the right transfer lemma. Needs an `AlgEquiv`.
- **`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.ofBijectiveAlgebraMap`** (`Submersive.lean:523`) —
  the construction template for the dim-0 case. The dim-1 case (free `R[T]`
  over `R`) is **not** shipped; needs custom construction.
- **`Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan`** (already in `Mathlib`,
  threaded into `projectiveLineBarAffineCover`) — provides the per-chart
  affine open + sheaf section identification needed by Final assembly of
  smoothness.
- **`AlgebraicGeometry.GeometricallyIrreducible.iff_geometricallyIrreducible_fiber`** —
  the fiber-reduction lemma for the geom-irred sketch. Reduces the
  field-extension quantifier to fibers `f.fiberToSpecResidueField s`.

## Dead-end log

- **Search "IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension MvPolynomial"**: empty.
- **Search "Proj.GeometricallyIrreducible" / "Proj.IrreducibleSpace"**: empty.
- **Search "ProjectiveSpace.smooth" / "ProjectiveSpace.geometricallyIrreducible"**: empty.
- **`MvPolynomial.standardSmooth` / `Polynomial.standardSmooth`**: do not exist.

## Note for next iter

PROGRESS.md should keep this file under `## Off-limits this iteration`
unless the smoothness/geom-irred sketches above are explicitly chosen as a
dedicated iter-183+ lane (with the LOC budget allocated). No prior
task_result for this file (it's never been actively worked) — the planner
is correctly leaving it as a Mathlib-gap scaffold pending a real lane.
